title: Harden recall projection latency on large production stores
description: Production restore proof on 2026-07-03 showed the narrow recall path works, but an initial sinexctl recall --window 30m --limit 40 JSON probe against prod timed out at the gateway after 30s and left DB statement-timeout / slow-pool evidence in sinexd logs. A later tight 5m recall succeeded in 140ms after raw backlog cleanup, and events query/sources status were fast. This issue should make recall/context projection robust for broader windows on an ~80M-row production store without relying on manual retry timing. Start by decomposing the recall path: base event-card query, diversity top-up source queries, self-observation filtering, and source coverage caveat query. Add bounded timing/telemetry and remove sequential query amplification where possible.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl recall --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --window 30m --limit 40 --format json completes reliably under a healthy production runtime; implementation records per-stage recall timings or equivalent evidence; tests cover the projection path without adding a silo; broad recall failure produces an actionable caveat/error instead of tying up DB pool silently.
notes: Evidence packet: .agent/demos/sinex/prod-restore-recall-proof-20260703T095311Z. Failure evidence: initial 30m recall command was stopped after exceeding the useful wait; journal showed RPC 504 at 30s, DB statement timeout, and slow sqlx pool acquire. Successful counter-evidence: 5m recall with --include-self-observation returned 12 events across 5 sources in ~140ms; events query 5m returned in ~389ms; sources status returned in ~1041ms.
close_reason: Implemented first hardening slice: recall/context now records per-stage timings in machine-readable query_echo.stage_timings_ms and bounds base event-card query, diversity top-up, and source-status caveat stages with explicit recall.* timeout caveats. Verification: focused sinexctl tests passed as job 2001371; xtask check -p sinexctl passed as job 2001372; xtask build -p sinexctl passed as job 2001373; live prod /var/cache/sinex/.../target/debug/sinexctl recall --window 30m --limit 40 --format json returned exit 0 in 9.1s with stage timings and recall.diversity_top_up_timeout instead of hanging. Follow-up created for replacing sequential diversity top-up.

comments.author: Sinity
comments.text:
Executable issue packet added after read-only code/evidence pass.

Research packet: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-0tj-recall-latency.md. Related Bead linked: sinex-g3k owns the broader source-filtered latency / ts_orig read-model branch decision; this Bead should harden the recall command path and feed measurements into sinex-g3k if the shared EventQuery listing remains the bottleneck.

Likely root causes:
- CLI query amplification: crate/sinexctl/src/commands/context.rs:263 builds the base recall EventQuery, then :289 calls events.cards, :290 runs top_up_context_diversity, and :292 runs source caveats. top_up_context_diversity at :456 issues sequential events.cards LIMIT 1 RPCs for every missing CONTEXT_DIVERSITY_SOURCES entry.
- EventQuery listing filters original time but orders/cursors by interpretation id: crate/sinex-db/src/repositories/events/composable_query.rs:128 builds the listing, push_filters at :577 applies ts_orig range predicates, and :157 orders by relevance/id or id. Existing indexes in crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/events.rs:387 include ix_events_ts_orig, ix_events_source_ts_orig, and ix_events_source_type_ts_orig, but the generic listing does not order on ts_orig.
- Recall excludes self-observation after fetch: context.rs:291 calls apply_self_observation_mode after the DB/RPC work, so small limits can be consumed by telemetry rows that are later dropped.
- Source caveats are useful but non-essential: context_source_caveats at context.rs:500 calls sources_status_view_filtered(None, None, false); server side source status still performs material aggregation, event presence probes, provider/session-state reads, and view assembly in crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/source_status.rs:123 and :256.

Implementation slices:
1. Instrument and bound recall stages in context.rs: base cards, diversity top-up, self-observation filter, source caveats, render. Emit timings in JSON/YAML metadata or caveats. Add stage-specific timeout caveats such as recall.diversity_top_up_timeout and recall.source_status_timeout instead of letting the operator see only gateway 504/slow-pool symptoms.
2. Remove sequential top-up amplification. First acceptable slice: run top-up queries concurrently under a small cap and shared budget. Better shared-substrate slice: add a server-side source-diverse event-card query under the general events/context substrate, returning base latest cards plus latest card per requested source/family with one disclosure pass. Avoid a recall-only silo.
3. Push self-observation exclusion into EventQuery/composable_query. Preserve --include-self-observation by not applying the predicate. Add tests so recall limits cannot be exhausted by rows that are immediately dropped.
4. Measure/fix the ts_orig listing shape. Run bounded EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) for the generated base recall query and source-filtered top-up query. If an index/order fix suffices, consider ts_orig DESC, id DESC listing/cursor semantics for time-window queries plus any missing required indexes. If it still touches too much data because core.events partitions by id/ts_coided, defer the projection/read-model branch to sinex-g3k.

Acceptance-criteria refinements:
- 30m production recall returns either full output or finite recall output with stage-specific caveats; opaque gateway 504 without stage attribution is not acceptable.
- Recall uses a bounded number of RPCs independent of how many diversity sources are absent; target shape is one event-card RPC plus one bounded/optional source-status RPC.
- Stage timings or equivalent evidence are present in machine-readable recall output.
- Self-observation exclusion is applied before DB result limiting unless --include-self-observation is set.
- If broad latency is still dominated by core.events ts_orig scans after slices 1-3, this Bead records the measured plan and hands the read-model decision to sinex-g3k rather than silently expanding scope.

Verification commands:
- Static/read-only: rg -n "execute_context_request|top_up_context_diversity|context_source_caveats|is_self_observation_card" crate/sinexctl/src/commands/context.rs; rg -n "execute_event_listing|push_filters|ORDER BY id|ts_orig" crate/sinex-db/src/repositories/events/composable_query.rs; rg -n "ix_events_ts_orig|ix_events_source_ts_orig|ix_events_source_type_ts_orig" crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/events.rs crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs.
- Focused tests after implementation: xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(recall_)'; xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(composable_query)'; xtask check -p sinexctl; xtask check -p sinex-db.
- Live/prod proof only when operator-approved and runtime is healthy: sinexctl recall --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --window 30m --limit 40 --format json; compare with --include-self-observation and sinexctl events query --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --since 30m --limit 40 --format json.
- DB plan proof only with explicit read-only session and statement_timeout: EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT * FROM core.events WHERE ts_orig >= now() - interval '30 minutes' AND ts_orig <= now() ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 41; repeat with source='webhistory' LIMIT 2 for the top-up shape.

title: Make schema backfills explicit, resumable, and observable
description: Production restore on 2026-07-03 showed backfill_source_material_event_counts scanning ~80.8M core.events rows during sinexd startup. The work is legitimate first-run convergence, but it is opaque, timeout-sensitive, non-resumable, and guarded by a weak sentinel (any parsed_event_count > 0). Design and implement a migration/backfill substrate for one-time schema data repairs: versioned sentinel, progress/observability, resumability/idempotency, cancellation behavior, and operator-facing proof. Immediate seed case is parsed_event_count backfill on raw.source_material_registry.
design: Executable packet integrated from research subagent Carson; full local packet: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-exc-schema-backfills.md. Shape: schema apply creates objects and registers backfill specs; unbounded data repairs move to an explicit sinex-schema backfill runner surfaced as xtask schema backfill. Add sinex_schemas.schema_backfill_runs keyed by (backfill_key, version) with status, phase, cursor, target, progress, operation_id, timestamps; use pg_try_advisory_lock per key/version; use core.operations_log for operator-visible lifecycle after adding a schema-backfill operation type, but keep checkpoint truth in schema_backfill_runs. First slice parsed-event-count-v1: install existing count triggers first, snapshot target.max_event_id, scan material events by id chunks into an accumulator table, apply accumulated counts to raw.source_material_registry in material-id chunks, persist progress after every chunk, no-op successful reruns unless --restart. Initial implementation may require quiescence; online exactness is a child issue.
acceptance_criteria: apply_schema_for_url no longer runs backfill_source_material_event_counts or any unbounded data repair; xtask schema backfill status --format json reports registered backfills and run state; xtask schema backfill run parsed-event-count-v1 resumes after interruption from persisted cursor; progress is visible in DB and CLI (phase, rows scanned, target max id, materials accumulated/applied, updated timestamp); successful rerun no-ops and partial rerun resumes without double-counting; fixture proof covers zero-event, one-event, multi-event, and derived-only source materials; command refuses or warns unless run in supported quiescent mode.
notes: Code references: crate/sinexd/src/main.rs startup schema apply; crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs backfill_source_material_event_counts weak sentinel/full aggregate; crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/events.rs future count triggers; crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/source_materials.rs parsed_event_count; xtask/src/commands/schema.rs operator entrypoint. Suggested child Beads: online-safe delta capture; measured source_material_id index evaluation; sinexctl ops backfill status; xtask schema backfill verify parsed-event-count-v1.
close_reason: Implemented explicit schema backfill substrate and parsed-event-count-v1 runner. Schema apply now only registers backfill metadata and no longer runs the unbounded source-material event-count repair. Added persisted run/progress tables, resumable chunked scan with quiescence acknowledgement, status/run xtask commands, generated docs, and DB-backed resume/count/idempotence tests. Verified: xtask test -p sinex-schema -E 'test(parsed_event_count_backfill)' job 2001377 passed; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(command_catalog_exposes_core_public_surface)' job 2001378 passed; xtask check -p sinex-schema job 2001379 passed; xtask check -p xtask job 2001380 passed; xtask docs check passed; xtask schema backfill status --format json reported parsed-event-count-v1 registered.

title: Serialize xtask schema apply before background job queueing
description: Starting two xtask checks concurrently on 2026-07-03 caused both command frontends to enter checkout-local schema apply before one was queued behind the coordinated slot. One frontend hit PostgreSQL duplicate index/pg_class race creating ix_archived_events_ts_orig while the other was also applying schema. The job queue serialized the later background work, but not the pre-queue schema-apply preflight. Fix xtask so schema apply is covered by a process-wide/interprocess lock before any concurrent frontend can run it, or move schema apply behind the same coordinated slot assignment.
acceptance_criteria: Two simultaneous xtask check/test invocations cannot run schema apply concurrently. A regression launches two commands against a schema-pending checkout and proves one waits before DDL starts. Duplicate index/constraint races from concurrent schema apply no longer occur.
close_reason: Implemented in flake.nix: launcher-only xtask --bg requests now skip pre-exec SQLx bootstrap and enter the coordinator first; the actual foreground/background child still bootstraps SQLx under a wrapper-level flock at .sinex/state/xtask-sqlx-bootstrap.lock. Verified with nix-instantiate --parse flake.nix, nix eval --raw .#devShells.x86_64-linux.default.name, a regenerated nix develop launcher returning Started background job 2001365 in ~1s with no pre-launch schema output, a concurrent launcher returning Queued behind job 2001363 immediately, and xtask check -p sinexd job 2001366 passing.

comments.author: Sinity
comments.text: Implementation note 2026-07-03: the pre-queue DDL race is in the devshell xtask wrapper, not the Rust xtask background branch. Rust `xtask check --bg`/`xtask test --bg` already enter `coordinate_and_spawn` before foreground preflight, but the wrapper classified check/test/build/fix/deps as SQLx-needing before execing xtask, so concurrent frontends could both run checkout-local schema apply before one became a queued background job. Patch in progress in `flake.nix`: add a current-shell `flock` around `_sinex_xtask_ensure_sqlx_database` and skip SQLx bootstrap for launcher-only `--bg` requests (no `XTASK_BG_JOB_ID`/`XTASK_BG_INVOCATION_ID`, no `--fg`) so the coordinator owns serialization before schema work. Foreground commands and spawned bg children still run SQLx bootstrap.

title: Ack invalidations only after recompute and checkpoint
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. The invalidation consumer can ack before recompute/checkpoint. A crash in that window can lose the invalidation and leave derived state stale until another invalidation happens.
design: From checkpoint-loss packet .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-1-checkpoint-loss-window.md. Affected paths: crate/sinexd/src/runtime/automaton/adapter/mod.rs recv_invalidation acks immediately; crate/sinexd/src/runtime/automaton/adapter/run.rs buffers/debounces; crate/sinexd/src/runtime/automaton/adapter/invalidate.rs prepares/recomputes/applies/checkpoints. Current risk: scan-driven invalidation consumer can ack a JetStream invalidation before debounce, recompute, archive, output emission, and checkpoint. A crash in that window loses the invalidation. Implementation target: remove this legacy scan-driven direct path if deployed event-bridge/operation recovery supersedes it, or move ack after successful recompute and durable checkpoint/pending-invalidation persistence. Also update crate/sinex-primitives/docs/transport.md so invalidation durability claims match the code.
acceptance_criteria: Invalidation messages are acked only after successful recompute and durable checkpoint, or a durable pending-invalidation record exists before ack. Add a crash/restart test proving an invalidation cannot vanish between ack and recompute.

title: Make automaton confirmed-event catch-up provably lossless
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. Confirmed-events delivery currently depends on a Limits/DiscardOld stream with comments noting lagging consumers can lose tail unless DB catch-up repairs the gap. Either switch retention/consumer semantics or add per-automaton DB catch-up that detects retention gaps from checkpoint to current.
design: From checkpoint-loss packet .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-1-checkpoint-loss-window.md. Affected paths: crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/jetstream_consumer/bootstrap.rs confirmed-events stream config; crate/sinexd/src/runtime/jetstream_consumer.rs durable pull consumer creation and handle_confirmed_message; crate/sinexd/src/runtime/stream/runner/automaton_runtime.rs run_automaton_event_bridge and startup DB historical scan; crate/sinexd/src/automata/registry.rs. Current risk: confirmed-events stream uses Limits/DiscardOld, while new/recreated consumers can use DeliverPolicy::New and the DB catch-up proof is not audited across first start, existing checkpoint, retention gap, and all 14 automata. Implementation target: either change retention semantics so messages cannot discard before durable automaton ack, or keep Limits/DiscardOld and make every automaton detect sequence gaps/consumer lag from durable checkpoint and run DB catch-up to current before acknowledging newer live events.
acceptance_criteria: Each registered automaton has a proven recovery path from its durable checkpoint to current even if confirmed-events retention drops messages. A test simulates lag/retention gap and proves no parent event is skipped or the runtime fails loudly with a repairable status.
notes:
2026-07-04 catch-up proof slices: PR #2299 made the generic automaton confirmed-event bridge require supports_historical and run DB historical catch-up even from Checkpoint::None before subscribing with DeliverPolicy::New; focused bridge tests passed as job 2001447 and sinexd check passed as job 2001448. Follow-up branch feature/runtime/automata-bridge-contract adds an AutomatonSpec contract probe derived from each concrete registered runtime type and a registry invariant test proving every registered automaton is bridge-backed, continuous, and supports historical catch-up. Verification after final assertion tweak: job 2001451 passed CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(registered_automata)'; job 2001452 passed CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask check -p sinexd.

2026-07-04 widened checkpoint-start closure: feature/runtime/automaton-lossless-catchup handles r6d.6 + vxu together under greedy-batch policy because both remaining AC are the same generic automaton confirmed-event bridge proof family. Bridge-managed automata now load the saved bridge checkpoint before DB historical catch-up and before subscribing to the live confirmed-event tail. Regression automaton_bridge_runs_historical_catchup_from_saved_bridge_checkpoint proves a preloaded bridge checkpoint is passed to the historical scan instead of Checkpoint::None. Verification jobs: 2001684 passed xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(automaton_bridge)' --no-reuse; 2001685 passed xtask check -p sinexd. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/automaton-lossless-catchup-20260704T1356Z.
close_reason: Completed generic automaton confirmed-event catch-up recovery path. Bridge-managed automata now load the saved bridge checkpoint before historical catch-up and before subscribing to DeliverPolicy::New. Regression automaton_bridge_runs_historical_catchup_from_saved_bridge_checkpoint proves a preloaded bridge checkpoint is passed to the historical scan instead of Checkpoint::None. Existing registry invariant proves registered automata are bridge-repairable. Verification: job 2001684 passed xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(automaton_bridge)' --no-reuse; job 2001685 passed xtask check -p sinexd. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/automaton-lossless-catchup-20260704T1356Z.

title: Remove recovery-spool discard cap or make overflow durable
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. The recovery spool has a bounded remaining-lines path that can permanently discard entries after sustained publish failure or malformed backlog. Replace the discard cap with a bounded but lossless quarantine/segmented spool/operator backlog so overflow is observable and replayable.
design: From checkpoint-loss packet .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-1-checkpoint-loss-window.md. Affected path: crate/sinexd/src/runtime/event_transport.rs, especially EventBatcher::recover_recovery_spool_events and store_recovery_spool_events_at_path. Current risk: recover_recovery_spool_events preserves only MAX_REMAINING_LINES=1000 malformed/still-unpublished lines and discards overflow permanently. Implementation target: remove the discard cap as a loss mechanism. If bounded disk behavior is required, overflow must move to a durable quarantine/backlog file with operator-visible accounting. Malformed lines are preserved verbatim with metadata sufficient to inspect/retry/delete by explicit operator action; no automatic discard.
acceptance_criteria: No code path permanently drops recovery-spool entries because a line-count cap is exceeded. Overflow moves to a durable quarantine/backlog with operator-visible accounting. Tests cover cap overflow and malformed-entry handling without silent loss.
notes: 2026-07-03 codebase-audit note: crate/sinexd/src/runtime/event_transport.rs:~538 carries a stale/misleading comment ('The original code had retry logic here... For now, we'll attempt to send once') that reads as a durability gap. It is NOT — send_batch routes failed batches to the recovery spool (store_recovery_spool_events, same file ~583) which is exactly the mechanism this bead hardens. Fix the comment while here so future readers don't mistake it for silent loss; the actual bounded-discard risk is the spool cap this bead addresses, not the send path.

title: Make source emit durable before cursor checkpoint
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. Adapter-backed live/continuous sources can accept an event into the in-memory EventEmitter/EventBatcher, advance their cursor, and then crash before durable NATS publish or spool. Gate cursor/checkpoint advancement on durable transport acceptance, or introduce an explicit durable handoff confirmation for cursor-bearing sources.
design: From checkpoint-loss packet .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-1-checkpoint-loss-window.md. Affected path: crate/sinexd/src/runtime/parser/adapter_source.rs around AdapterBackedSource::drain_adapter, prepare_pending_adapter_record, materialize_adapter_batch, and persist_stream_checkpoint_if_due, plus runtime event transport boundary in crate/sinexd/src/runtime/event_transport.rs. Current risk: EventEmitter::emit accepts into the runtime channel/batcher, while cursor advancement and checkpoint persistence can happen before NATS/direct admission/spool durability. Implementation target: introduce an explicit durable handoff result for cursor-bearing sources and allow cursor advance only after NATS publish ack, direct admission commit, or failed publish durably fsynced into spool/quarantine. The test must be able to pause after emitter acceptance and before durable handoff and prove the cursor has not advanced.
acceptance_criteria: For cursor-bearing sources, a crash after emit acceptance cannot lose an event silently: either the event is durable in NATS/spool before cursor checkpoint, or the source replays it after restart. Add a focused crash-window/regression test covering adapter_source cursor advancement and event_transport publish/spool ordering.

title: Make SQLx schema bootstrap safe against Timescale CA refresh jobs
description: Starting all-sources after dev infra restart failed during cargo SQLx bootstrap with PostgreSQL XX000 tuple concurrently deleted while schema apply tried DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW sinex_telemetry.file_activity_summary. Timescale CA refresh jobs were active after increasing background workers. Fix schema/bootstrap orchestration so apply either serializes with Timescale jobs, disables/pauses CA policies during destructive CA replacement, or avoids destructive reapply when schema is current.
design: Fix slice in progress: schema apply now guards steady-state trigger/function DDL in crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs. The intended no-change path skips public updated-at function replacement, dlq/privacy triggers, per-table trigger bundles, operations/cascade/lifecycle/jsonb/search function bundles, and embedding-index trigger bundle when the expected pg_proc/pg_trigger objects already exist. This targets the Timescale CA refresh race by avoiding unnecessary AccessExclusive-trigger DDL and CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION during SQLx bootstrap. Verification: xtask check -p sinex-schema job:2001346.
notes: Implemented local fix in crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs: steady-state apply now skips destructive telemetry read-model rebuild when check_telemetry_drifts is empty, skips telemetry CA/policy SQL unless drift exists, makes add_continuous_aggregate_policy calls idempotent, and guards boot-sensitive function/trigger DDL. Verification: xtask check -p sinex-schema job 2001349 exit 0; xtask test -p sinex-schema -E guard-contract-tests job 2001350 exit 0 with schema-apply, nextest-compile, and nextest-run stages successful.
close_reason: Fixed by guarded/idempotent schema apply in crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs. Proof: xtask check -p sinex-schema job 2001349 exit 0; targeted guard-contract tests job 2001350 exit 0, including successful schema-apply stage after prior reproductions failed on Timescale CA drop/policy re-add.

title: Fix xtask cancel to terminate child process trees
description: xtask jobs cancel reported job 2001309 as canceled but left cargo-nextest and rustc children alive under the user session. This causes hidden compile/I/O load after an agent believes verification stopped. Fix cancellation to terminate the full process tree/process group and add a regression around orphaned child cleanup.
design: Implemented local fix: JobManager::cancel now terminates the descendant process tree for the background xtask PID before signaling the top-level job process group; process-tree termination uses process-table start-tick matching and sends TERM/KILL to descendant process groups where possible. Regression added: test_cancel_terminates_nested_process_group spawns a nested setsid sleep and proves cancel kills it.
acceptance_criteria: xtask jobs cancel terminates nested child process groups, not only the top-level xtask process group. Regression test test_cancel_terminates_nested_process_group passes. Existing cancel behavior for missing processes and SIGTERM escalation remains unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed locally. Verification: xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(test_cancel_terminates_nested_process_group)' job 2001352 exit 0; nextest compile/run succeeded and the regression proved a nested setsid child is killed by cancel.

title: Fix hourly summaries rejected by future ts_orig
description: Live dev run on 2026-07-03 routed a derived.hourly-summarizer activity.summary.hourly batch to JetStream DLQ with DB constraint events_check7 (ts_orig <= ts_coided + 1s). The payload batch contains hourly summaries whose window-boundary timestamp can be in the future relative to coid/admission when the current hour is emitted. Align hourly summary ts_orig with the latest included input timestamp instead of the hour_end boundary, mirroring the daily-summarizer fix, and verify no new hourly future-ts DLQ appears after runtime restart.
close_reason: Completed by 49175df99. Automata regression passed as invocation 2001313; live DLQ is empty after restart, so hourly summaries no longer reproduce future-ts rejection.

title: Fix current-day daily summaries rejected as future ts_orig
description: Live dev run on 2026-07-03 routed a derived.daily-summarizer activity.summary.daily batch to JetStream DLQ because the current-day summary used day_end 2026-07-04T00:00:00Z as ts_orig, exceeding the event-engine future-skew guard. Fix the daily summarizer so in-progress/current-day output uses a non-future event timestamp or does not emit until the window is closed. Verify the reproducing DLQ shape no longer appears under the full runtime workload.
close_reason: Completed by 49175df99. Automata regression passed as invocation 2001313; live DLQ is empty after restart, so daily summaries no longer reproduce future-ts rejection.

title: Stop fs source ingesting Beads/Dolt internals
description: Live full-workload run on 2026-07-03 showed recent source materials from /realm/project/sinex/.beads/embeddeddolt/sinex/.dolt/noms/* and .beads temp files. The dev fs source ignored .git/.sinex/.direnv/.claude/target but not .beads, so task-database churn becomes source-material churn and pollutes the demo/runtime backlog. Add .beads to the fs ignored directories or source-binding defaults, verify a restarted full-source run no longer creates .beads source materials, and keep normal project-file capture intact.
close_reason: Completed by ea9d597fb. xtask infra regression passed as invocation 2005024; restarted runtime fs config includes .beads ignore, and recent source-material query found 0 .beads materials in 15 minutes.

title: Fix Postgres CA background-worker starvation in dev runtime
description: Dev-local Postgres is continuously logging 'failed to launch job ... Refresh Continuous Aggregate Policy ... out of background workers' while the full Sinex runtime/source workload is running. This undermines the steady-state target because CA refresh jobs cannot make progress and postgres.log grows noisily. Investigate the Timescale/background-worker config and schema policy fanout, then fix the dev/runtime configuration or CA policy scheduling so the full workload reaches a quiet steady state without hiding the workload.
close_reason: Completed by 17a0ef892. xtask infra regression passed as invocation 2005024; live dev Postgres reports max_worker_processes=28/timescaledb.max_background_workers=20 and latest log tail has no background-worker starvation warnings.

title: Impact planner: staged-hunk blindness + no evidence staleness gating (false-skip vectors)
design:
INVESTIGATION FINDINGS (Fable, 2026-07-03, verified against source — fix, do not delete):

DEFECT 1 — staged-hunk blindness (realistic false-skip):
- changed_files() = `git diff --name-only HEAD` (staged+unstaged) + untracked (xtask/src/affected.rs:354-412).
- changed_hunks() = `git diff --unified=0 -- '*.rs'` — UNSTAGED ONLY (xtask/src/impact.rs:~505).
- A fully-staged .rs modification therefore arrives with hunks=[]; then collect_coverage_impacts and collect_manifest_impacts skip all line filtering (`if !hunks.is_empty()` guards, history/db/impact.rs:230,334) and changed_item_fully_covered returns TRUE on empty hunks (impact.rs:477-487). Balanced mode then narrows to the file's historical tests regardless of what the staged change touches. Agents routinely `git add` before testing — this is a live workflow, not an edge case.
FIX 1: compute hunks against the same baseline as changed_files: `git diff HEAD --unified=0 -- '*.rs'`; represent untracked .rs files as whole-file hunks.
FIX 2 (defense in depth): changed_item_fully_covered returns FALSE for a changed .rs file with empty hunks (evidence gap -> package fallback), never true.

DEFECT 2 — evidence never goes stale:
- coverage_regions and test_execution_manifests queries filter ONLY on file_path (history/db/impact.rs:203-213,312-318); test_dependency_edges has a fingerprint column that is WRITTEN on upsert (:502-517) and never read by any query.
- The planner computes content_hash per ChangedItem (impact.rs:240,469) and uses it for nothing.
- Consequence: line ranges seeded at content X are overlap-matched against hunks of content Y after edits shift lines — unsound both directions (false-narrow when the covering function moved; false-broad on unrelated overlap). Evidence is immortal.
FIX 3: record the subject file's content hash (or git blob oid) at seed time for coverage regions + manifests (the fingerprint column pattern already exists); at query time compare with the current file hash; on mismatch demote the evidence to stale (counts as an evidence gap -> package fallback) instead of trusting shifted ranges. Optionally: a seed-refresh hint in the plan output listing stale-evidence files.

SOUND PARTS (verified, keep): balanced mode falls back to package scope when ANY changed file lacks evidence; aggressive mode is opt-in and records accepted risks + audit sampling; exact-proof reuse keys on current_scoped_tree_fingerprint (content-based) and is not affected by these defects.

VALIDATION (after fixes): the merged-PR replay experiment (meta-plane bead) measures the residual false-skip rate of the FIXED planner — run it then, not before, so the number reflects the shipped logic.
acceptance_criteria: Fix 1: a fully-staged .rs change produces identical plans to the same change unstaged (test both states); Fix 2: empty-hunk changed .rs files are evidence gaps (unit test); Fix 3: editing a seeded file invalidates its coverage/manifest evidence (plan shows the gap + stale hint) until re-seeded; existing impact tests green; xtask impact explain output shows the new gap reasons. Then the PR-replay validation experiment runs against the fixed planner.
close_reason: Implemented impact-planner soundness fixes: staged and unstaged Rust hunks now use git diff HEAD, untracked Rust files become whole-file hunks, empty Rust hunk sets are evidence gaps, coverage/manifest evidence records content hashes and stale evidence is ignored with a plan hint. Verification: impact-focused test job 2001422 passed; xtask check -p xtask job 2001423 passed.

title: attention.stream: the unified attention timeline (THE recall substrate)
design: RECIPE: wm focus spans ⊕ browser visits ⊕ terminal bursts ⊕ fs activity -> one interleaved attention timeline (derived interval/point events). All inputs LIVE today. Implementation: Windowed automaton doing interval lifting (wm transitions -> focus spans is L2 prework where missing) then time-join; emits attention.span events with contributing-source refs as parents. Strictly better than any single source for 'what was I doing at T'. NOTE: quality currently degraded by the attention-source bug (activitywatch empty content, hyprland bulk snapshots) — build against the fixed streams or terminal/browser/fs first with wm joining when repaired. Volume budget declared up front (rule 6); semantics_version from day one.
acceptance_criteria: attention.span events queryable and consumed by recall (replacing single-source timeline assembly); every span's parents resolve to the contributing raw events; a window with a known capture gap produces an honest hole, not interpolation; focused automaton tests + live dev-store proof.
notes:
2026-07-04 first implementation slice on branch feature/attention/stream: add attention.span as a real derived event over existing derived.activity-window/activity.window.summary outputs. This deliberately uses the already-live multi-source activity-window layer as the first attention substrate so recall/demo code can consume a stable event kind now; richer interval-lifting/focus-span enrichment remains follow-up rather than blocking the first artifact.

2026-07-04 live proof after PR #2316 merge (6bf8e8c0c): current-master dev runtime job 2001518 started with .agent/dev/dev-source-bindings.json and SINEX_AUTOMATA_ENABLED=all. Schema deploy created one new payload schema (discovered=166, created=1). attention-stream heartbeat was healthy. Active DB proof at 2026-07-04T05:27Z: derived.attention-stream/attention.span rows=101500, schema_bound=101500, min_ts_orig=2013-03-27T21:28:54.117+01, max_ts_orig=2021-10-01T15:22:47.145875+02, max_ts_coided=2026-07-04T07:27:43.276+02. Parent sample resolved 100/100 to activity.window.summary. Demo artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/attention-stream-live-20260704T0526Z. Tooling caveat closed by sinex-4uu / PR #2317: xtask run list/all-automatons now exposes attention-stream.

2026-07-04 recall-consumption slice: ContextSummaryView now carries attention_spans populated from derived.attention-stream/attention.span event cards; recall diversity top-up includes derived.attention-stream; missing-session caveat distinguishes attention-span timeline fallback from source-latest fallback. Live proof artifact .agent/demos/sinex/attention-recall-live-20260704T0555Z: sinexctl recall against dev gateway returned attention_spans=15, sessions=9, source_count=5, total_events=50, first attention span parent_refs=1. Verification: job 2001525 passed focused sinexctl recall tests; job 2001526 passed xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl; job 2001527 passed xtask build -p sinexctl. Remaining scope: direct raw-parent expansion, known-gap rendering, interval lifting (sinex-8y8), and zero-duration span/source-window quality.
2026-07-04 recall support-ref slice: ContextAttentionSpanView now exposes parent_refs (immediate derived parents) and support_refs (material/raw ancestors from events.lineage) so recall consumers do not have to unpack latest_event to find evidence. Live proof artifact .agent/demos/sinex/attention-recall-support-live-20260704T0619Z: attention_spans=15, attention_spans_with_parent_refs=15, attention_spans_with_support_refs=12, first support ref webhistory/page.visited; bounded caveat recall.attention_lineage_bounded is expected at cap=12. Verification: job 2001530 passed focused sinexctl recall projection test; job 2001531 passed xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl; job 2001532 passed xtask build -p sinexctl. Remaining scope: interval lifting (sinex-8y8), zero-duration/source-window quality, richer gap rendering.
2026-07-04 recall interval evidence slice: ContextSummaryView now exposes intervals: Vec<ContextIntervalView>, and sinexctl recall populates it from derived.interval-lift/state.interval cards alongside attention_spans. Projection preserves parent_refs/latest_event and recovers concrete state_kind from payload_preview.state_kind or, when the bounded preview omitted that field, from interval_id (interval:<state_kind>:...). Live proof artifact .agent/demos/sinex/recall-interval-evidence-live-20260704T1242Z: sinexctl recall --window 6h returned interval_count=1, attention_span_count=1, source_count=10, total_events=27, interval_state_kinds=[desktop.activitywatch.window]; first interval includes parent_refs and latest_event. Verification: job 2001666 passed xtask test -p sinexctl --lib -E 'test(context)' --no-reuse; job 2001667 passed xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl; job 2001668 passed xtask build -p sinexctl. Remaining sinex-a4w.3.1 scope: richer attention.stream raw-parent expansion, known-gap rendering, zero-duration/source-window quality, and broader interval types as sinex-8y8 grows.
2026-07-04 recall timeline slice: ContextSummaryView now exposes timeline: Vec<ContextTimelineItemView>, a sorted shared projection over sessions, attention_spans, lifted intervals, and remaining event cards. This collapses recall consumers onto one inspectable evidence timeline while preserving specialized arrays for current consumers. Live proof artifact .agent/demos/sinex/recall-timeline-live-20260704T1254Z: sinexctl recall --window 6h returned timeline_count=9 with item kinds attention_span=1, interval=1, session=1, event=6; first items include wm.hyprland/window.focused, derived.attention-stream/attention.span with 100 support_refs, derived.interval-lift desktop.activitywatch.window with parent_refs, and derived activity/window/hourly events. Verification: job 2001669 passed xtask test -p sinexctl --lib -E 'test(context)' --no-reuse; job 2001670 passed xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl; job 2001671 passed xtask build -p sinexctl. Remaining sinex-a4w.3.1 scope: known-gap rendering, zero-duration/source-window quality, and richer support refs for non-attention derived timeline items.
2026-07-04 completion phase: recall timeline now carries explicit gap items from source/readiness caveats and attention support expansion now attempts every attention.span in the finite recall packet instead of silently bounding to 12. ContextTimelineItemView supports non-event-backed gap items via optional latest_event and caveats. Live AC proof artifact .agent/demos/sinex/attention-stream-ac-live-20260704T1310Z: attention_span_count=1, attention_spans_with_support_refs=1, attention_spans_without_support_refs=0, max_attention_support_refs=100, timeline_count=13 with attention_span=1 interval=1 session=1 event=6 gap=4, timeline_gap_kinds=[coverage.unmeasurable, window.partial], first gap latest_event_present=false. Verification: job 2001675 passed xtask test -p sinexctl --lib -E 'test(context)' --no-reuse; job 2001676 passed xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl; job 2001677 passed xtask build -p sinexctl. AC matrix: attention.span queryable+consumed by recall=YES; span parents/raw support resolve in finite packet=YES via support_refs; known capture/source gaps produce honest holes=YES via timeline gap items; focused tests+live dev-store proof=YES.
close_reason: Completed attention.stream recall substrate AC. Live proof .agent/demos/sinex/attention-stream-ac-live-20260704T1310Z shows attention spans consumed by recall, all packet attention spans have raw support_refs, and source/readiness caveats render as explicit gap timeline items; focused test/check/build jobs 2001675/2001676/2001677 passed.

title: Cross-source composites (L3): the payoff enumeration
description:
The derivation catalog's L3 layer — where cross-source composition pays. Canonical reference: crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md (recipes, join algebra, extension rules). Every child here: joins ONLY via time (interval overlap on ts_orig) or entity (resolved ids); implemented as an automaton with semantics_version + volume budget; confidences evidence-derived or absent.

PRIORITY SEQUENCE (settled): attention.stream -> project.attribution -> work.episode, with interruption.event as the cheap side-demo. That quartet proves the tower with zero new capture and zero LLM. The rest sequence behind their capture prerequisites (browser content, email, calendar).
design: SPEC SOURCE: crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md — the composite table, join algebra, and extension rules are the authority; every child cites its recipe row. RULES (from the catalog, binding here): every composite = an automaton with semantics_version, joins ONLY via the two keys (time-join on overlapping/adjacent ts_orig intervals; entity-join on resolved ids — a third ad-hoc key means a missing entity kind); interval lifting is L2's job (capture stays points/transitions); volume-class before wiring anything into wildcard fan-in; confidence values evidence-derived or absent. PRIORITY SEQUENCE (value-per-effort, all inputs live): attention.stream (.1) -> project.attribution (.3) -> work.episode (.4), with interruption.event (.2) as the cheap side-demo — that quartet proves the tower with zero new capture and zero LLM. THEN: machine.context (.5) and routine.baseline (.6) once daily grain exists (k4c.2); screen.grounding (.7) after xv1 OCR; plan.vs.actual (.8) after calendar (rkv.4); change.episode (.9) after polylogue deep ingestion (4j2) + beads lane (hlv); consumption.episode (.10) after browser content (rkv.3); comm.thread (.11) after email runtime (di7). PREREQ QUALITY GATE: sinex-60r (attention sources unusable) blocks .1 in practice — fix first or .1 reconstructs garbage.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: the priority quartet (.1-.4 with .2) live on real data with query units and honest gaps; each composite's emitted kind is in the catalog with its recipe row updated from 'planned' to 'live'; no composite joins on an ad-hoc key; a recall window visibly renders composite output (sessions/episodes/attributions), not just raw families.

title: Demo: self-diagnosing capture outage (fault injection)
description: Wave 1 — productizes what the by-hand devloop probes did all week. Induced fault = built-in construct validity.
design: CLAIM: Sinex detects, explains, and heals its own capture failures. PROTOCOL: induce a real fault live (kill a source process; revoke a file permission); measure time-to-detection via the health aggregator; evaluate the remediation plan the runtime produces (the #2248-#2255 remediation machinery); let catch-up heal; verify source-continuity records the scar as an honest gap. INSTRUMENT: the induced fault is exact ground truth — detection latency and remediation-plan accuracy are real measurements, not narration. FALSIFIER: missed detection, wrong remediation plan, or a silently smoothed-over gap. PREREQ: none. CAPABILITY LANDING (required): the recurring by-hand probes this productizes (DLQ grouping, runtime presence, catch-up readiness) graduate into sinexctl ops verify checks — the demo may not remain a script that reimplements them.
acceptance_criteria: Two fault classes demonstrated (process kill + permission revocation); detection latency and remediation verdict reported as numbers; continuity gap visible in recall/source-status afterwards; the probes it used exist as ops verify checks; one-command rerun; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: planted-secret retroactive privacy (positive + negative controls)
description: Wave 1 — shippable with existing machinery. The unique story: retroactive redaction with the originals preserved under operator authority — no reingestion, no data loss, an interpretation-layer rewrite.
design: CLAIM: policy redaction applies retroactively via replay. PROTOCOL: plant N canary secrets + M benign lookalikes in a shell-history slice; ingest with zero rules -> DB grep finds all N (the honest baseline, demonstrating default-zero-redaction is real); enable rules; replay the source -> all N absent from live events, all M still present (false-positive control), originals inspectable in audit.archived_events under operator authority. INSTRUMENT: the grep, both directions, plus archive inspection. FALSIFIER: any canary survives replay, or any lookalike is eaten. PREREQ: none — privacy chokepoint, replay, and archive machinery exist end-to-end (chokepoint audited clean). CAPABILITY LANDING: the planted-canary fixture generator becomes a reusable test fixture; any rule-authoring friction found lands as sinexctl privacy UX fixes, not demo-local workarounds.
acceptance_criteria: One-command regeneration script committed with the artifact; README states claim/controls/falsifier; both grep directions shown in output; archive inspection command included; a cold reader can rerun it against a fresh dev store. Artifact on the shelf with complete summary.json (claim/non-claim/proof/caveats).

title: Demo portfolio: the selected, sequenced set (selection is settled here, not in a radar)
description:
This epic IS the demo selection — the radar file/script were purged 2026-07-03 (operator direction). Demo candidates are beads under this epic, each carrying Claim / Instrument / Falsifier / Prereq in its design field plus a capability-landing requirement (a demo's packaging may be one-off; the facts it uses must land as composable capability).

INTAKE RULE (replaces the radar): a new demo idea = bd create with label demo + parent this epic, design filled with the four fields; the conductor judges it in Direction against this portfolio. The artifact shelf (.agent/demos/sinex, refreshed by devloop-refresh-demos) holds outputs; selection lives here.

THE SELECTED ARC (rationale):
- The recall-v2 baseline-arm campaign (sinex-9j9) is the committed UPLIFT demo and outranks everything here; the production restore campaign (sinex-mhk) is the live-substrate proof. Both are operator directives, tracked as top-level campaigns, not under this epic.
- Wave 1 (no prerequisites, machinery exists): planted-secret retroactive privacy; self-diagnosing outage. Both have built-in controls, both are cheap, both exercise the substrate story no competitor can show.
- Wave 2 (gated on one correctness fix): retroactive bug-fix over months — the deepest single differentiator; doubles as the replay-crash fix's acceptance test.
- Wave 3 (gated on the embedding worker): run-twice-costs-once + hybrid hit@k — the "LLM plane comes alive" pair; the lane-duel adjudication demo follows once lane promotion exists.
- Headline (gated on the campaign): the blinded resumption duel — the thesis demo, an upgrade of the campaign's baseline arm to a blinded two-agent protocol.

FOLDED (not separate demos): "Reconstruct my Tuesday with honest gaps" -> IS the recall-v2 campaign artifact (sinex-9j9). Closed-loop focus actuation -> the acceptance demo of the instruction-verb registry (sinex-r7h). Devloop-as-source self-hosting -> the demo packaging of the beads/devloop ingestion issue (sinex-hlv).
design: STRUCTURE: children are the selected portfolio — each carries Claim / Instrument / Falsifier / Prereq in its design and a capability-landing requirement (demo may be one-off; its facts must land as composable capability — see the refinement doctrine in notes). WAVES: 1 = cem.1 (planted secret) + cem.2 (outage self-diagnosis), no prereqs, buildable now; 2 = cem.3 (retroactive bug-fix; doubles as sinex-afd acceptance); 3 = cem.4 + cem.5 (both gated on 5v6 embeddings), then cem.6 (lane duel; + lane-promote 2nd + golden set a4w.1); headline = cem.7 (blinded resumption duel); breadth = cem.8 (Tuesday; gated on recall latency 0tj/g3k for full-day windows); frontier = cem.9 (actuation; gated r7h), cem.10 (devloop-as-source; gated hlv). EVIDENCE GENRE: every terminal packet has claim/non-claims/falsifier-run/regenerate.sh/cold-reader pass — the recall-v2 packet (.agent/demos/sinex/sinex-recall-v2-multisource-20260701T2200-0000) is the reference shape. External promotion of scrubbed packets is ej9/83g scope, not this epic's.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: every child either terminal (packet + independent cold-reader PASS + falsifier actually run + capability landed in product surface) or explicitly parked with its gate named; at least one wave-3 semantic demo and the cem.7 headline reach terminal; no demo contains logic that bypasses product query surfaces.
notes: 2026-07-03 refinement doctrine (operator): demos should be refined and made MORE IMPRESSIVE — but impressiveness is bought by enhancing actual capability (the algebraic-composability rule), never by demo-side machinery. A demo is thin glue: a script composing query units/refs/cards/projections that already exist as product surface, plus narration. If making a demo more impressive requires code, that code lands as a shared capability (query unit, view, composite, renderer) and the demo shrinks back to glue. Applies retroactively: recall-v2 is terminal as a campaign artifact but should be superseded by a more impressive successor once attention.stream (a4w.3.1) + the derived plane wiring (sinex-vhm) land — the successor's wow should come from those capabilities, its glue no thicker than regenerate.sh. Test for every portfolio child: could an agent reproduce the demo's substance through public product surfaces alone? If no, a capability is missing — bead it, build it, then demo it.

title: Consumption: recall and the derived plane pay rent
description: The hourglass diagnosis: derivation runs but almost nothing consumes it. This epic makes the derived plane pay rent — truthful product recall, query latency/read-model, query units for derived events, the embedding worker, lane promotion, ambient agent context, and honest object resolution. The strategic lever for every demo.
design: DOCTRINE: the derived plane pays rent when consumption surfaces answer real questions faster/better than raw tools, with provenance. LIMBS by function — TRUTHFUL: vhm (derived-plane wiring, no fabricated confidences). FAST: g3k (ts_orig read-model decision) + x69 (bounded diversity query); 0tj hardening feeds both. SALIENT: uxd (measured salience terms). RICH: a4w.3 composites (the payoff enumeration — attention.stream first), lv8 (query units over the derived plane), a4w.4+a4w.1 (entity quality: bounded multi-entity + golden set). DELIVERED: a4w.2 (citation-required brief), the 7z0 UX epic (daily surface/adjudication/agent arrival) consumes this epic's outputs. SEMANTIC: 5v6 (embedding worker) unlocks cem.4/5/6 and the vector plane. SEQUENCING: vhm + g3k first (truthful+fast recall is the substrate everything renders on); attention.stream next; brief once sessions/summaries are real; 5v6 parallel-safe.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: recall answers session-anchored, family-diverse, honestly-gapped timelines from derived events (not degenerate heuristics) at interactive latency on the production store; day-grain and window-grain questions are one query expression; consumption telemetry (nbi.2) shows operator+agent surfaces invoked routinely; demos cem.5/6/7 unblocked.

title: Pipeline robustness under load
description: The pipeline must survive sustained ingestion, historical imports, and its own telemetry: stream retention semantics, import pacing, boot-time schema-apply races, reflection material fragmentation, confirmation-plane consolidation, and telemetry lane separation. Most items trace to the 2026-06 prod flap and dev-loop jam incidents.
design: DOCTRINE: robustness = everything bounded and budgeted, nothing surprising at 10x. LIMBS: 9u4 (stream retention: Limits+discard:Old for confirmed-events, WorkQueue decision for raw — supersedes gh#2204's Interest claim, see bead); 2n9 (import pacing — the 1.24M-backlog prod flap must be unrepeatable); ddy remaining scope (paced restart drain, DLQ triage); exc (backfills explicit/resumable/observable); egs (confirmation-plane simplification removes the watermark lane's failure modes); x9i (telemetry lane separation so self-observation cannot drown activity). MEASUREMENTS that settle designs by numbers: n23.1 (derivation lag p50/p99 per automaton), n23.2 (storage growth model — settles granularity/pacing), n23.3 (NATS capacity model — one budget table, enforced loud at startup; the #2186->#2200->#2204 lesson generalized). SEQUENCING: measurements can run anytime (analysis lane); 9u4+egs are the structural pair; pacing before any next big import.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: a deliberate 10x backlog event (test or paced import) drains without operator surgery, OOM, or stream jam; startup fails loud when consumer/stream worst-case exceeds the host budget table; import pacing is operator-visible (rate/position/ETA); lag and growth numbers exist and the granularity/pacing decisions cite them.

title: Substrate honesty: no silent data loss, no false claims
description: The substrate's honesty is the product's premise. Groups every verified correctness/data-loss defect: replay crash-window loss, source checkpoint prongs, automaton ack window, the small-bug sweep, the CAS path escape, and schema convergence gaps. Order of attack inside this epic: crash-loss windows first (replay, checkpoints, acks), then hardening (CAS, schema-apply gaps).
design: THE LOSS-WINDOW MAP (this epic is done when this map is closed): (1) source emit-before-cursor-checkpoint — r6d.4; (2) recovery-spool discard cap — r6d.5; (3) automaton confirmed-event catch-up gap — r6d.6; (4) invalidation ack-before-recompute — r6d.7; (5) automaton ack-on-mpsc-enqueue — vxu (NB: much already on unmerged automaton-confirmed-delivery branch, merge via egs first); (6) source checkpoint prongs (page-0, journalctl cursor, bash dedup, browser context) — jda; (7) replay crash between archive-commit and re-ingest — afd. INSTRUMENTS: r6d.1 (the matrix: per-window classification bounded/unbounded, evidence, verdict) frames the campaign — run it FIRST if not concurrent; r6d.9 (crash harness) is how fixes get proven beyond inspection; r6d.8 (replayability classification) determines which windows are self-healing via replay vs true loss. HONESTY LIMBS beyond data loss: r6d.3 (health that cannot lie), r6d.2 (privacy shadow audit), w98 (backup/CAS scrub — the L0-forever claim), qig (CAS read boundary). SEQUENCING: egs (confirmation-plane merge) before vxu/r6d.6 — do not fix the old plane the branch deletes.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: the r6d.1 matrix shows every enumerated window either CLOSED (with a r6d.9 harness scenario or focused test proving it) or ACCEPTED with recorded operator rationale; no health check defaults to true; ops verify has at least one correctness-class probe per subsystem; backup/scrub capability exists (w98). The epic's claim — no silent data loss, no false claims — is then a checked assertion, not a slogan.

title: Verify deployed static imports (git + raindrop) after next Sinnix switch/restart
description: Nix module eval already exposes default static imports for git-commit-history and raindrop-bookmarks; dev-local drivers run. Next proof: after a Sinnix switch/restart, confirm sinexd receives those bindings through SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH and emits/updates source coverage WITHOUT relying on xtask infra dev-bindings. Cheap, gates trusting the deployment path for the rest of P12.
design: DECISION: this is a pure verification task riding the next Sinnix switch — no code unless the proof fails. Proof sequence on the live host after switch/restart: (1) systemctl show sinexd -p Environment | grep SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH and cat the manifest — confirm git-commit-history + raindrop-bookmarks bindings present from the NixOS module (not xtask dev-bindings); (2) journalctl -u sinexd since the restart — both drivers initialize, no binding-parse errors; (3) sinexctl sources status git-commit-history / raindrop-bookmarks — materials registered, recent output nonzero after the scheduled timer fires; (4) sinexctl events query with source filter — events present with post-restart ts_coided. FAILURE BRANCH: if bindings absent, the gap is nixos/modules/sources.nix staticImports rendering vs SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH plumbing — diagnose there, file discovered-from bead if structural.
acceptance_criteria: Both sources show materials + fresh events on the production runtime sourced from the NixOS-rendered binding manifest, with zero xtask/dev-bindings involvement; the four proof commands + outputs recorded in the closing comment; failure branch either empty or filed.

title: Ambient context injection: SessionStart hook feeding agents machine context
description:
Cheap + big: once sinexctl recall is honest and compact, a SessionStart hook (like the polylogue-recall hook) gives EVERY future agent session ambient machine context — the compounding consumption unlock. Implementation lives in Sinnix (dots/claude hooks + codex equivalent); Sinex owns the command output contract and runtime-target descriptor. Budget-bounded, honest-caveats output (no fabricated confidence). Gate on recall truthfulness (P7 continuation) being good enough that the injected context is not misleading.

Refs: handoff pack P13.
design: DECISION: SessionStart hook in the sinnix repo (dots/claude hooks; pattern: sessionstart-polylogue-recall.sh) injecting compact sinexctl recall output against the prod/dev runtime target via the runtime-target descriptor for auth; degrade SILENTLY when sinexd is down; strict token budget (<1K tokens). Sinex side owns only the command output contract. Coordinate: implementation commit lands in sinnix, not sinex.
acceptance_criteria: New Claude/Codex sessions open with a machine-context block; zero hang and zero error noise when the daemon is down; injected block stays under budget on a busy window.
notes: 2026-07-03 completed via Sinnix commit 247888330d8b04431adb994241d113969a1822f3: added sessionstart-sinex-recall hook, Claude SessionStart registration, Codex generated SessionStart hook registration, executable shim, and shared docs. Activated with nix develop --command switch and pushed to Sinnix master. Installed verification: ~/.claude/hooks/sessionstart-sinex-recall.sh and ~/.local/bin/sessionstart-sinex-recall are executable store paths; ~/.codex/hooks.json SessionStart includes sessionstart-sinex-recall; installed hook emitted a 98-word checkout-local machine-context block; no-target mode emitted 0 bytes stdout/stderr. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/ambient-agent-recall-sessionstart-20260703T141826Z.
close_reason: Completed: Sinnix commit 247888330d8b04431adb994241d113969a1822f3 adds the shared sessionstart-sinex-recall hook, Claude SessionStart registration, Codex generated SessionStart hook registration, executable shim, and docs; it was activated with nix develop --command switch and pushed to Sinnix master. Installed verification: ~/.claude/hooks/sessionstart-sinex-recall.sh and ~/.local/bin/sessionstart-sinex-recall are executable store paths; ~/.codex/hooks.json SessionStart includes sessionstart-sinex-recall; installed hook against the checkout-local Sinex target emitted a 98-word machine-context block; no-target mode emitted 0 bytes stdout/stderr. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/ambient-agent-recall-sessionstart-20260703T141826Z.

title: Embedding worker over the local LiteLLM gateway: activate the dormant vector/LLM plane
description:
One worker loop activates five dormant subsystems: events_without_embeddings -> embed via LiteLLM gateway (127.0.0.1:4000, modules/services/litellm.nix) -> store_event_embedding, recording ModelEffectRecord and emitting llm.budget.ledger events.

This turns on: pgvector knn/hybrid search (currently zero production callers), doc-search VectorSearchParams, model_effects repo, sinexctl llm budget-report (currently reports over events nothing emits), and — most importantly — establishes the record/replay ModelEffectRecord pattern the whole L3 semantic plane needs (llm.rs is a complete typed contract with NO model client anywhere).

Embedding_producer currently emits receipts for embeddings never computed (embedding_producer.rs:50-71) — make the receipts true or replace them.

Refs: handoff P8; audit 015 §B6.
design: DECISION: a worker loop, preferably an automaton for provenance (not a sidecar): events_without_embeddings -> embed via LiteLLM local model (127.0.0.1:4000; model name from litellm model_list in sinnix modules/services/litellm.nix) -> store_event_embedding + write ModelEffectRecord (provider/model/input-hash) + emit llm.budget.ledger event. Wire hybrid doc search's dormant VectorSearchParams (document_search.rs:80-88). TARGETS: sinex-db/src/repositories/embeddings.rs:79-270 (store_event_embedding, knn_search, hybrid_search, events_without_embeddings — all currently caller-less), automata/embedding_producer.rs:50-71 (receipt-only producer to make truthful or replace). Record dimensions/model in the effect key so model swaps re-derive. Batch + rate-limit.
acceptance_criteria: llm budget-report non-zero; knn/hybrid search returns results on the dev store; re-run over the same corpus -> ~100% ledger cache hits (this is the record/replay demo substrate). VERIFY: xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(embedding)'; live: embed a backlog on the dev store, run a hybrid search via sinexctl docs.

title: Recall/event-query latency: diagnose source-filtered path, decide ts_orig projection read model
description:
Live evidence: browser/git source-specific event queries TIMED OUT via the API; broad direct core.events source scans were slow enough to cancel. First proof: bounded EXPLAIN (or query-planner instrumentation) of the generated event-card/source-filter query — no more unbounded scans.

Hard constraint (U5, verified): a ts_orig continuous aggregate is IMPOSSIBLE — TimescaleDB CAs must bucket on the partition dimension, and core.events partitions by UUIDv7 id (ts_coided). ts_orig btree indexes exist but give NO chunk exclusion. If historical recall windows need speed, the answer is a projection/read table keyed by ts_orig (maintained by trigger or consumer), NOT a CA and NOT repartitioning (rejected in gh#2182 — replay economics depend on id partitioning).

Deliverables: EXPLAIN evidence, indexed-path fix if one exists, else a designed recall read-model projection with freshness/invalidation semantics (DerivationSpec).

Refs: handoff pack U5 + P16; ACTIVE-LOOP Next Action 2.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03): measurement-gated two-branch plan. The EXPLAIN evidence picks the branch; BOTH branches are fully designed so the executor only measures and executes.

STEP 1 — EVIDENCE (always): EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) the exact generated shapes: (a) recall/context window query (ts_orig range + activity families), (b) source-filtered event query (source='browser.history' + ts_orig range — the one that timed out), (c) show-ref lookup by id. Run on the dev store at current scale. Record plans in the bead/PR. Constraint (verified, do not re-derive): core.events partitions by UUIDv7 id, so NO ts_orig chunk exclusion is possible and a ts_orig CA on core.events is IMPOSSIBLE; repartitioning is rejected (replay economics depend on id partitioning; see gh#2182 trail).

BRANCH A — index path suffices (plans show index scans with acceptable buffers after, e.g., a missing composite index like (source, ts_orig) or (event_type, ts_orig)): add the composite index(es) to EVENTS_REQUIRED_INDEXES (note gh#2196: indexes outside the required-set are invisible to drift detection — add them THERE), done. Choose A when interactive windows (<=48h) come in under ~200ms on dev-scale data.

BRANCH B — recall projection table (choose when index scans still touch all chunks / historical windows stay seconds-slow):
- recall.timeline: a ts_orig-PARTITIONED TimescaleDB hypertable. KEY UNLOCK: the ts_orig-partitioning impossibility applies only to core.events (partitioned by id); a PROJECTION table may partition on ts_orig freely — giving chunk exclusion for time windows AND, later, real ts_orig-bucketed CAs (hourly activity rollups by occurrence time) for free.
- Columns: ts_orig, event_id, source, event_type, family, session_id (nullable), title_snippet (card-grade, disclosure-filtered), lane. No payloads — cards hydrate by id on demand.
- Maintenance: insert hook in the event engine post-admission (same site as confirmed publish), activity lane only; plus a rebuild operation. Declared as DerivationSpec derivation:recall.timeline_projection@v1 with RebuildOnInputChange freshness and replay/archive/redaction invalidation (redaction invalidation matters: title_snippet must re-render when privacy rules change).
- This is honest cache doctrine: the projection is rebuildable, never authoritative; recall reads it, show/trace resolve against core.events.

SEQUENCING: coordinate with telemetry lane (sinex-x9i) — if the lane split lands first, measure AFTER it (reflection volume may be most of the scan cost, changing the branch decision).
acceptance_criteria: EXPLAIN(ANALYZE,BUFFERS) plans for the three shapes recorded BEFORE code; branch decision written with the numbers; Branch A: new composite indexes present in EVENTS_REQUIRED_INDEXES and interactive windows meet budget; Branch B: recall.timeline hypertable partitioned on ts_orig with DerivationSpec + rebuild op + engine insert hook, browser/git source queries return interactively on the dev store, and a redaction change invalidates/re-renders snippets. VERIFY: EXPLAIN artifacts in the PR; live dev-store latency before/after; xtask test -p sinex-db on the projection repo.
notes:
Branch B variant note (U5): a PLAIN day-partitioned postgres table (native range partitions) is the acceptable simpler alternative to a ts_orig hypertable — same chunk-exclusion benefit, less machinery. Choose the hypertable ONLY if ts_orig-bucketed CAs (hourly occurrence-time rollups) are actually wanted at implementation time; historical-backfill CA refresh windows are a known caveat either way.

2026-07-03 Branch A decision/proof: live dev store had ~69.3M core.events rows and ~57.8M webhistory rows. Before: source-filtered browser query with source = ANY(singleton), ts_orig >= now()-48h, ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 201 scanned pkey/id order, removed 880,681 rows, hit/read 193,876/107,747 buffers, and took 18,647.982 ms. Seven-source recall-family shape with ORDER BY id took 1,341.830 ms. Branch A proof: singleton source equality plus ORDER BY ts_orig DESC, id DESC used ix_events_source_ts_orig and returned full event-card rows in 1.156 ms; broad 48h occurrence-window shape used ix_events_ts_orig and returned 201 full-card rows in 9.923 ms. Decision: fix generated composable-query SQL shape; no projection table for this slice. Tracked report: crate/sinex-db/docs/recall_query_latency_2026_07_03.md. Verification: xtask test -p sinex-db -E test(time_window_listing_paginates_by_ts_orig_then_id) job 2001387 exit 0; xtask check -p sinex-db job 2001388 exit 0; xtask check -p sinex-primitives job 2001389 exit 0.
close_reason: Completed by PR #2271. Branch A selected from live EXPLAIN evidence: singleton source equality plus occurrence-order event-card listings use existing ts_orig indexes and meet interactive budget on the 69.3M-row dev store; no projection table needed for this slice. Verification: focused sinex-db regression job 2001387, sinex-db check job 2001388, sinex-primitives check job 2001389, and pre-push changed-strict passed before merge.

title: Recall truthfulness: derived-plane wiring, honest confidences, browser/git participation
description:
The highest-value consumption packet, partially landed (root sinexctl recall + source caveats exist on local master). Remaining to make the command truthful and complete:
1. Wire recall/context to the derived plane: consume activity.session.boundary + hourly/daily summaries instead of the degenerate one-session heuristic (context.rs:718-732). Six automata currently run as pure cost.
2. Kill fabricated numbers: hardcoded confidences 0.72/0.55/0.45 at context.rs:508,530,847 — replace with evidence-derived values or remove the field (every number carries evidence-provenance or is absent).
3. Diagnose browser/git non-participation: recall windows 2h-48h returned only journald/shell.atuin/fs-watcher despite browser/git events existing (~58M page.visited parsed). Depends on the query-latency diagnosis issue.
4. Sessions/summaries/caveats as real product semantics so demos never need raw SQL.

Refs: handoff pack P7; feeds the recall-v2 campaign.
design: DECISION: session-anchored, family-diverse timeline built from activity.session.boundary + activity.summary.hourly + canonical commands + page visits + docs; every row an EventCardView with resolvable refs; honest gaps rendered from source-continuity data (caveats already landed). Replace hardcoded confidences (context.rs:508,530,847) with evidence-derived values (evidence-count/recency) or drop the numbers — every number carries evidence-provenance or is absent. Degenerate focus-session heuristic (context.rs:718-732) is replaced by real session boundaries. Proven query shape: demo/sinex-recall/recall.sh + recall-v2 shelf artifacts. Keep --desktop heuristics only if re-derived honestly.
acceptance_criteria: Recall rows resolve via show <ref>; session rows come from the session detector (disable it -> recall degrades visibly); capture gaps render as gaps; JSON stable for MCP; no fabricated confidence numbers remain (rg '0\.72|0\.55|0\.45' crate/sinexctl clean). VERIFY: live checkout-local dev store (xtask run core --bg, manifest .agent/dev/dev-source-bindings.json); parity check against a recall-v2 shelf artifact. PITFALL: measure ts_orig query latency with EXPLAIN first (sinex-g3k) — do not promise interactive latency.
notes:
2026-07-03 follow-up proof update: recall source caveats now include source-status gap details even when expected sources contribute rows, and expected source descriptors split event_source_id from coverage_source_id (shell.atuin->terminal.atuin-history, webhistory->browser.history, fs-watcher->fs, git->git-commit-history). Proofs: focused recall tests job 2001344 exit 0 (4 passed); final sinexctl build job 2001345 exit 0; live recall --window 1m --limit 50 showed terminal/browser/filesystem/git absent from the tiny window while active source-status caveats pointed at source_driver:terminal.atuin-history, source_driver:browser.history, source_driver:fs, and source_driver:git-commit-history; terminal/git gap caveats carried runtime_binding_stalled details. Demo packet: .agent/demos/sinex/recall-source-gaps-live-20260703T0919Z (manifest refreshed). 2026-07-03 PR #2269 added ContextSessionView and recall payload sessions[] populated from derived.session-detector/activity.session.boundary cards, plus recall.sessions.absent caveat when recall has activity but no session rows. title: Confirmation-plane consolidation: merge automaton-confirmed-delivery branch, delete watermark lane
description:
Two confirmation planes run simultaneously: SSE consumes the WATERMARK stream + DB refetch (api/sse_bus.rs:1-7,351) while the confirmed stream carries full payloads (persist.rs:135-143); ConfirmationBuffer is a constructed, registered, health-probed zombie fed by nothing (initialize.rs:150-159, service_container.rs:658-713).

PRIOR WORK — USE IT: unmerged branch feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery already implements most of this (Option-C, verified 2026-06-28 live dogfood: per-automaton confirmed-Event consumers, ConfirmationBuffer/provisional/refetch/watermark-consumer/#2202-fallback DELETED, SSE migrated, producer durability gate, net -2021 lines, e2e test_jetstream_e2e_event_flow passing). Rebase/merge that branch rather than reimplementing. Then finish Phase-3b: watermark-topology teardown (watermark still published with no reader).

Interacts with gh#2204 (retention: Interest policy fits the post-merge consumer topology) — sequence them together.

Refs: handoff pack P3; memory session_2026_06_28/29; branch log.
design: DECISION: one bus — the full-payload confirmed stream. Order: (1) rebase/merge feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery (verified: SSE migrated, ConfirmationBuffer/provisional/refetch/watermark-consumer deleted, -2021 lines, e2e passing) instead of reimplementing; (2) Phase-3b watermark-topology teardown — watermark publish paths (persist.rs:174-376, jetstream_consumer/prepare.rs:346-504), retry queue, _CONFIRMATIONS stream provisioning; (3) any residual ConfirmationBuffer/confirmation_handler.rs/service_container health plumbing on the merged result. Pre-release: no deprecation shims. NATS stream config lives in runtime/jetstream_streams.rs AND nixos provisioning — update both. Preserve the SSE disclosure boundary (filter by source/type before payload disclosure).
acceptance_criteria: SSE clients receive events with no DB refetch; rg -n 'ConfirmationBuffer|confirmations\.' crate/ --type rust -> tests only, then tests deleted; NATS stream inventory no longer provisions the watermark lane; each event written exactly once to NATS post-persist. VERIFY: e2e SSE subscription test; xtask test -p sinexd; xtask infra smoke --run-core; check sinexctl events stream/watch paths for watermark assumptions.

Confirmed-events stream now carries one full post-redaction event per persisted event; tests updated away from per-kind watermark semantics. title: Telemetry lane separation: self-observation must not drown the activity plane
description:
Fable packet P5 + live evidence: self-observation-like sources were 84.8% of top-source 1h events and 53.1% of 24h during dev catch-up; bursts of derived.activity-window catch-up hit 97% of 5-min windows; a classifier bug counted bare source='sinex' as ingestion in telemetry components. Short recall windows drown in the system's own reflection.

Design decision (do NOT just add a CLI flag): physically separate telemetry (own stream/table/retention), throttle/sample self-observation, or a projection/read-model that keeps product recall activity-first. Source role/lane semantics belong in shared primitives or schema. Ground in current counts (whole-store mix is ~84% page.visited / 11% entity-derived / 2.6% telemetry-like — the dominance is window-relative, not absolute).

Overlaps the stream-retention issue prong 3 (self-obs volume). Refs: handoff pack P5; ACTIVE-LOOP Next Action 3.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03): physical lane separation. Sampling/throttling and query-side-only filtering are REJECTED (rationale at bottom).

SHAPE:
1. Shared SourceRole in sinex-primitives (Activity | Reflection), resolved from the source registry — ONE definition site. The throughput classifier bug (bare source='sinex' counted as ingestion) is fixed by consuming this enum; no string prefix matching anywhere. Land this first as its own slice — it is independently valuable and the migration key.
2. Reflection events publish to their own stream: {env}.sinex.reflection.> with its own retention (short, e.g. 7d / tight byte cap — reflection is operational evidence, not life history). Coordinate naming with the self-observation -> reflection rename (sinex-lb8): do the rename's event-type/source-id decisions in the same design pass so streams/tables are named once.
3. Reflection persists into its own hypertable (reflection.events, IDENTICAL column schema to core.events, same provenance constraints). The decisive argument for a separate table over a role column: retention and compression policy can only diverge at hypertable level, and reflection wants aggressive retention that must NEVER be able to touch operator life-events. Rebuildability makes short retention safe.
4. Default consumption reads activity only: automaton fan-out subscribes the activity confirmed stream (reflection-consuming automata opt in explicitly); recall/context/query units default to activity; --include-reflection (or lane: reflection query constraint) opts in. Telemetry CAs move to the reflection table (they bucket on ts_coided — fine there).
5. Cross-lane queries UNION explicitly through the query substrate's lane parameter — uniform event semantics, two physical homes.

MIGRATION ORDER: (1) SourceRole + classifier fix; (2) reflection stream + publish routing; (3) reflection.events table + engine consumer; (4) CAs move; (5) retention policies; (6) default-exclusion in consumers/query surfaces. Each step independently verifiable; the system runs mixed mid-migration (old rows stay in core.events; queries treat pre-migration reflection rows via SourceRole filter until a scoped archive sweep moves or ages them out).

REJECTED:
- Sampling/throttling: destroys operational evidence and does nothing for the rows already dominating short recall windows.
- Query-side filtering only (projection/WHERE): keeps paying storage, stream fill, fan-out, and retention coupling; the classifier bug proved string-filter approaches drift.
- Same table + role column: cannot diverge retention/compression; every activity query still scans reflection chunks in shared windows.

EVIDENCE BASE: self-observation-like sources = 84.8% of top-source 1h events, 53.1% of 24h during catch-up; whole-store mix only ~2.6% telemetry — the dominance is window-relative, which is exactly why lane separation (not deletion) is right.
acceptance_criteria: SourceRole enum is the single classification site (rg finds no ad-hoc self-observation string matching in telemetry components); classifier counts bare 'sinex' as reflection; reflection stream + table exist with their own retention; automaton consumers and recall/query default to activity lane (disable-and-observe test: reflection volume no longer appears in recall windows); telemetry CLI + CAs still work against the reflection table; a mixed-lane UNION query works through the lane parameter. VERIFY: live dev capture before/after; CA refresh; xtask test vm --category smoke for the deployment shape.

Moved self-observation telemetry read models/current_health/gateway/stream/assembly/source/metric/batch CAs to reflection.events while keeping activity read models on core.events. Updated transport decision doc to state remaining scope is lane-aware event-engine writes and general query defaults. Verification: xtask test -p sinex-schema --lib -E 'test(reflection_event_trigger_sql_targets_reflection_table)' job 2001705 passed; xtask test -p sinex-db --test database job 2001709 passed; xtask check -p sinex-schema -p sinex-db job 2001710 passed; xtask schema strict-diff reported drift_count=0; git diff --check passed.
