title: Automaton ack-before-process loss window (ack on mpsc enqueue)
description:
Fable packet P2 (verified on master): the automaton bridge acks a JetStream message when the event enters a 1024-slot mpsc (stream/listener.rs:110-132 + runtime/jetstream_consumer.rs:322-331), not after processing. A crash loses up to ~1124 acked events. Only supports_historical automata are healed by startup DB catch-up (automaton_runtime.rs:224-239). Failure mode is SILENT LOSS, not duplicates.

CHECK PRIOR WORK FIRST: the unmerged branch feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery (Option-C, 2026-06-28, verified live) rebuilt automaton consumption on per-automaton confirmed-events consumers — the ack semantics there may already close or reshape this window. Audit that branch's ack path before writing anything new; prefer merging it (see the confirmation-plane issue).

Fix directions if still open after that: ack after process (or after durable enqueue), or mandate supports_historical catch-up for every automaton and document at-least-once.

Refs: handoff pack 01-work-packets.md P2; audit 015 §A2.
design: DECISION (settled): prefer making historical catch-up mandatory for ALL bridge-checkpointed automata (uniform) over restructuring ack ordering; only a pure-stream automaton that cannot scan history gets its ack moved downstream of processing. TARGETS: runtime/jetstream_consumer.rs:322-331 (ack site), stream/runner/listener.rs RunnerConfirmedEventHandler, automaton_runtime.rs:224-239 (catch-up), :267-270 (checkpoint cadence), :530 (DeliverPolicy::New assumption — you are making its catch-up assumption universally true). PRECONDITION: audit feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery first — its per-automaton confirmed-events consumers may already reshape this; do not write against master if the merge (sinex-egs) lands first.
acceptance_criteria: kill -9 sinexd with a backlog in the mpsc channel; on restart every automaton's derived output covers the gap (DB-verifiable rows for the lost window). Code comments claiming duplicates-only are corrected to honest at-least-once semantics. VERIFY: e2e crash-recovery test (pattern: tests/e2e node crash tests); xtask test -p sinex-e2e-tests -E 'test(crash)'.
notes:
2026-07-04 catch-up proof slices: PR #2299 made ACK-after-enqueue repairable for bridge-backed automata by mandating historical catch-up before live confirmed-event consumption. Follow-up branch feature/runtime/automata-bridge-contract tests that all currently registered automata are covered by that bridge repair contract via AutomatonSpec::contract. Remaining vxu AC is the kill -9/e2e backlog crash proof.

2026-07-04 deterministic repair phase on feature/runtime/automaton-lossless-catchup: generic bridge-managed automata now load their saved bridge checkpoint before historical catch-up and before subscribing to the live confirmed-event tail. Regression proves catch-up starts from the saved bridge checkpoint, which is the mechanism that repairs acked-but-unprocessed in-process queue contents after crash. Verification jobs: 2001684 focused automaton_bridge tests passed; 2001685 xtask check -p sinexd passed. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/automaton-lossless-catchup-20260704T1356Z. Remaining vxu AC: literal kill -9 hosted sinexd with mpsc backlog and DB-verifiable derived output coverage after restart; existing e2e fixtures do not yet provide that hosted automaton process-kill harness.

title: Stream retention jams pipeline: bounded confirmed-events + WorkQueue decision for raw
description:
Sustained ingestion wedges the pipeline: Limits+discard:New streams fill (2M msgs), rejecting publishes -> the durability gate (raw-ack gated on confirmed publish) turns rejection into an unbounded redelivery storm. Interim fix already applied: confirmed-events -> discard: Old (safe: Postgres is the archive).

Remaining structural work:
1. confirmed-events -> RetentionPolicy::Interest (drain once every durable automaton consumer acks; verify ephemeral SSE consumers do NOT pin retention).
2. raw events stream: if event-engine is the sole consumer (automata on confirmed-events, replay via source scan, DLQ separate), RetentionPolicy::WorkQueue keeps it to in-flight size — verify consumer topology first.
3. self-observation firehose volume for dev loop (lower-rate/sampled profile) — overlaps telemetry-lane issue.
4. Live reconfiguration: create_or_update_stream may not change discard/retention on existing streams — prod/dev streams may need explicit recreation.

Evidence: 2026-06-29 dogfood stall at exactly 2M confirmed-events, 12,661 max-messages errors.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03 — this SUPERSEDES the gh#2204 body's "Interest is the correct shape" claim):

confirmed-events steady state = LIMITS-BASED + discard:Old (already applied as interim), kept deliberately — paired with the mandatory DB catch-up contract (sinex-vxu: DeliverPolicy::New + startup catch-up from Postgres is how any consumer reads pre-subscription history; the stream is a bounded delivery bus, Postgres is the archive).

WHY NOT Interest (the reconciliation): Interest retention deletes a message once all bound durable consumers ack — but (a) an ORPHANED durable consumer (the consumer-name-embeds-filter-list problem: changing an automaton's input set strands the old durable) never acks, so Interest re-creates exactly the fill-and-jam this issue exists to kill, just via a different mechanism; (b) new-automaton history reads would silently depend on retention accidents instead of the DB catch-up contract. Interest becomes eligible ONLY after consumer-GC exists (runtime startup deletes durables matching the service prefix but not current names — tracked in the perf packet) AND a test proves ephemeral SSE consumers don't pin retention. Until both hold, Limits+discard:Old is strictly safer and loses nothing (tail loss recovers from Postgres).

raw events stream: WorkQueue iff verified single-consumer — check consumer topology in dev AND prod (only the event-engine durable may exist; automata are on confirmed-events, replay re-ingests via source scan, DLQ is separate). Add a startup assertion that fails loud if another durable appears on the raw stream. Live reconfiguration caveat stands: create_or_update_stream cannot change discard/retention in place — plan explicit stream recreation/migration for existing dev+prod streams.

self-observation volume prong: superseded by the telemetry-lane design (sinex-x9i) — reflection gets its own stream; do not implement sampling here.
acceptance_criteria: Sustained-load dev run (2026-06-29 repro shape) never jams: confirmed-events bounded by limits+discard:Old with consumers recovering tail from DB catch-up (test: force a consumer >max_messages behind, verify it heals); raw stream WorkQueue only with the startup single-consumer assertion in place and passing; stream recreation/migration executed explicitly for existing dev+prod streams; a written note in the PR states the Interest preconditions (consumer-GC + ephemeral non-pinning proof) for future revisit.
notes:
2026-07-03 raw-consumer topology slice: live dev NATS before restart showed DEV_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS with stale pre-consolidation automaton durables causing ~1.15M aggregate pending while event-engine-dev itself was caught up. Branch feature/runtime/raw-consumer-topology adds startup reconciliation: keep only event-engine durable on raw, delete legacy sinex-*-automaton-confirmed-v2 durables from raw, reject any other unexpected raw durable. Live proof after restarting dev sinexd job 2001424: raw stream consumer_count=1, consumer=event-engine-dev, pending=0, ack_pending=1, redelivered=0. Remaining sinex-9u4 scope: WorkQueue/recreation decision, confirmation watermark stream cleanup via sinex-egs, DLQ triage.

2026-07-04 automaton catch-up prerequisite strengthened on feature/runtime/automaton-lossless-catchup: bridge-managed automata now load the saved bridge checkpoint before DB historical catch-up, supporting the bounded confirmed-events stream model in nats-transport-decision-record.md. This does not close 9u4: raw WorkQueue migration, explicit dev/prod stream recreation, and sustained-load jam proof remain.

2026-07-04 confirmed-consumer GC slice on feature/runtime/confirmed-consumer-gc: startup now deletes stale confirmed-event durable consumers for the same automaton service root, including old filter-sibling names. Live proof after patched all-automatons job 2001689: confirmed stream DEV_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_CONFIRMED consumer_count dropped 19 -> 16; stale interval-lift durables with pending 5787, 6022, and 961 disappeared; current interval-lift durable pending=0/ack_pending=0. Verification jobs: 2001686 focused confirmed_consumer_retirement/confirmed_filter_subject tests passed; 2001687 xtask check -p sinexd passed. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/confirmed-consumer-gc-live-20260704T1412Z. This satisfies the consumer-GC prerequisite for revisiting Interest retention, but does not close 9u4: raw WorkQueue migration/recreation decision and sustained-load jam proof remain.

2026-07-04 raw WorkQueue migration slice on feature/runtime/raw-workqueue-recreation: disposable NATS experiment proved Limits->WorkQueue cannot be updated in place (error 10052), so runtime now recreates only a drained raw stream. Guard requires one expected event-engine consumer, no unexpected consumers, pending=0, ack_pending=0, redelivered=0, and ack_floor >= stream tail. Live dev proof: DEV_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS migrated from Limits with 195157 retained messages / 2147483533 bytes to WorkQueue discard:Old with 0 messages / 0 bytes and one event-engine-dev consumer. Runtime jobs 2001692 core and 2001693 automata are running. Verification jobs: 2001690 raw_workqueue_recreation tests passed; 2001691 xtask check -p sinexd passed. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/raw-workqueue-migration-live-20260704T1426Z. Remaining 9u4: sustained-load no-jam proof and production deployment/recreation proof if production NATS is re-enabled.
2026-07-05 stream-retention behavior proof slice: added focused sinexd NATS tests in crate/sinexd/src/runtime/jetstream_streams_test.rs proving confirmed-events Limits+discard:Old stays bounded and accepts publishes beyond max_messages, and raw WorkQueue removes acked messages. Verification: xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(confirmed_discard_old_stream_accepts_past_message_cap) | test(raw_workqueue_stream_removes_acked_messages)' job 2001781 passed 2/2; xtask check --changed-strict origin/master job 2001782 passed for sinexd. Earlier e2e proof attempt job 2001777 failed before tests while building runtime binary with SIGTERM, so this proof uses the cheaper crate-local NATS behavior surface. Remaining sinex-9u4 scope after this slice: full sustained-load no-jam/live proof and production stream-recreation proof if production NATS is re-enabled.

title: Attention sources unusable: activitywatch empty content, hyprland bulk-snapshot timestamps
description:
The two operator-attention sources ingest unusable data — this is what blocks a genuine multi-source "what was I doing around T" demo:
1. activitywatch: 2,397,839 events, EVERY one has empty app AND title (buckets ingest empty strings; url also empty).
2. wm.hyprland: 582K events bulk-imported at duplicate timestamps (107,344 at one second) — periodic full-snapshot dumps, not focus transitions; titles are kitty spinner frames.

Fix: (a) repair activitywatch extraction to populate app/title/url from aw buckets; (b) switch wm.hyprland to a real-time Hyprland IPC focus-event stream with true per-event timestamps.

Directly improves the recall-v2 campaign (fs+git+shell+browser still work without it, but attention reconstruction needs these). Verification SQL in gh#2197. Notes: .agent/scratch/research-crosssource-demo.md.
design: TWO INDEPENDENT FIXES: (a) activitywatch — the extraction reads aw bucket rows but lands empty app/title/url on 2.4M events; diagnose whether the aw SQLite datastr JSON keys are mis-addressed (aw stores event data as JSON in datastr; keys differ per watcher: app/title for window watcher, url/title/audible for web watcher) vs empty-at-source; fix the parser to per-bucket-type key mapping and REPLAY the material through the normal pipeline (this is exactly what replay is for — the 2.4M bad interpretations archive, fresh correct ones mint); (b) wm.hyprland — replace bulk snapshot ingestion with a real-time IPC subscription driver (Hyprland socket2 events: activewindow/workspace/focusedmon) emitting per-transition events with RealtimeCapture ts_orig; the 582K snapshot-era events with duplicate timestamps get archived via replay-with-current-rules or an explicit lifecycle archive (decide by whether any consumer wants them — likely archive, they are attention-useless). SEQUENCING: (a) unblocks attention.stream's browser/app legs immediately; (b) is a new driver following the source-pattern (register_source_contract + continuous run loop on the IPC socket). Verification SQL in gh#2197.
acceptance_criteria: activitywatch events carry non-empty app/title (and url for web buckets) — SQL: count FILTER (WHERE coalesce(payload->>'title','')='') drops to ~0 on new ingestion; wm.hyprland becomes a real-time IPC focus-event stream with true per-event timestamps (no more 100K-events-per-second snapshot clusters); a cross-source reconstruction over a live window shows attention data interleaved with terminal/journald. The activitywatch fix lands via replay — closing comment records archived-count vs fresh-count for the material; the hyprland driver emits single events per focus transition with distinct timestamps under live window switching.
notes: 2026-07-05 derived ActivityWatch heartbeat repair proof: interval-lift now buffers zero-duration activitywatch/window.active and afk.changed heartbeat markers by state-kind/bucket and merges same-subject markers within a 30s window before emitting state.interval. This follows Lynchpin's raw-vs-derived split: raw AW rows keep upstream SQLite truth; derived intervals repair heartbeat spam for attention/recall consumers. Verification: job 2001805 passed xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(interval_lift)' --no-reuse (18 passed); job 2001806 passed xtask check -p sinexd. Remaining #2197 gaps: terminal/journald rows are not present in the same 2026-07-05 09:14-09:47+02 attention proof window, and ActivityWatch replay/archive fresh-vs-archived counts are not proven.

title: Source checkpoint silent loss: remaining prongs (page-0, journalctl cursor, bash dedup, browser context)
description:
STATUS CORRECTION vs the GitHub body: the headline prong (cursor advanced+persisted BEFORE emit in adapter_source.rs, = Fable packet P1) is FIXED on local master commit 6b07b0078 "fix(runtime): advance adapter cursors after emit" — NOT yet pushed/PR'd (see the master-reconcile issue). Verify that commit covers snapshot+historical+continuous paths, then treat prong 1 as done.

Remaining prongs:
2. Page-0 crash indistinguishable from source-exhausted (api_cursor.rs:413,459): page-0 records carry cursor_after=None; a mid-page-0 crash checkpoint equals fully-drained -> rest of page 0 skipped forever. A test currently ASSERTS the broken behavior.
3. Synthetic frame:N cursor corrupts journalctl resume (journalctl_stream.rs:188) — passed later as --after-cursor=frame:N which journalctl rejects/mis-seeks.
4. SharedJournalctlStream always spawns cursor=None, ignoring persisted checkpoints.
5. bash dedup window not checkpointed -> re-emits on rotation crash.
6. browser history declares Metadata at contract but emits Clipboard per-event (misrouted URL redaction).

Privacy chokepoint itself audited CLEAN (redact_batch gates the sole insert path). Per-finding file:line+scenario+fix+AC: .agent/scratch/research-audit-ingestion.md.
design: Settled per-prong fixes (research-audit-ingestion.md has file:line + scenario + draft AC per finding): page-0 -> make an explicit 'page 0 incomplete' cursor state distinguishable from exhausted, and fix the test that asserts the broken behavior; journalctl -> never synthesize frame:N cursors, treat missing __CURSOR as un-checkpointable record (emit but do not advance, or route to debt); SharedJournalctlStream -> spawn from the persisted checkpoint; bash dedup window -> include window in checkpoint state; browser -> emit with the contract-declared Metadata context. Note the generic cursor-before-emit fix landed at 6b07b0078 — pattern-match its shape for the remaining prongs.
acceptance_criteria: Per prong: a focused test simulating the fault (crash mid-page-0, missing __CURSOR, rotation crash) shows no silent skip — record re-read or visible in DLQ/debt. The page-0 test asserting broken behavior is inverted. xtask test -p sinexd on the touched test files passes; changed-strict passes.
notes:
2026-07-05 Codex partial implementation: closed prong 3 by refusing to synthesize journalctl frame:N cursors when __CURSOR is absent, with test_cursor_after_missing_cursor_is_not_checkpointable proving the record is not checkpointable as a fake cursor; closed prong 6 by aligning browser history per-event privacy_context and parser manifest to ProcessingContext::Metadata, with browser_history_intent_uses_metadata_privacy_context proving emitted intents match the contract. Verification: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(journalctl)' passed invocation 2005751 (19 tests); CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd --test browser_history_parser_test passed invocation 2005752 (6 tests); CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask check --changed-strict origin/master passed, affected package sinexd. Remaining prongs: page-0 incomplete/exhausted state, SharedJournalctlStream persisted checkpoint wiring, bash dedup checkpoint window.

2026-07-05 Codex cursor batch: closed prong 2 by adding explicit ApiCursorPosition.page_incomplete state, so a page-0 mid-page checkpoint with last_cursor=None re-fetches page 0 instead of being treated as terminal exhaustion; added page_zero_mid_page_checkpoint_refetches_page_zero and legacy-terminal compatibility coverage. Closed prong 4 at the shared-stream API boundary by adding SharedJournalctlStream::from_cursor / with_capacity_from_cursor and passing the recovered JournalctlCursor into the underlying JournalctlStreamAdapter::open path instead of hardcoding None; existing journalctl_args_resume_after_runtime_cursor coverage proves runtime cursor precedence over config/default start. Verification: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(api_cursor) | test(journalctl)' passed invocation 2005764 (37 tests); CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask check --changed-strict origin/master passed (sinexd only). Resource note: first two attempts were earlyoom-killed during sinexd rustc at available RAM ~=5%; pausing lynchpin-materialize plus btrfs metadata/btrbk timers/services gave enough headroom. Remaining sinex-jda scope: bash dedup window checkpointing.
2026-07-05 Codex final checkpoint batch: closed remaining bash dedup window prong by adding parser-local checkpoint hooks to MaterialParser, persisting AdapterModuleState.parser_checkpoint alongside adapter cursors, and wiring bash/zsh/text history parsers to serialize/restore their ContentHashWindow. Added rollback so parser-local state is restored when parse/emit/conversion fails and the cursor stays behind. Also fixed merge_cursor_update so JSON null from one ChainedCursor leg means no update instead of clearing the existing leg. Regression proof: checkpointed_dedup_window_suppresses_rotation_overlap_after_restart, snapshot_round_trips_recent_hashes, adapter_source_updates_parser_checkpoint_after_successful_parse, adapter_emit_failure_rolls_back_parser_checkpoint, adapter_cursor_update_preserves_chained_leg_state. Verification: focused broad probe CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(adapter_source) | test(dedup) | test(bash_history)' compiled and ran 49 tests; invocation 2005768 had 48 pass and exposed the chained-cursor merge bug, then targeted rerun invocation 2005769 passed 5/5 after the fix. CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask check --changed-strict origin/master passed: changed-strict: 2 packages checked, all passed (sinex-primitives, sinexd). All sinex-jda / gh-2198 named prongs are now implemented.
close_reason: Completed: all #2198 source-checkpoint loss prongs are fixed and covered by focused tests; changed-strict passed for sinex-primitives and sinexd.

title: Replay crash between archive-commit and re-ingest loses events permanently
description:
CRITICAL silent data loss, verified against source. Archive cascade commits in one TX (collect.rs:333, correct), but a crash between the committed archive and re-emission (replay_writer.rs:324) is unrecoverable: commit_recovery (state_machine.rs:1152/1193) only sets state=Failed, never restores the archived cascade. Events survive only in audit.archived_events; nothing re-creates them. Host has demonstrated OOM-kill history.

Related HIGH in same subsystem: core.execute_cascade_restore uses ON CONFLICT (id) — occurrence-blind; a restore against re-emitted events can produce TWO live interpretations per occurrence.

Fix: on crash recovery of stale EXECUTING/COMMITTING, detect archive-committed-but-not-reemitted and restore the archived cascade (same path as in-process abort) before marking Failed — or make the boundary durable (op-log-driven resume that re-issues the scan). Key restores on (source_material_id, anchor_byte), not id.

Verify: sed -n '1152,1245p' crate/sinex-db/src/replay/state_machine.rs; rg 'restore_archived_cascade' crate/sinex-db/src/replay/. Full notes: .agent/scratch/research-audit-provenance-replay.md. = Fable packet P4.
design: DECISION: on crash recovery of a stale EXECUTING/COMMITTING op, detect archive-committed-but-not-reemitted and restore the archived cascade (compensation pattern already exists: abort_before_scan_ack / restore_archived_cascade) before marking Failed — or make the boundary durable via op-log-driven resume that re-issues the scan. The restore keys on occurrence (source_material_id, anchor_byte), never ON CONFLICT (id) — the id-keyed restore is how you get two live interpretations per occurrence. TARGETS: state_machine.rs:1152-1245 (commit_recovery), replay_writer.rs:324 (re-emission), core.execute_cascade_restore.
acceptance_criteria: kill -9 between archive-commit and scan-ack -> restart restores archived events or resumes the scan; never permanent loss; no occurrence ends up with two live interpretations (test both recovery orderings). VERIFY: replay e2e (tests/e2e replay tests) + targeted state_machine unit tests; xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(replay)'.
close_reason: Completed on master via PR #2207 / commit bd27d6147 and reverified 2026-07-03. Code evidence: ReplayStateMachine::commit_recovery restores archived replay cascades from the durable scope-invalidation journal before marking stale operations failed; execute_cascade_restore is occurrence-safe and skips archived material rows when the same (source_material_id, anchor_byte) is already live. Verification: job 2001453 passed CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(cascade_restore_skips_material_occurrence_already_live)'; job 2001454 passed CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(recover_stale_executing_restores_archived_cascade)'.

title: xtask jobs lacks a stop/cancel surface for background runtime jobs
description: During interval-lift systemd proof on 2026-07-04, xtask run module interval-lift --bg created job 2001574, but xtask jobs stop 2001574 is not a supported command. That forced PID-level cleanup for a normal devloop lifecycle operation. Add a first-class xtask jobs cancel/stop command that terminates the job process tree, records exit/cancel state in history, and is safe for long-running runtime jobs.

title: xtask proof reuse accepted stale interval-lift result after edit
description: During sinex-8y8 AFK interval-lift work on 2026-07-04, job 2001564 passed before a one-line clamp hardening edit. A subsequent plain xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(interval_lift)' returned 'Fresh result from invocation 2005414' even though the working tree had changed; I had to force job 2001565 with --no-reuse. Investigate freshness fingerprints for focused test invocations, especially unstaged source changes made shortly after a run completes, and make stale reuse impossible or loudly explain why the tree fingerprint is still valid.

title: Interval-lift live bridge skips later fresh focus rows
description: Live proof for sinex-8y8 on 2026-07-04 showed rebuilt interval-lift consumed the first fresh wm.hyprland/window.focused transition after startup and emitted derived.interval-lift/state.interval, but later fresh focus rows in the same induced focus sequence were present in core.events and not used as interval parents before artifact capture. Investigate confirmed-event live delivery, ordering, checkpoint, or durable consumer behavior for narrow event-type automata so the lifter can continuously close every subject-change interval, not only startup/historical catch-up rows. Evidence artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/interval-lift-live-20260704T0700Z/summary.json.
notes: 2026-07-04 diagnosis: live bridge did NOT skip later focus rows. Controlled focus probe delivered/acked 18 fresh wm.hyprland/window.focused confirmations through the interval-lift confirmed-event consumer, but only one interval persisted. Root cause is interval_id/equivalence_key using a restored local sequence; fresh kitty interval collided with existing interval:desktop.focus:<subject>:<sequence> rows and was suppressed by event-engine equivalence_key admission. Fix in progress: key interval identity by state_kind + subject + start_parent_event_id + end_parent_event_id, matching the SourceContract occurrence_identity.
close_reason: Fixed. Interval identity now uses state_kind + subject + start/end parent event ids instead of a restored local sequence counter. Targeted interval-lift tests passed (job 2001546); sinexd package check passed (job 2001547/fresh reuse); fixed runtime job 2001548 live-probed WeeChat -> Sinex kitty -> WeeChat and persisted the previously suppressed kitty interval with parent-id equivalence key.

title: Keep xtask run automaton targets in sync with sinexd registry
description: xtask run all-automatons and xtask run list use a hard-coded AUTOMATON_TARGETS list in xtask/src/commands/run.rs. After adding attention-stream, sinexd's runtime registry can run it via SINEX_AUTOMATA_ENABLED=all, but xtask run list/all-automatons still omit it. Fix the tooling so dev runtime proof does not require an env workaround and add a regression test that registered hosted automata are visible in the xtask run surface.
close_reason: Fixed by adding attention-stream to xtask run's automaton target list and updating run/completions tests. Verification: job 2001520 passed focused xtask run target tests; job 2001521 passed xtask check -p xtask; xtask run list --json now exposes attention-stream.

title: Make pre-push xtask rebuild path observable and cheap
description: Why this exists: pushing PR #2305 rebuilt checkout-local xtask through the pre-push changed-strict hook because this branch touched xtask/src and the hook rejected stale/direnv wrapper candidates. The behavior was correct but opaque and expensive enough to look like a reliability failure. What needs to be done: make the pre-push path explain why each candidate is rejected, expose whether branch changes actually require rebuilding xtask, and prefer a warm checkout-local binary path when safe without bypassing changed-strict. Acceptance: a branch that does not touch xtask build inputs uses an existing current xtask binary without nix develop fallback; a branch that does touch xtask build inputs emits a compact reason and rebuilds once through the intended checkout-local path; tests or shell-level smoke cover both decisions.
close_reason: Completed: pre-push now reports whether a branch changed xtask build inputs, uses a warm checkout-local/PATH/cache xtask for non-xtask branches without rebuild, requires fresh binaries for xtask-changing branches with per-candidate rejection reasons, and exposes SINEX_PRE_PUSH_DRY_RUN=1 for shell-level selector smokes. Verification: bash -n .githooks/pre-push; SINEX_PRE_PUSH_DRY_RUN=1 .githooks/pre-push; BASE_REF=HEAD^ SINEX_PRE_PUSH_DRY_RUN=1 .githooks/pre-push; git diff --check.

title: Make infra smoke --run-core non-disruptive when dev runtime is live
description: Why this exists: sinex-egs closure wants xtask infra smoke --run-core evidence, but the command currently fails with INFRA_SMOKE_NOT_STOPPED when the current checkout dogfood infra is already running unless the operator permits --reset-first or a stop. During an active devloop that makes the closure proof disruptive. What needs to be done: add an explicit non-disruptive smoke mode or teach --run-core to use an isolated temporary namespace/job when checkout infra is already live, preserving the current runtime while still proving managed core startup/observe/cancel behavior. Acceptance: xtask infra smoke --run-core can produce closure-grade evidence without stopping the existing dogfood runtime; the old reset-first behavior remains available for destructive lifecycle tests.
close_reason: Completed: added xtask infra smoke --allow-running so run-core proof can observe an already-running dogfood stack without stopping Postgres, NATS, or sinexd. Live verification 2026-07-03T22:44:47Z: xtask infra smoke --allow-running --run-core passed; baseline and final state both running; observed existing sinexd pid 2230434.

title: Avoid cold schema bootstrap rebuilds for narrow proofs
description: Observed while working sinex-9u4 on 2026-07-03: focused xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(raw_stream_reconciliation)' was earlyoom-killed twice at default test-profile codegen-units=256 (rustc RSS 8.2-8.7 GiB; earlyoom threshold 5% RAM). Retrying with CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 succeeded, but it forced a cold schema-apply bootstrap derivation and then a broad dependency compile: 410s compile overhead for 0.03s of test body. xtask should provide a first-class low-memory/narrow-proof path that does not route through a cold bootstrap rebuild when the checkout-local schema is already prepared, or otherwise explains and caches that bootstrap work predictably.

title: Structural-join demo tier: capture.coincidence + provenance_pack (zero new capture)
description: The cheapest real L3 composites, available TODAY from provenance columns while interval-lift is being built: capture.coincidence (containment-join over one source_material's co-events - 'these observations came from one capture moment') and derivation.provenance_pack (provenance-join: any derived event to its material roots). They demo the join algebra with today's data and today's columns.

title: Close the failure loop: verify postmortem to next session's context seed
description: xtask test failures and their postmortems should seed the NEXT session's recall so agents do not rediscover the same failure. Today failures live in xtask-history.db and are not surfaced at session start. Polylogue close-the-failure-loop analog.

title: A read-model primitive: materialization-state, rebuild, and readiness unified
description: recall, context_pack, projections, and CAs are built ad-hoc with no unifying abstraction (018 meta-gap). nbi.3 migrates documents off the DB trigger toward a projection mechanism - that mechanism SHOULD BE the read-model primitive, so the convergence-contract has one place to read materialization state from.

title: change.episode: the self-hosting cold-start demo
description: agent-session (from devloop-dogfood) time-joined and entity-joined with git commits, xtask history, and CI to render 'this change: intent, toil, verification, outcome'. The one derivation guaranteed dense, real, and present on ANY deployment - the deployment's own construction feeds it - which makes it the cold-start answer for horizontal expansion.

title: Derivation DSL: query expressions as composable, agent-buildable derivations
description: SinexQuery already parses typed queries over events/entities/relations/materials (parse_sinex_query in crate/sinex-primitives/src/query_units.rs) and ORDER BY is landing (sinex-882). Extend into DERIVATION expressions: derivation := input_query (join input_query)* to aggregation to output_kind@lane. A new composite becomes a lane record (DATA), compiled to an AutomatonSpec - not hand-written Rust. This operationalizes algebraic composability and makes composites agent-buildable: an agent writes a derivation expression, the control plane runs it as a shadow lane, the diff evaluates it, a judgment promotes it.

title: Formalize the 4-operator join algebra in the derivation catalog
description: On adopting dec-join, record the formal core in crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md: every composite is (time | entity | provenance | containment)+ aggregation + interpretation, where time/entity are semantic (need lifting/resolution first) and provenance/containment are structural (free from provenance columns today). Reclassify screen.grounding as a containment-join (exact) rather than a time-join (fuzzy).

title: Beads-native work loop: session-bead cross-links and archive-rendered work history
description: Agent sessions and beads should cross-link so work history is queryable from the substrate rather than reconstructed. Polylogue beads-native-work-loop analog; polylogue-lio already tracks a cross-repo devloop-on-beads contract for Sinex parity.

title: Light the evidence graph: entities and relations as a query target
description: entities and relations are ALREADY QueryUnitId query units (query_units.rs) but the entity/relation lane is write-only. Promote it to a queryable knowledge graph - the cheap CONSUME half of dec-entity. Tier-gated so heuristic relations return tier:heuristic, honest by construction.

title: Legibility campaign: Nix-for-personal-data framing, skim ladder, one-command demo
description: The positioning frame that makes the architecture legible in one sentence: declarative convergence + provenance + replay = the Nix analogy for personal data (declarative schema apply not migrations; replay = reproducibility; CAS material + provenance = the content-addressed store). Plus a README artifact-first skim ladder (L0-L3 distillation) and a one-command public demo (30s to first result). Polylogue legibility-campaign import.

title: Decision: adopt the 4-operator join algebra (time, entity, provenance, containment)
description: derivation_catalog.md states two join keys (time-join on ts_orig intervals, entity-join on resolved ids) and 'a third ad-hoc key = a missing entity kind'. That is right for SEMANTIC joins but omits two STRUCTURAL joins intrinsic to the provenance model: provenance-join along the source_event_ids DAG (already the trace/replay substrate) and containment-join on shared source_material_id + overlapping anchor ranges. Both are available TODAY from provenance columns and are the cheapest honest L3 - and several catalog composites filed under time-join (screen.grounding) are actually containment-joins done exactly instead of fuzzily.

title: Dogfood the conductor devloop state into the Sinex substrate
description: The .agent/conductor-devloop state (OPERATING-LOG.md, HANDOFF-LATEST.md, EVENTS.jsonl) is markdown/JSONL OUTSIDE Sinex - the silo the devloop is fighting. The self-observation lens says Sinex's own construction is its richest always-present derivation testbed. Evidence: OPERATING-LOG.md appears in the live fs recall window as a plain file, not as events. Publish devloop state as events via the producer protocol so change.episode can consume it.

title: Decision: bound the wildcard fan-in (five automata reading *)
description: Five automata read the wildcard event stream (session, analytics, entity_extractor, tag_applier, and the summarizers via activity). This is the automaton-side of the telemetry-lane lesson: x9i separated self-observation for the ENGINE, not for the automaton fan-out. Every new *-reader is a linear cost on the busiest stream.

title: Adopt an area:* label namespace across the bead graph
description: Sinex bead labels are ad-hoc single words (capture, recall, derivation, data-loss...). Polylogue's navigability comes from an area:* namespace plus orthogonal cross-cutting labels. Small change, disproportionate leverage on every future planning turn.
close_reason: Executed this session: 97 open beads relabeled via scripted mapping (label->area precedence, epic-prefix fallback); verified every open bead carries exactly one area: label. Namespace as designed plus area:architecture added for the nbi family (17 -> 18 areas). Legacy single-word labels retained (non-destructive; existing bd list -l queries keep working). Verify: bd list --json -n 0 | check area: labels — 151/151 exactly-one.

title: Producer protocol as a public contract: any tool becomes a source, no Rust adapter
description: polylogue already publishes EventIntent to NATS with no sinexd consumer process (the external-producer pattern; see crate/sinexd/src/sources/source_contracts/polylogue.rs). Promote it to a DOCUMENTED public wire contract plus self-serve schema registration, so scripts/agents/other tools/phone apps become sources trivially. This is how Sinex scales in source VARIETY horizontally at zero core cost - the app-store surface.

title: xtask: serialize checkout-local schema bootstrap before job queueing
description: Concurrent xtask invocations can run checkout-local SQLx/Postgres schema bootstrap before the coordinated job slot is assigned. During recall verification on 2026-07-03, launching test/check commands in parallel emitted duplicate pg_class_relname_nsp_index errors for ix_archived_events_archived_at / ix_archived_events_ts_orig, even though xtask later queued jobs behind the active slot. This is confusing observability and a real reliability smell: the queue boundary should cover schema bootstrap, or schema apply must be idempotent under concurrent bootstrap.
design: Move checkout-local SQLx schema bootstrap behind the same coordinated slot used by xtask jobs, or add a per-checkout bootstrap lock before apply. Do not paper over this by telling agents never to use --bg; xtask should make queued parallel verification safe and quiet. Evidence jobs: 2001381-2001383, with foreground wrapper output showing duplicate pg_class_relname_nsp_index while jobs were queued/running.
acceptance_criteria: Parallel xtask test/check launches against one checkout no longer emit duplicate-index schema-apply errors; one command owns bootstrap and the others wait or reuse the completed bootstrap. Add a regression test or smoke command that starts two bootstrap-needing xtask invocations concurrently and asserts one bootstrap path, no pg_class_relname_nsp_index errors, and correct job queue status.

title: Moment search / evidence windows: seed hits become intervals, intervals gather heterogeneous evidence, output states support and overreach
description: The flagship consumption capability of the substrate claim, and a genuinely new query shape: 'commands I ran while discussing X' is not an event search — it is a MOMENT search. Pipeline: (1) SEED: full-text/semantic hits for X in agent messages (or any content family) produce candidate anchor points; (2) WINDOW: anchors lift to candidate time intervals (session-bounded, gap-merged — interval logic shared with attention.stream, not reinvented); (3) GATHER: within each window, pull heterogeneous evidence across families — shell commands, window focus, browser visits, fs activity, git operations, machine context — via the normal ts_orig window queries; (4) REPORT: for each moment, state SUPPORT (evidence consistent with the seed topic), CAVEATS (families with no coverage in the window — from source-continuity data), CONTRADICTIONS (evidence inconsistent with the claim), and TEMPORAL OVERREACH (the seed discussed X at 14:02-14:15; commands at 14:40 are adjacent, not during — the boundary is stated, not smoothed). This is where Sinex-as-backend pays: heterogeneous evidence without forcing everything into Polylogue's chat schema. Polylogue consumes it for 'what was happening around this session'; the operator consumes it for any 'while' question; agents consume it via MCP.
design: DECISIONS: (1) build as a composition over existing primitives — seed search (docs/semantic search + payload text match), interval lifting (shared L2 helper), window evidence pull (the recall/context substrate) — the new code is the orchestration + the four-verdict report shape, NOT a parallel query engine (algebra rule). (2) Surface: sinexctl moments <query> [--families ...] [--range ...] + an events/context RPC + MCP tool; output = moment cards, each with interval, seed refs, per-family evidence refs, and the four verdict sections; every claim in the report is a resolvable ref. (3) Support/contradiction classification starts MECHANICAL (family presence, term co-occurrence, count thresholds — each a measured quantity per standing rule 1); LLM-graded relevance is a later recorded-effect enrichment (k4c.1 lane), never a hardcoded score. (4) Coverage caveats reuse the source-continuity machinery from recall verbatim. (5) Latency: moment search is explicitly a bounded-work operation (N windows cap, per-family limits) — it inherits the g3k read-model outcome; do not promise interactive full-archive moments before that lands. PITFALL: seeds from agent messages need the observation vocabulary sibling landed first for message-grain anchors; the fallback seed source (documents/browser content) works today — build the pipeline against what exists, add the agent family when the lane flows.
acceptance_criteria: The motivating query works end-to-end: given a topic discussed in a known session, sinexctl moments returns the discussion interval(s) with the shell commands actually run during them, families absent from the window listed as caveats, and adjacent-but-outside evidence marked as overreach rather than included silently; all refs resolve; JSON stable for MCP; a falsifier run (topic never discussed) returns zero moments, not fabricated ones.

title: Polylogue observation vocabulary: the full normalized domain as anchored Sinex events
description: Layer 2->3 of the epic: replace the metadata-thin session_indexed lane with the complete normalized-observation vocabulary. Kinds (each a stable-semantics observation, per the epic's admission boundary): agent.session.{started,resumed,compacted,ended} (Point/transition; compaction is a first-class observation — it explains context loss in any later forensic question); agent.message.emitted (Point; role, model, block summary, content BY ANCHOR into session JSONL material + content hash — never embedded text); agent.tool_call.{started,returned} (Point pair; tool name, args digest, outcome class, duration — the span the work-packet derivation lifts into intervals); agent.subagent.{started,stopped}; agent.attachment.referenced (ref to the attachment material); polylogue.export.imported (provider export landed — ts_orig = export's content time span, NOT import time); polylogue.annotation.{marked,read,analyzed} (operator/agent actions on sessions — intentional-role events); polylogue.usage.observed (provider usage/accounting rows — the cost plane). Producer stays the polylogued daemon (external-producer mode, EventIntent to NATS — no sinexd-side consumer process). Materials: per-session JSONL registered with growing-file re-registration (content-hash + size watermark); attachments as CAS blobs; provider export archives as import materials.
design: DECISIONS: (1) every kind declares (catalog extension rules) temporal character, occurrence key (session_id + JSONL line identity for message/tool kinds; provider row identity for usage), ts_orig quality (IntrinsicContent — provider/JSONL timestamps) and consumer derivation (work.episode/change.episode for session+tool kinds; consumption/cost views for usage; moment search for messages). (2) Volume class declared up front: messages ~low-millions total, comparable to page.visited — admissible at full grain; tool calls similar order; usage tiny. Backfill of the historical archive runs PACED (sinex-2n9 mechanism) — do not repeat the journald incident with chat history. (3) Occurrence identity must survive Polylogue re-parses: JSONL line identity = (material, anchor_byte) with equivalence key from (session_id, message/provider id) so a re-normalized session does not duplicate observations. (4) No polylogue.source_material.observed — epic correction 2. (5) Schema evolution: kinds versioned via semantics_version; Polylogue-side normalization changes replay through the normal pipeline (archive + re-derive). CONSTRUCT-VALIDITY WIRING (what consumes this, named now): moment/evidence-window search (sibling child), change.episode/work.episode composites (a4w.3.9/.4), cost/accounting query units, recall timeline family 'agent'.
acceptance_criteria: All kinds flowing from live polylogued against real sessions; a sampled agent.message.emitted resolves via show -> material anchor to the exact JSONL line and the provider timestamp equals ts_orig; compaction events present for a session known to have compacted; historical backfill of the archive completes paced with counts recorded; re-running Polylogue normalization over an already-ingested session creates zero duplicate occurrences; recall --window over a coding hour shows the agent family interleaved with terminal/git.

title: Agent arrival: MCP profile registration + ambient injection + self-teaching tool surface
description: The place agents ARRIVE. The MCP server already exposes the right tools (sinex.context_pack/query/search_events + 40 more in crate/sinexctl/src/mcp.rs) but no agent profile loads it — sinnix's mcp-registry (flake/data/mcp-registry.nix) registers Polylogue/Lynchpin/Serena but not Sinex, so the widest surface in the codebase has zero agent consumers. Three legs: (1) register sinex-mcp-server in the sinnix MCP registry (appropriate profiles; read-only role default); (2) ambient injection — the SessionStart hook feeding agents recent machine context (sinex-cfr executes this leg; child-linked); (3) self-teaching surface: tool descriptions + a sinex.orient tool (or MCP instructions block) that teaches a cold agent the data model — refs, materials-vs-derived, query expression shape, honest-gap semantics — so first-contact agents use it correctly without reading repo docs. Agent-tangibility test: a fresh Claude session with no Sinex knowledge answers 'what was I doing yesterday 14:00-15:00' correctly via MCP, citing resolvable refs.
design: DECISION: registration follows the existing mcp-registry.nix pattern (sinnix-side change; coordinate like other cross-repo wiring); default profile gets read-only token via runtime-target descriptor; the orient/instructions content is generated from the same source as the concepts-page ladder (sinex-ej9) so internal and external explanations never fork; cfr remains the hook leg and is not duplicated here.
acceptance_criteria: claude/codex default profiles list sinex tools; the cold-agent test passes (fresh session, correct answer, resolvable refs); orientation content generated not hand-forked; read-only by default with explicit opt-up.
notes: 2026-07-03 progress: implemented sinex.orient in crate/sinexctl/src/mcp.rs backed by shared docs crate/sinexctl/docs/agent_orientation.md; updated MCP docs/tests. Sinnix side registers sinex in the recall MCP tier for Codex/Claude/Gemini and installs mcp-sinex without forcing Sinex compilation during Home Manager activation. Verification so far: xtask test job 2001393 passed focused MCP catalog/orient/doc tests; stdio protocol proof listed 66 tools and called sinex.orient; registry eval shows sinex in lean/evidence/browser/full profile selections; Home Manager activationPackage build produced /nix/store/d1d9xxv65rkhhrplx8ia3gb6gzfkapk9-home-manager-generation. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/agent-arrival-mcp-registration-20260703T144026Z. Residual: switch/commit/PR flow in progress; package fmt check job 2001394 exposed pre-existing broader sinexctl formatting drift outside this slice.
close_reason: Completed implementation substrate: PR #2277 merged as 4cb3f5d299da471f41e6106c682576263e021566 adds sinex.orient, shared orientation docs, MCP docs, and tests; Sinnix commit 57e2f0f registers/installs mcp-sinex in agent profiles and was switched live. Focused MCP tests passed (job 2001393), pre-push changed-strict passed, and stdio proof listed/called sinex.orient. Deferred behavioral fresh-agent natural-language proof tracked by sinex-4bn.

title: The adjudication seat: a judgment queue an operator can actually clear
description: The place you JUDGE — the missing workflow for the doctrine's only path to truth. Today proposals (shadow-lane entities/relations/curation diffs) accumulate with no queue, no triage order, no fast accept/reject loop; when the semantic plane activates (sinex-5v6, sinex-2nd), an unworkable judgment UX means L4 silently stops mattering. Build: a proposal queue surface (TUI pane + CLI) ordered by evidence strength x staleness, single-keystroke judge (accept/reject/edit-then-accept/defer), batch judgment over a selection with one recorded judgment covering N proposals (the judgment event carries all proposal parents), and session summaries ('you judged 40 proposals, 3 deferred'). Delegated policy comes AFTER the manual loop proves its shape: a recorded standing judgment ('auto-accept entity-merge proposals with >=N corroborating sources from lane L') is itself an adjudication object with provenance — but do not build delegation before the manual queue has real usage evidence.
design: DECISION: judgments stay typed and recorded exactly per doctrine (§adjudication) — the UX changes throughput, never the authority model; batch = one judgment event, N proposal parents (legal: NonEmptyVec); queue ordering factors are each measured quantities surfaced in the row (standing rule 1); defer is a first-class verdict so the queue can reach zero honestly; queue age/depth are telemetry so the epic's 'judged within days' acceptance is measurable. Surfaces: sinexctl semantic judge (interactive TUI mode + scriptable flags) over the existing curation/proposal read models — check sinexctl semantic + curation_proposals MCP tool for the current read surface before adding any.
acceptance_criteria: Operator clears a real 50-proposal backlog in one sitting without leaving the TUI; every canonical write traces to a judgment event with proposal parents; queue depth/age queryable; deferred items resurface; no auto-promotion path exists.

title: Operator daily surface: today-view TUI pane + morning brief as the default entry points
description: The place you LOOK. Two entry points sharing one substrate: (1) sinexctl tui 'today' view — the attention timeline (a4w.3.1 when it lands; sessions+summaries before then), source health strip (stall states from the continuity data), pending-judgment count, latest brief — the pane an operator keeps open or opens by keybind; (2) the morning brief — the citation-required daily brief (sinex-a4w.2) delivered ambiently: written to a stable path + desktop notification + oracle-digest inclusion, so consumption requires zero initiation. The habit loop closes when checking Sinex is cheaper than not checking it.
design: DECISION: build on the existing sinexctl tui workbench — extend, never fork a new binary; every widget = an existing query unit/card rendered (no TUI-only queries); brief delivery = systemd user timer invoking sinexctl report daily --output <stable-path> + notify-send, and the sinnix oracle script includes the latest brief file (one-line change there); keybind/alias wiring in sinnix dots. Instrument invocations (consumption telemetry) so nbi.2 can measure whether the habit loop actually closed — the epic's acceptance depends on this being measurable.
acceptance_criteria: Today-view renders timeline + health + judgment count from live data with honest gaps; brief lands at a stable path every morning with a notification; both are used-in-anger for a week and the telemetry shows it; no new query paths that bypass query units.

title: Polylogue on Sinex: evidential substrate, not ontology replacement
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03, deepened from the recovered plan): take Sinex-as-Polylogue-substrate FAR — but the plan was never 'merge Polylogue into Sinex'. Polylogue stays the conversation/agent-work PRODUCT with its own semantics: it knows sessions, messages, blocks, tool calls/results, attachments, providers, agents/subagents, context compactions, exports, marks, read states, analysis notes, usage/accounting. Sinex is the generalized evidence/event substrate underneath. Full collapse is rejected — it would dissolve the product boundary into generic event-browser sludge. THE FIVE LAYERS (each must be real, see construct-validity gate in design): (1) raw material — provider exports, session JSONL/Markdown, attachments, blobs; (2) normalized Polylogue domain — the ontology above, authority stays in Polylogue; (3) Sinex event/material substrate — normalized observations with provenance, material refs, timestamps, lineage; (4) derived/correlation layer — session-commit-PR-check RELATIONS (derived, never intrinsic), moment candidates, evidence windows, Agent Work Packets, context packs, coverage caveats; (5) Polylogue UX/read models — timelines, session views, agent-work views, search, daily summaries. BACKEND TRAJECTORY (staged, both keep Polylogue standalone-functional): Phase A = deep export/sync (external-producer publishing, today's shape deepened to the full observation vocabulary + materials); Phase B = Sinex-primary evidence — Polylogue's SQLite demotes from source-of-truth to REBUILDABLE read-model/index over Sinex-held materials and events; the rebuild test (drop SQLite, reconstruct from Sinex) is the proof the substrate claim is real. A generic storage-trait 'alternative backend' middle option is skipped: what moves is EVIDENCE, not Polylogue's read models — those stay Polylogue's forever. Children carry the capability work; cross-links: hlv (bd: refs), a4w.3.9 (Agent Work Packet derivation), cem.10, 266 (@@-DSL).
design: SETTLED CORRECTIONS (operator-established; DECISION lines, do not re-litigate): (1) ADMISSION BOUNDARY: not 'selected/important events' — anything that is a normalized observation with stable semantics is admissible: session occurred/resumed/ended, message emitted, tool call started / result returned, subagent started/stopped, context compacted, attachment/material referenced, provider export imported, mark/read/analyze performed, provider usage observed. This supersedes the earlier 'no per-message events' line in this epic: messages ARE domain observations; volume is handled by anchoring (content-bearing events carry (material_id, anchor_byte) into session JSONL + metadata/hashes, never embedded bulk — catalog rule 2) and by declared volume class (catalog rule 6), not by exclusion. (2) MATERIAL IS NOT AN EVENT: no polylogue.source_material.observed or any '*.material.observed' kind — raw exports/logs/attachments are substrate (layer 1) registered in the material registry; domain observations (layer 2->3) are what become events. Violating this recurses implementation detail into the domain. (3) ts_orig IS SOURCE-DOMAIN TIME: message -> provider message timestamp; session -> actual session occurrence/start; export-import -> when the exported thing happened, not when imported. Ingestion time is ts_coided/provenance/audit — a Polylogue-on-Sinex that degrades ts_orig to 'when my daemon noticed' is broken (this is the existing temporal-ledger doctrine applied strictly). (4) NO session_commit OBJECT: sessions (Polylogue), commits/branches/PRs (git), commands/spans/checks (telemetry) are separate domains; their connection is a DERIVED relation computed in layer 4 (Agent Work Packet / evidence window), never an intrinsic event type pretending a session is a commit. (5) CONSTRUCT-VALIDITY GATE: every named layer/kind/derived object must be wired into a real read model or user-facing operation (context packs, evidence windows, work packets, timelines, accounting views, coverage caveats, audit trails) — a backend that hoards evidence without answering questions now is the named failure mode; no child of this epic closes on schema+ingestion alone. (6) ONTOLOGY AUTHORITY: Polylogue's domain model defines what a conversation/message/tool-call/compaction/agent/work-packet MEANS; Sinex stores and serves the evidence. Sinex is Polylogue's evidential substrate, not its ontology replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Phase A: the full observation vocabulary flows (child 1) with byte-anchored content events and source-domain ts_orig spot-checked against provider timestamps; Polylogue standalone suite stays green with the Sinex flag off. Phase B: the rebuild test passes — Polylogue reconstructs its SQLite read models from Sinex materials+events with a recorded parity diff; at least two user-facing operations run THROUGH the substrate (moment search child; work-packet view via a4w.3.9); no '*.material.observed' kind exists (rg proves); no intrinsic cross-domain fusion kinds exist. Every child cites which layer-4/5 surface consumes it (construct-validity gate).

title: Daily-grain rollup lane + parity: per-domain (domain, local-day) derived events replace Lynchpin daily products
description: Lynchpin's layer-3 (derived daily products: daily_signals.ndjson, spotify/daily.ndjson, web_category_daily, operator_daily) and its DuckDB substrate tables exist to make cross-domain daily correlation cheap. Sinex replacement: a general per-domain daily-rollup lane — windowed/scope-reconciler automata keyed (domain, local-day) emitting typed daily summary events (activity, terminal, web, media, physiological once rkv.6 lands, machine telemetry) — plus query units over them (sinex-lv8 direction) so 'join domains on day' is one query expression, not an NDJSON copy step. The existing hourly/daily summarizers are the seed; this generalizes them from activity-only to per-domain and makes local-day (timezone-honest, cf. sinex-nbi.5) the scope key. Parity target: every field Lynchpin's daily products carried either exists in a Sinex daily event, is derivable by query, or is recorded as deliberately dropped in the ledger.
design: DECISION: one parameterized daily-rollup automaton family (domain -> input filter + reducer), not N bespoke automata; scope = (domain, local_day, tz) with DST-correct day boundaries; emitted kind activity.summary.daily.<domain> or a single kind with domain field (prefer single kind + domain field — parsimony rule); replayable (semantics_version); backfill over history via normal replay, paced (sinex-2n9). Query side: day-grain query units so 'sleep_hours x commits_count by day' is expressible in one expression — this is the acceptance query.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl query can answer a two-domain day-grain join (e.g. sleep duration vs commit count per day over a month) in one expression against derived daily events; parity table for daily_signals fields recorded in the subsumption ledger; timezone/DST day-boundary test passes.

title: Recorded-analysis lane: generalize the effect ledger from model calls to arbitrary computations
description: The abstraction improvement the subsumption forces. Lynchpin's real advantage over Sinex was never data — it was that a one-shot cross-source investigation (weather_correlations, substance_kinetics, mood_correlations) cost one Python file over DuckDB. Sinex's only sanctioned derivation shape is a Rust automaton with deployment ceremony, so ad-hoc analysis either doesn't happen or happens as untracked scratch SQL — the exact provenance hole the system exists to close. Fix: generalize the L3 doctrine ('every model call is a recorded effect') to 'every ANALYSIS is a recorded effect'. A recorded analysis = a computation over the store whose inputs (query-unit specs + as-of watermark + input event-id set digest), code identity (script/notebook hash + semantics tag), and outputs (artifact and/or derived events) are registered — making one-shot investigations replayable, auditable, and citable without automaton ceremony. This is the ModelEffectRecord pattern with 'model call' replaced by 'computation', and it is how ~30 of Lynchpin's ~40 analysis modules translate (the standing ~10 become real L3 automata).
design: DECISION SKETCH (settle details in implementation, doctrine is fixed): (1) New registry surface analysis_records: id, title, question, code_hash, code_ref (repo path or CAS blob), input_spec (query-unit expressions + as-of watermark), input_digest, started/finished, artifact refs, optional emitted derived-event ids. (2) Execution shape: sinexctl ops analysis run -- <script> (or an xtask lane for dev): wrapper snapshots the input spec, executes, registers the record, stores the artifact in CAS, and — when the analysis emits typed conclusions — publishes derived events whose parents are the input event set (bounded: for large input sets, parent = the analysis-record ref via a synthetic material or a designated provenance mode; DECIDE: derived-from-analysis provenance representation, this is the one open design question — options: (a) cap parent lists + record overflow in the analysis record, (b) analysis record as source material and conclusions as material-provenance events anchored into the record — (b) is cleaner and matches 'the record IS the evidence'). (3) Replay semantics: re-run = new record, new interpretation (never mutate); as-of watermark makes reruns comparable. (4) Language: Python/SQL scripts are fine — the LANE is Rust-registered, the analysis is not; agents author analyses as easily as Lynchpin modules. (5) Standing promotion path: an analysis that proves recurring value gets promoted to an L3 automaton (the record's question/spec is the automaton's spec). (6) The demo/report shelf artifacts and cem falsifier packets can also register through this lane — one evidence genre.
acceptance_criteria: An agent can run a cross-source one-shot analysis (e.g. reimplement mood_correlations over Sinex data) in one command; the record is queryable (sinexctl show analysis:<id>) with input digest, code hash, artifact ref; re-running with unchanged inputs+code is detectable as identical (digest match); conclusions, when emitted, carry provenance per the decided representation; one real Lynchpin analysis reproduced through the lane end-to-end as the proof.

title: Lynchpin subsumption: absorb the higher-order layers properly, retire the stopgap
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03): ~total subsumption of Lynchpin into Sinex — not just sources. Lynchpin is the dormant stopgap; Sinex is the real system. Its four layers map onto the tower, and the higher layers are the hard, valuable part. Layer map: (1) lynchpin.sources.* typed iterators -> Sinex source contracts over RAW provider data (never Lynchpin's processed intermediates); (2) lynchpin.substrate.* DuckDB read models (commit_fact, ai_work_event, machine_metric_sample) -> Sinex query units / consumer-maintained projections; (3) /realm/data/derived/lynchpin daily products (daily_signals, spotify/daily) -> L2 windowed daily-rollup automata, typed + versioned + replayable; (4) lynchpin.analysis.* (~40 modules: burnout_warning, mood_correlations, lifestyle_correlations, sleep_architecture_productivity, substance_kinetics, productivity_predictors, life_phase, operator_rhythm, weather_correlations...) -> split into standing derivations (L3 automata: baselines, anomaly, episode composites) and one-shot investigations (the recorded-analysis lane — the new abstraction this epic forces, see child bead); (5) graph/context packs -> recall/context (already Sinex's). EXCLUDED for now (operator): codebase analytics (dms/, code_index, code_quality, code_history_claims, tool_rules), thoughtspace (dead end). Existing beads that become this epic's limbs: sinex-44q (source promotion ledger), sinex-rkv.6 (health raw-fidelity), sinex-stm (import glue), sinex-a4w.3.6 (routine.baseline/anomaly), sinex-a4w.3 composites generally.
design: DOCTRINE FOR TRANSLATION (settled): Lynchpin things are not ported line-for-line — each is re-derived from what question it answered. Rule per module: name the question -> classify {standing derivation | one-shot investigation | read model | dead} -> map to {L3 automaton | recorded analysis | query unit/projection | retire}. Raw-data rule: every subsumed source re-lands on ORIGINAL provider data as materials; Lynchpin processed intermediates are never inputs (they are evidence for parity checks only). Parity-then-retire rule: a Lynchpin module turns off only when a named Sinex surface answers its question with an evidence artifact proving parity or superiority; the ledger records question -> lynchpin module -> sinex surface -> parity artifact. Daily-grain rule: cross-domain daily correlation (sleep x productivity, weather x mood) requires the (domain, local-day) rollup lane as query units — the composite plane's daily grain, not ad-hoc NDJSON.
acceptance_criteria: Every non-excluded Lynchpin module has a ledger verdict with evidence; the 'port' rows have child/linked beads; the recorded-analysis abstraction is decided and implemented (child); at least the health+sleep and daily-signals families run natively in Sinex with parity artifacts; Lynchpin runs nothing on a schedule that Sinex now answers.

title: Capture-stall alerting: sources must not silently stop capturing (push, not caveat)
description: Today a stalled source surfaces only as a pull-side caveat: the recall-v2 terminal packet itself shipped with browser.history in runtime_binding_stalled, and nobody was notified — the stall was discovered because a demo happened to render caveats. The recall caveat plumbing (source-status gap details, runtime_binding_stalled classification) already computes the right facts; what is missing is the push leg: when a source with an expected cadence stops producing output beyond its family-appropriate threshold, the operator gets an actionable desktop notification (and the fact lands as a reflexive event). This is the capability behind demo sinex-cem.2 (fault injection proves the detector); the demo must not be the only consumer. Distinct from sinex-r6d.3 (honest health = truthful check results) — this is delivery of those results to the operator without being asked.
design: DECISION: reuse existing source-continuity/source-status classification as the single detector (no parallel staleness logic). A small watcher (sinexd-internal tick or systemd timer running sinexctl sources status --format json) evaluates per-source expected-cadence thresholds from the source contract/binding (continuous sources: minutes-hours; scheduled imports: their period + slack); emits notify-send on transition into stalled and on recovery, plus a reflexive event for history. Threshold config lives with the binding, not hardcoded per source. Anti-flap: alert on state transition only.
acceptance_criteria: Killing the browser runtime binding (or the cem.2 fault injection) produces an operator notification within one evaluation period and a queryable stall event; recovery notifies once; no notification storms from a flapping source; thresholds visible in sources status output.

title: Backup/restore drill + CAS integrity scrub: make 'L0 retained forever' a verified claim
description: The doctrine says L0 (source materials + CAS blobs) is retained forever and events are a cache over materials — but nothing verifies that claim. The 2026-07 production incident proved restore had never been executed until forced; it succeeded (sinex-mhk), but as a one-off manual operation. Absent capabilities: (1) automated backup verification — prod pg backup exists per crate/sinex-db/docs/backup_restore.md but no scheduled restore-drill proves backups are restorable; (2) CAS integrity scrub — blobs are BLAKE3 content-addressed, so a scrub walking <stateRoot>/blobs/sinex-cas verifying digest-vs-content and cross-checking against raw.source_material_registry is cheap and detects bitrot/truncation/orphans in both directions; (3) beads/Dolt database inclusion in the backup story. Without this, every replay/eviction/retention design (sinex-qky, sinex-lpz) rests on an unverified premise.
design: DECISION: scrub is a sinexctl ops subcommand (registry-vs-CAS bidirectional check: missing blob, digest mismatch, orphan blob), runnable bounded (--sample N) or full; results are events (reflexive role) so honest-health can consume them. Restore drill is an operator-invoked documented procedure first (script + runbook), scheduled later; drill target is a throwaway DB, never prod. Fold into ops verify as a correctness-class probe per sinex-r6d.3.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl ops verify (or ops scrub) detects a deliberately corrupted CAS blob and a deleted-but-registered blob in a test store; restore drill runbook executed once end-to-end with row-count proof; backup coverage documented to include the beads Dolt db.

title: Stop devshell xtask launchers rebuilding schema-apply bootstrap repeatedly
description: During repeated xtask test/check/build launches on 2026-07-03, the devshell wrapper rebuilt schema-apply-bootstrap before queueing jobs, even for quick reruns. This adds ~30-60s of serial latency before useful background work starts. Investigate the flake/devshell wrapper path and make schema bootstrap reuse/cache/queue behavior explicit without bypassing SQLx correctness. Evidence jobs around 2001369-2001373 repeatedly printed 'this derivation will be built: ... schema-apply-bootstrap-0.4.2.drv' before job start.
design: DIAGNOSE FIRST (the fix depends on which layer rebuilds): evidence shows 'this derivation will be built: schema-apply-bootstrap-0.4.2.drv' repeatedly — determine whether (a) the drv inputs genuinely change per invocation (e.g. a timestamp/env/store-path leak into the derivation), (b) the wrapper calls nix build without --no-link/reuse so the result is GC'd or not cached, or (c) the wrapper unconditionally rebuilds instead of checking an existing result symlink/profile. FIX SHAPE by branch: (a) pin the leaking input; (b/c) cache the built bootstrap at a stable per-checkout path (.sinex/ or the /var/cache/sinex tree) keyed by the drv hash, and have the wrapper exec the cached result when the hash matches — rebuild only on real input change. HARD CONSTRAINT: never skip SQLx schema correctness — the cache key must cover schema defs; a stale bootstrap running against changed defs is worse than the latency.
acceptance_criteria: Back-to-back xtask check launches show zero 'will be built' schema-bootstrap lines on the second run (cold->warm proven with timestamps); a schema-defs edit DOES trigger rebuild (cache-key correctness); serial pre-job latency for warm launches drops by the measured 30-60s; root cause written in the closing comment (which branch it was).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #2286: devshell cargo/xtask wrappers now cache the schema-apply-bootstrap executable path under SINEX_DEV_STATE_DIR, keyed by a source fingerprint over Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, flake.nix, crate/sinex-schema, and crate/sinex-primitives. Warm in-devshell SQLx-needed xtask launch verified at 1.657s with no schema-apply-bootstrap build line; temporary schema definition edit triggered the rebuild path.

title: Replace recall diversity top-up with bounded shared query
description: The sinex-0tj hardening slice made recall finite and observable, but live proof still shows diversity_top_up consuming its full 8s budget and returning recall.diversity_top_up_timeout. Replace the sequential per-source events.cards loop in crate/sinexctl/src/commands/context.rs with either a bounded concurrent implementation or, preferably, a server-side source-diverse event-card query in the shared events/context substrate. Preserve algebraic query composition; do not add a recall-only silo.
design: DECISION: server-side source-diverse event-card query in the shared events/context substrate (option 2 of the 0tj packet — the bounded-concurrent CLI loop is acceptable only as an interim commit if the server query stalls). Shape: one RPC extending events.cards semantics — given a base filter + a diversity spec (source/family list), return base latest cards PLUS latest card per requested source in one disclosure pass; implemented in the composable-query layer (crate/sinex-db/src/repositories/events/composable_query.rs) as a lateral/union-per-source plan bounded by the existing indexes (ix_events_source_ts_orig), NOT a recall-only handler. CLI top_up_context_diversity (context.rs:456) collapses to one call. Keep stage timings from the 0tj instrumentation; keep the diversity budget caveat for the degraded path. PITFALL: per-source LIMIT 1 over ts_orig on the hypertable is the exact shape g3k is diagnosing — run EXPLAIN on the generated plan before declaring the latency win; if chunk-exclusion kills it, this bead's RPC consolidation still lands (1 RPC vs N) and the plan evidence hands off to g3k.
acceptance_criteria: Recall issues a bounded number of RPCs independent of missing-source count (target: 1 cards RPC + optional source-status); diversity_top_up stage time drops from 8s-budget-exhaustion to sub-second on the dev store with the EXPLAIN plan recorded; composable-query tests cover the diversity spec; no recall-only query silo (the RPC is usable by any events consumer).

title: Emit bounded multi-entity outputs from entity extractor
description: Dead-lane sweep follow-up from sinex-y7l and fixture prerequisite for sinex-a4w.1. entity_extractor currently returns only find_first_entity from a single-output transducer, hiding co-occurring URL/email/file/tool entities in one input event. Move to bounded multi-output extraction or equivalent shared runtime support.
design: DECISION: extend the extraction to bounded multi-output while keeping the Transducer runtime shape — emit ONE entity.extracted event whose payload carries a bounded entities[] array (deterministic order: by kind priority then first-occurrence offset; cap configurable, default ~8 with an overflow count field), rather than N events per input. Rationale: one input -> one interpretation event preserves 1:1 occurrence mapping and avoids multiplying the already-noisy lane (the 2h demo window showed 503 extractor events — one per input; N-per-input would make it the top volume source); consumers and the golden set (a4w.1) score the array. The single-output DerivedNodeImpl constraint (one event_type per node) is untouched — same type, richer payload. Schema: bump the payload schema + semantics_version so replay re-derives history under the new semantics when invoked. PITFALL: keep find_first_entity's kind-priority logic as the ORDER, not the FILTER.
acceptance_criteria: An input containing multiple supported entity kinds emits all bounded matches with deterministic ordering and volume limits. Existing priority/first-match behavior is replaced by tests that assert URL/email/file/tool co-occurrence. Entity golden-set scoring can evaluate all predicted entities. One entity.extracted event per input (bounded entities[] payload), never N events per input; overflow count present when the cap truncates.

title: Fix entity enricher active-hours timezone semantics
description: Dead-lane sweep follow-up from sinex-y7l. entity_enricher active_hours currently buckets by raw UTC hour, which is a misleading behavioral surface for operator-local activity. Decide and implement either operator-local timezone with explicit metadata or delete the field.
design: DECISION (settled now per design-now doctrine): operator-local timezone with explicit metadata — not deletion. Rationale: active-hours is exactly the shape routine.baseline (a4w.3.6) consumes, so the field has a named consumer derivation; raw-UTC bucketing is the bug, not the field. Implementation: bucket by the operator's local tz (from config, one authority — the same local-day source k4c.2 standardizes; do NOT invent a second tz-resolution path), stamp the payload with the tz used (tz_id field) so the value is self-describing, bump semantics_version, and replay re-derives history when invoked. While in the file: grep the automata for other raw-UTC-hour bucketing (this is the template instance of the timezone-class sweep noted on the nbi epic).
acceptance_criteria: active_hours semantics are explicit and tested: either operator-local timezone is applied with metadata/caveats, or the field is removed from payload/output and docs. Tests assert a non-UTC-offset timestamp lands in the intended bucket.

title: Add crash-window harness for checkpoint and ack ordering
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. Create a targeted harness that can inject crashes/restarts around source emit/checkpoint, automaton confirmed-event consumption, invalidation ack/recompute, and recovery-spool overflow so the loss-window fixes can be verified with more than code inspection.
design: DECISION: build inside xtask::sandbox (the intentional shared harness — do not create a parallel test substrate). Shape: a crash-window scenario runner that (1) seeds a deterministic event/material stream, (2) runs the component under test (source driver loop / automaton consumer / invalidation handler / spool) to a named INJECTION POINT, (3) kills hard (SIGKILL, not graceful shutdown — the whole point), (4) restarts and asserts the recovery contract. Injection points map to the r6d children: after-emit-before-checkpoint (r6d.4), spool-at-cap-plus-one (r6d.5), confirmed-consumer-behind-retention (r6d.6), invalidation-acked-not-recomputed (r6d.7), plus ack-on-enqueue (vxu). Injection mechanism: prefer explicit test hooks (a fail-point feature flag compiled in test builds — check for an existing pattern in the sandbox before adding one) over timing races; each scenario must be deterministic, not flaky-by-design. Assertion vocabulary: DURABLE (all seeded occurrences present after recovery, possibly as new interpretations), or REPAIRABLE (loss detected AND surfaced as an operator-visible signal — DLQ row, health event, gap caveat); silent loss = failure. Scenarios run under xtask test --heavy (they restart processes; not the default loop).
acceptance_criteria: At least the four named windows have scenarios that FAIL on the pre-fix code (prove by running against a git rev before the corresponding r6d fix, recorded once) and PASS after; each r6d.4-7 close cites its scenario; scenarios deterministic across 10 consecutive runs; the r6d.1 matrix links each window row to its harness command.

title: Classify source contracts by replayability and loss authority
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. The loss-window matrix needs source rows grouped by whether the source is a live observation, mutable snapshot, append stream, staged export, or external producer, and what authority can replay/catch up after a crash.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-4.md#sinex-r6d8---source-recovery-policy.
