Add explicit SourceRecoveryPolicy to SourceRuntimeBinding, not SourceContract, because binding owns deployment/runtime recovery shape: runner_pack, checkpoint_family, runtime_shape, material_lifecycle, and transport_semantics. Make the policy typestate-required in SourceRuntimeBindingBuilder, add a builder method, extend SourceMeta/SourceDefinition attrs, export to source catalog/package completeness/source-status/replay views, and make replay planner/executor reject archive-then-scan replay for non-replayable bindings unless an explicit operator override records degraded recovery. Taxonomy: RetainedMaterial, AppendStream, MutableSnapshot, JournalCursor, ExternalProducer, LiveObservation, DerivedInternal.
acceptance_criteria: Every registered SourceRuntimeBinding has explicit SourceRecoveryPolicy. SourceMeta/SourceDefinition can declare it and manual builders must do so. Package completeness and source-status expose replayability_class, catch_up_authority, and accepted_loss_policy. Non-replayable/live bindings either prove zero silent-loss windows or link fix Beads/accepted-loss records. Replay archive/execution refuses non-replayable source replay before archiving live rows unless explicit operator override records degraded/non-replayable recovery. Existing TransportSemantics.replayable is kept consistent or becomes derived/display context. Docs updated for source package template, evidence lanes, package completeness, and replay control.
notes: Research packet integrated 2026-07-03: see .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-2.md#sinex-r6d8-source-replayability-and-loss-authority. Implementation shape: add required SourceRecoveryPolicy per SourceRuntimeBinding; wire through SourceMeta/builder/package completeness/startup validation; reject non-replayable sources before replay archive; keep declared recovery policy separate from observed continuity scorecards.

title: GitHub/CI activity source (API sync): the outcome leg of every work episode
design: Highest leverage-per-effort missing source (S, API-sync shape). Issues/PRs/reviews/CI runs as events — for a development-centric life this is the OUTCOME leg of every work episode; xtask history covers local verification, nothing covers remote. Shape: scheduled API sync (the email provider-sync substrate is the pattern: cursor = updated-since + per-resource ids, rate-limit/backoff via provider runtime state); occurrence identity = (repo, resource kind, resource id, updated_at); ts_orig = the GitHub event timestamp. Start with the operator's own repos; auth via the same secret-deployment boundary as email. Feeds: work.episode, change.episode composites.
acceptance_criteria: Issues/PRs/reviews/CI-run events flowing for the active repos with correct ts_orig; cursor survives restart without duplicates (equivalence keys); rate-limit produces visible provider state, never silent skip; change.episode can join a commit to its PR and CI outcome.

title: work.episode: the unit of resumption (session x attention x git x agent-sessions)
design: RECIPE: session ⊕ attention.stream ⊕ git commits ⊕ agent sessions -> work.episode intervals answering 'what was I doing and did it land'. THE unit the resumption demos score against, and the composite that beads-ingestion enriches with intent labels (bead claim->close intervals). Prereqs: attention.stream (parent composite), git-commit-history flowing in deployment (verify via the static-imports bead), agent-session events (integration.polylogue lane). Episode boundaries = session detector output, refined by attention continuity; outcome leg = commits/PRs in the window.
acceptance_criteria: work.episode events with resolvable parents across >=3 source families; a known real work session reconstructs as one episode with its commits attached; recall can render episodes; the resumption demo scores against these instead of ad-hoc window assembly.

title: project.attribution: honest time-per-project via entity-join
design: RECIPE: command cwd ⋈ git repo paths ⋈ fs paths ⋈ window titles ⋈ browser context -> per-interval project attribution. The project entity is derivable MECHANICALLY (path prefixes + repo roots) — no LLM needed; this is the first real entity-join composite and will surface missing entity kinds (rule 7: a needed third key = a missing entity). Emits project.attribution interval events over attention.stream output. Ambiguous intervals attribute to nothing with an explicit unattributed share — never force a label.
acceptance_criteria: Per-day project attribution queryable; unattributed share reported honestly; attribution of a known worked-on-repo day sanity-checked against git/terminal ground truth; parents resolve.

title: interruption.event: notification x focus-change correlation (cheap side-demo)
design: RECIPE: notification event ⊕ focus-change within Δt -> interruption event carrying the source (which app interrupted) and the attention cost (time to return to prior focus, when measurable). Both inputs LIVE. Δt and the return-detection window are declared constants with provenance notes, not magic numbers. Transducer-with-small-window shape.
acceptance_criteria: interruption events flow on the dev store with real notifications; each carries notification + focus-change parents; a no-focus-change notification produces nothing (control); the derived kind is registered per catalog rule 1 (names consumer: attention analytics + recall annotations).

title: Honest health: replace default-true checks and presence-only verify probes
design: PROBLEM (construct-validity inventory): the system's self-assessment conflates liveness with correctness. Health checks default to true (Level 6 convention — a check that measures nothing); ops verify probes assert presence ('automaton emitted something'), not correctness ('output is right'). Both name constructs they do not measure. DESIGN: (1) health checks must compute their verdict from evidence or report unknown — a default-true path becomes a typed Unknown{reason} state rendered distinctly from Healthy (no more free greens); (2) ops verify gains correctness-class probes alongside presence: for each derived family, a cheap invariant check (e.g. session boundaries non-overlapping, daily summary parent-set spans the day, canonical commands parse back) declared next to the automaton; (3) the health aggregator distinguishes liveness / correctness / unknown per component in its report events, so current_health stops laundering unknown into healthy. RULE this operationalizes (doctrine rule 1): every verdict carries evidence-provenance or is absent.
acceptance_criteria: No health path returns a default-true verdict (rg proves; defaults become Unknown); ops verify reports presence and correctness columns per family with at least one real invariant probe per running automaton; current_health view exposes the three-state distinction; a deliberately-broken automaton output flips correctness while liveness stays green (test).

title: Demo: reconstruct my Tuesday — full-day timeline with provenance to bytes and honest gaps
design: CLAIM: a full-day timeline with provenance to bytes and honest absence. PROTOCOL: interleaved sessions/commands/pages/docs for one real day where every row resolves via show <ref> down to source-material bytes, and capture outages render as EXPLICIT gaps from source-continuity data — displayed, not footnoted. INSTRUMENT: random-sample K rows (K>=10, sampled by script not by hand) and trace each to raw bytes; the gap rendering is the anti-cherry-picking control. FALSIFIER: any sampled row fails byte-trace, or a known outage window renders smooth. PREREQ: recall truthfulness (sinex-vhm) + continuity caveats (landed). Distinct from the campaign (sinex-9j9): the campaign proves a bounded work-window with a baseline arm; this proves breadth (full day, all families) and drilldown depth. CAPABILITY LANDING: the random-sample byte-trace script becomes a standing recall-integrity check.
acceptance_criteria: One full real day rendered; K script-sampled rows each traced to source bytes in the artifact; at least one genuine outage window shown as a gap; one-command regeneration; complete summary.json.

title: Citation-required daily brief: sinexctl report daily with machine-checkable refs
design: DESIGN: sinexctl report daily <date> — a narrative brief over activity.summary.daily + session boundaries + git commits + docs/pages, where EVERY bullet embeds event refs that resolve via show <ref> (the oracle digest pattern, upgraded with machine-checkable citations). Structure: sections per family, each bullet = statement + [ref, ref]; a --verify mode re-resolves every embedded ref and fails the brief on any dangling citation (the construct-validity gate built into the feature). Honest gaps: source-continuity caveats render as explicit absent-source lines. Reuse: the recall/context card + caveat substrate (sinex-vhm) and query units — this is a composition, not a new query silo; if it needs a primitive recall lacks, add the primitive there. LLM-narrated prose is optional and comes later (would be an L3 effect with a ModelEffectRecord); the first version is template-rendered — no model dependency.
acceptance_criteria: report daily produces a brief where every bullet's refs resolve (—verify green); disabling a source renders an explicit gap line; refs sample-traced to source bytes; JSON output stable for MCP; zero raw SQL outside the shared query substrate. Small enough to demo the same week recall truthfulness lands.

title: Projection mechanism unification: migrate documents off the DB trigger (operator-decided)
design: DECISION (operator, 2026-07-03): documents MIGRATE off the trigger path. The standing rule this establishes: consumer-maintained projections (event-engine post-persist hooks or dedicated consumers on the confirmed stream) are THE read-model mechanism for product surfaces; TimescaleDB CAs remain for telemetry rollups only (and move to the reflection table per the telemetry-lane design); DB triggers are retired as a projection mechanism. TARGET: core.fn_document_projection() + trg_document_projection on core.events (crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/documents.rs:292-345) — an AFTER-INSERT trigger folding document.ingested/document.chunked events into core.documents/core.document_chunks. MIGRATION: (1) implement a document-projection consumer on the confirmed stream (same site/pattern as the recall.timeline hook design in the latency bead — build them on one hook mechanism, that is the point); (2) declare it as a DerivationSpec with rebuild + invalidation (replay/redaction), which the trigger version cannot express; (3) backfill/verify parity against the trigger-maintained tables; (4) DROP the trigger + function and remove their schema-apply emission (also shrinks the gh#2182 boot-lock surface — one fewer unconditional CREATE FUNCTION/TRIGGER at boot). WHY the trigger is the odd one out: it is invisible to the derivation registry (no DerivationSpec, no rebuild operation, no invalidation semantics), runs inside the insert transaction (couples ingest latency to projection cost), and is exactly the class of unconditional DDL the boot-race fix has to guard around.
acceptance_criteria: Document projection maintained by a confirmed-stream consumer with DerivationSpec + rebuild op; parity proven against trigger output on the same input set before cutover; trigger + function dropped from defs and from live DBs via convergence; doc search behavior unchanged (existing document tests green); one projection mechanism documented as the standing rule in the schema design docs.

title: Entity-extraction golden set: ~200 hand-labeled events (the lane-evaluation fixture)
design: QUESTION: what is the measured P/R of the current 4-regex entity baseline — and what fixture will every future lane comparison score against? INSTRUMENT: hand-label ~200 events (entities + relations) sampled across sources, LABELED BEFORE any lane runs (pre-registration); score the current extractor against it. ARTIFACT: the committed golden set + the baseline P/R number. PURPOSE: the prerequisite instrument for the semantic-lane duel demo and all LLM-lane work — without it the A/B harness has nothing valid to measure against. Also becomes the standing monthly drift-check epoch fixture (U9 class 4).
acceptance_criteria: Golden set committed with labeling protocol documented; git history proves labels precede lane runs; 4-regex baseline P/R reported; lane-duel demo unblocked.
notes: 2026-07-03 subagent packet integrated: full sampling protocol, label schema, fixture paths, and baseline scorer shape live at .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-subagent-beads-packets.md#sinex-a4w1---entity-golden-set. The issue is executable now: export deterministic stratified corpus from core.events, sanitize to input_text, commit golden_v1 before lane runs, add scorer, then record baseline_v1.json and P/R.

title: Analysis: checkpoint-loss-window matrix (the data-loss close-out instrument)
design: Executable issue packet written: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-1-checkpoint-loss-window.md. Close by driving five non-zero rows to zero/accepted: source cursor durable handoff (sinex-r6d.4), recovery spool no-discard quarantine (sinex-r6d.5), confirmed-event retention-gap catch-up or non-discarding retention (sinex-r6d.6), invalidation no ack-before-recompute/checkpoint or remove legacy scan path (sinex-r6d.7), and source replayability/loss authority policy (sinex-r6d.8). Verification carrier: crash-window harness sinex-r6d.9 plus packet-listed xtask commands.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix covers all sources + all 14 automata + the transport spool; every non-zero row links a fix bead or an explicit acceptance; the epic's close cites this artifact.
notes: 2026-07-03 subagent packet integrated: full matrix design and code-reference map lives at .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-subagent-beads-packets.md#sinex-r6d1---checkpoint-loss-window-matrix. Executable follow-ups are being created under sinex-r6d for every likely non-zero loss row: source durable-before-checkpoint, spool overflow, confirmed-event catch-up, invalidation ack ordering, replayability classification, and crash-window harness.

title: Demo: hybrid retrieval hit@k — justify or kill pgvector with one number
description: Wave 3 — cheap once the worker exists; second of the LLM-plane pair.
design: CLAIM: embeddings add measurable recall over FTS on real personal history. PROTOCOL: PRE-REGISTER 30 'find the moment where I ...' queries with known target events from the operator's own history (pre-registration = the validity discipline; targets fixed before any retrieval runs); report hit@5 for FTS-only vs hybrid on the same store. INSTRUMENT: hit@k table, both arms. FALSIFIER: built into the design — hybrid <= FTS kills the pgvector investment honestly, and that outcome is as publishable internally as a win. PREREQ: sinex-5v6. CAPABILITY LANDING: the pre-registered query set is committed as a reusable retrieval-regression fixture; the two-arm runner becomes a docs-search eval command.
acceptance_criteria: 30 pre-registered queries committed BEFORE retrieval runs (git history proves ordering); hit@5 both arms reported; verdict stated either direction; one-command rerun; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: run-twice-costs-once (deterministic LLM effect ledger)
description: Wave 3 — the demo that separates Sinex from every pipe-your-life-into-GPT project. First of the LLM-plane pair.
design: CLAIM: LLM derivation is replayable provenance, not an oracle call. PROTOCOL: run an LLM lane (embedding worker) over a corpus; rerun the identical lane -> 100% effect-cache hits, $0 marginal cost, byte-identical derived outputs; bump the prompt/model version -> ONLY affected effects recompute and the budget report shows the exact delta. INSTRUMENT: ModelEffectRecord ledger + llm budget-report (non-zero for the first time). FALSIFIER: cache misses on the identical rerun, or nondeterministic outputs (same key, different output = the U9 class-3 alarm). PREREQ: sinex-5v6 (embedding worker). CAPABILITY LANDING: the rerun-vs-delta comparison becomes an llm effects diff command.
acceptance_criteria: Ledger shows 100% hits on rerun; version bump recomputes exactly the affected subset with the delta in budget-report; outputs byte-identical on hit; one-command rerun; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: retroactive bug-fix over months of history (replay as the product)
description: Wave 2 — the deepest single differentiator; no comparable system can show this. Doubles as acceptance evidence for the replay crash-window fix.
design: CLAIM: interpretations are disposable, evidence is permanent. PROTOCOL: fix a REAL parser defect (live candidates: journald salience, browser title parsing), replay that source across months of material through the normal pipeline. INSTRUMENT: machine-checked invariants, all asserted by script: ts_orig stable across replay; new id + new ts_coided on every replaced interpretation; archive count == replaced count; zero orphaned derived events; occurrence identity (source_material_id, anchor_byte) unchanged. FALSIFIER: any invariant fails — which is exactly what the open replay-crash bug predicts, so this demo IS that fix's acceptance test. PREREQ: sinex-afd closed (hard block). ENHANCEMENT once as-of lands (sinex-a11): show recall over the same window as-of pre-fix vs post-fix — the belief diff as the presentation layer. CAPABILITY LANDING: the invariant-checker script becomes a replay verification command (ops replay verify), not a demo-local assertion pile.
acceptance_criteria: Real defect fixed (not synthetic); replay across >=3 months of material; all five invariants asserted green by a committed script; before/after interpretation shown for at least one concrete record; one-command rerun against the dev store; complete summary.json.

title: Recall salience scoring: every term measured, breakdown in the DTO
description: Recall (sinex-vhm) needs ranking beyond family round-robin, and doctrine forbids unexplained numbers. This implements the honest scoring design from handoff U4. Sequence after the truthfulness slice (sinex-vhm) so scores rank real derived-plane rows, not the degenerate heuristic.
design: DESIGN (settled, from handoff U4): score(card) = w_r*recency + w_x*rarity + w_e*effort + w_a*anchor, with family-diversity as a re-rank pass (not a score term). Every term is a measured quantity: recency = exp-decay on ts_orig with half-life = the MEASURED session-gap median (not a magic number); rarity = -log(frequency of the canonical form in a trailing 30d window) computable from command_frequency_hourly CA + per-family equivalents; effort = dwell/duration evidence (session length share, fs edit churn); anchor = boundary bonus (session start/end, first-seen entity). Weights: fixed documented constants initially; the DTO carries per-term contributions (salience: {recency: .41, rarity: .32, ...}) so the number has provenance — doctrine rule 1. Calibration loop later via the predictions domain (operator marks rows that did/didn't matter).
acceptance_criteria: Recall rows carry a salience breakdown whose terms are each traceable to a measured quantity; removing a term's evidence (e.g. no session data) drops the term, never fabricates it; ranking is stable and explainable in the JSON output; a doc comment names each constant and its provenance. VERIFY: unit tests on the scorer with synthetic distributions; live recall output inspection.

title: Devloop and meta plane
description: The devloop-and-meta plane: the loop that builds Sinex must itself be honest, fast, and observable. Owns: task-substrate integrity (beads as the shared source of truth), verification trustworthiness (impact planner correctness — a false skip is a lie the loop tells itself), dev-infra friction (anything adding serial seconds to every iteration), and loop observability (the evidence a future agent needs to resume cold).
design: LIMBS by function — VERIFICATION TRUST: 1p2.2 (impact planner staged-hunk blindness + evidence staleness — fix before relying on narrowed test runs), 1p2.1 (dead-lane reachability lint). FRICTION: guw (schema-bootstrap rebuild tax on every launch), 1ul (5432 collision blocks concurrent lanes), jvs (2.5min/7GiB single-test compile tax). OBSERVABILITY: 8zy (JetStream state into xtask analytics store — the SQL-blind-spot lesson), pke (connect disconnected verification machinery). SUBSTRATE: hlv (beads+devloop as sources; ownership boundaries settled), 06d (schema-bundle drift in pre-push). INTEGRATION HYGIENE: keep the reconcile lesson — the ahead-train cost compounds per devloop pass; PR flow is restored (uzl closed), keep it. SEQUENCING: 1p2.2 and guw first — they tax or corrupt every single loop iteration; everything else is per-occasion.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: impact planner produces no false skips on the known vectors (staged changes, stale evidence) with regression tests; a second concurrent checkout/agent lane works without infra collision; a cold agent can reconstruct loop state from bd + committed artifacts alone; JetStream reality visible in xtask analytics.

title: Capture breadth and quality
description: Finish and deploy the capture surface: dark finished sources into deployment, attention-source data quality, the media and email runtime tiers, and the new predictions domain. Cheap breadth first (static-import glue), quality where it blocks cross-source value (attention sources).
design: DOCTRINE: breadth via the generic lane, quality gated before joining composites, portfolio pruned by decision not drift. LIMBS: stm (the scheduled static-import glue — cheapest breadth, unlocks the 21 dark contracts); 2bk (trust the deployment path first); 60r (quality gate: attention sources currently ingest garbage — blocks attention.stream); new sources rkv.1 (GitHub/CI — outcome leg), rkv.2 (nix generations), rkv.3 (browser content — opens topics), rkv.4 (calendar — the only planned-time capture), rkv.5 (mobile export bridges), rkv.6 (physiological — RAW provider data only), rkv.7 (satellite hosts — capture topology expansion); di7 (email runtime — opens people) and xv1 (media/OCR) are sibling epics feeding the same role table. GOVERNANCE: 5j8 (capture-domain portfolio decision) prunes; u3n (stall alerting) keeps deployed sources honest; the derivation catalog role table is the coverage scoreboard — a new source must name the derivation it feeds (extension rule 1).
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: the catalog role table has no unintentionally-zero row (Physiological and Communication filled or explicitly deferred by 5j8); every deployed source emits within its declared cadence or alerts (u3n); every new source cites its consumer derivation; no source is built on preprocessed intermediates when raw provider data exists.
notes: Opportunistic new-source tail (not beads until wanted): music-live (Spotify API), RSS/reading queue, bank feeds (via hledger import discipline rather than a direct bank source). Ranked below the four committed new sources (github, nix-generations, browser-content, calendar) + mobile bridges.

title: Verify finite-drain source-material closure on the live dev runtime
description: The finite-drain fix landed locally (cd1ac7ca7 "fix(runtime): finalize finite adapter materials" + c19052c6c) but its focused verification was killed twice by earlyoom (xtask jobs 2001255/2001256, no Rust diagnostic ever observed). Prove it on the live dev runtime: a static/directory finite drain (e.g. git source) finalizes its source material — material leaves `sensing` (the observed stuck case was 019f2545-2eb5-7d23-ad1a-8b393f78520c) — across snapshot, historical, and continuous paths. Retry the focused test only with sane host memory headroom (low-memory xtask form: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1).
acceptance_criteria: On the live dev runtime with the fixed binary (cd1ac7ca7 + c19052c6c): a finite drain (git source or a fixture static/directory adapter) finalizes its source material — registry state leaves 'sensing' and reaches its terminal state across all three scan modes (snapshot, historical, continuous); the previously-stuck material 019f2545-2eb5-7d23-ad1a-8b393f78520c either finalized by the orphan-recovery path or explicitly remediated; the focused Rust test that earlyoom killed twice (jobs 2001255/2001256) actually completes and passes under CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 with host memory headroom checked first. VERIFY: registry SQL by material state + the focused xtask test run to completion.

title: Ingest beads and devloop as Sinex sources (design settled: bd keeps authority, Sinex observes)
description:
Rawlog 2026-07-03 05:29-05:34 raises three related integration questions:
1. Devloop-as-source: ingest .agent/conductor-devloop/EVENTS.jsonl + OPERATING-LOG.md as a Sinex source (StaticFileAdapter/append-stream) so loop phases/focus changes/proofs become queryable events correlated with git/terminal/browser activity. Already a backlog queue item; now includes beads.
2. Beads-as-source: ingest .beads (Dolt commits or issues.jsonl export + events.jsonl audit trail) so task lifecycle (created/claimed/blocked/closed) becomes first-class events — work-item state joins the same timeline as the work itself. bd has events-export config for exactly this shape.
3. Conceptual mapping vs Polylogue 'assertions': assertions = general typed claims over evidence with provenance; beads = a narrow, opinionated work-item domain (lifecycle+deps+ready-computation) with its own store. Working hypothesis: beads is a specialized assertion class (task-shaped claims) whose authority stays in bd; Sinex ingests it as evidence, does NOT own it; Polylogue assertions reference bead ids rather than duplicating task state. Deliverable: a short decision doc settling ownership boundaries + the two source contracts.

Refs: rawlog tail 2026-07-03.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03) — answers the rawlog questions: devloop-in-sinex? beads<->sinex? beads vs assertions?

OWNERSHIP BOUNDARIES (the decision):
- bd keeps task AUTHORITY. Its Dolt store is the source of truth for task state; the ready/blocked/dependency computation is its value. Sinex NEVER writes task state and does not mirror beads into authoritative tables.
- Sinex ingests beads as EVIDENCE (material provenance), like any other source.
- Polylogue assertions REFERENCE bead ids (ref grammar bd:<issue-id>) instead of duplicating task state. Conceptually beads is a specialized assertion class (task-shaped claims with a lifecycle algebra); practically it must not be absorbed — no second task tracker anywhere.
- Devloops are NOT expressed/actuated in sinex. The loop's own files + beads stay authoritative; sinex observes them. (Rawlog "devloops themselves expressed in sinex" — answered: observation yes, actuation no.)

SOURCE CONTRACT 1 — project.beads:
- Enable bd events-export (.beads config events-export: true) -> append-only .beads/events.jsonl audit trail; that file is the source material (append-stream adapter, per-line anchors).
- Event types: task.created / task.claimed / task.blocked / task.closed / task.commented mapped from bd event categories; payload carries issue id, title, priority, labels, actor.
- Occurrence identity = (events.jsonl material, line byte offset); ts_orig = the bd event timestamp; ts_coided = ingestion now (correct: interpretation time).

SOURCE CONTRACT 2 — project.devloop:
- Source material: .agent/conductor-devloop/EVENTS.jsonl (already structured: phase/focus/timestamps, body_sha256, regenerated from OPERATING-LOG). StaticFileAdapter with re-scan on change; occurrence identity = (event body_sha256) via equivalence key so regeneration does not duplicate.
- Event types: devloop.phase, devloop.focus_change, devloop.checkpoint, devloop.demo.

THE PAYOFF COMPOSITE (follow-up derivation once both lanes flow): work-episode attribution joining bead claim->close intervals with git commits + terminal sessions + editor/fs activity in the same window. Beads supplies the INTENT labels the attribution problem was missing: intent (bead) x activity (terminal/git/fs) x outcome (close reason). File as a discovered-from bead when the lanes land.

DO NOT BUILD: sinex->bd writeback; bd state mirrored as sinex-authoritative tables; a polylogue task tracker; devloop actuation through sinex instructions.
acceptance_criteria: events-export enabled and .beads/events.jsonl flowing; both source contracts registered + deployed dev-locally; bd lifecycle events queryable (sinexctl query events where source = 'project.beads'); devloop phase events queryable; regenerating EVENTS.jsonl does not duplicate (equivalence-key test); a work-episode attribution bead filed as discovered-from this one when both lanes prove out. VERIFY: focused parser/adapter tests + live dev-store query.
notes:
Demo packaging (folded from the portfolio): devloop-as-source self-hosting closure — via recall ask 'what did the devloop decide about DLQ pressure on June 30 and what proof exists?' with answers cross-linked to git commit events; checkable word-for-word against the tracked files; falsifier = wrong or unresolvable citations.

2026-07-03 operator re-confirmation: beads integration explicitly named in the integration directive (item 3, 'possibly already encoded' — it is, here). Priority raised p3->p2. Ref-namespace convention (bd:<issue-id>) now also referenced by the Polylogue integration epic sinex-4j2 (ref-namespaces.md is the shared spec).

title: External legibility artifacts: concepts page, positioning, comparables
description: Make the project concept transmit. Diagnosis (settled, fables-sinex.md ~1147+): four failure modes — (a) category capture by wrong neighbors (elevator line pattern-matches to Rewind/MS-Recall and the listener stops updating; the real category 'personal data infrastructure where interpretations are versioned computations' has no name yet, so frame deliberately or lose by default); (b) the novelty is architectural and architecture doesn't screenshot — it only shows in temporal demonstration (fix a parser, watch months re-understand themselves), so static description structurally undersells; (c) private vocabulary with public equivalents nobody is handed (ts_coided, anchor_byte, automata... each maps to established theory — the translation table IS most of the fix); (d) claims outrun public evidence — legibility and the construct-validity demo arc are the same project. Deliverables and placement are fixed in the design field; content sources: fables-sinex.md lines ~1147-1470 (ladder, table, comparables, claims, credibility map) and ~1900-1990 (ecosystem analysis). Do the concepts page + positioning now that recall-v2 exists to point at; evidence-gallery publication stays gated on scrubbing (sinex-83g).
design: WHAT IS SAID WHERE (settled): (1) CONCEPTS PAGE fronting the README — the distillation ladder verbatim from the source: L0 hook ('AI models get smarter every quarter, but your data only ever gets interpreted once — Sinex makes every interpretation a rebuildable artifact, so each better model retroactively re-understands your history'); L1 mechanism paragraph (capture->anchored events->derived with lineage->replay->adjudication->agent as primary consumer); L2 the Nix frame ('Nix for personal data': captures=sources, versioned interpreters=builders, derived events=store paths, replay=rebuild, effect ledger=binary cache — plus what build systems lack: history of understanding + human gate) — structurally exact, native to the audiences most likely to care; L3 theory ('event sourcing plus four additions': material anchoring, bitemporal interpretation identity, non-idempotent replay as product, typed adjudication gate). Plus the 10-row vocabulary translation table (material/derived->W3C PROV+Datalog base-vs-derived; ts_orig/ts_coided->bitemporal valid-vs-transaction time; replay->rebuild-after-compiler-upgrade; archive cascade->downstream invalidation; automata->dataflow operators; equivalence key->occurrence-level idempotency key; effect ledger->memoization/binary cache; shadow lanes->blue-green for interpreters; Proposal/Judgment->HITL promotion gate; anchor_byte->content-addressed evidence w/ offsets). Rule: keep internal vocabulary, pair translation on first use; doc-alias ts_coided as 'interpretation time (transaction time)'. (2) docs/positioning.md — honest comparables by neighborhood: capture layer (Rewind/Recall/screenpipe/AW/timelinize/Dogsheep = ingestible sources, capture is being commoditized at OS level and that LOWERS Sinex's cost of breadth); personal-data infra (HPI = closest philosophical relative minus the persistence/provenance spine; Perkeep = L0-shaped, died on the consumption gap — cite AND heed); agent memory (Letta/mem0/LangMem = assertions without ground truth; Zep's Graphiti = independent convergence on the two-clock design — validates L3/L4, study before building the entity lane); pattern donors (Datomic/XTDB as-of, Materialize IVM deliberately rejected for auditable coarse recomputation, Salsa/BSàC replay economics, OTel semantic conventions + the 'personal observability — OTel for a human life' one-liner). Positioning sentence: the field has a capture layer and an agent-memory layer and nothing trustworthy between them; Sinex is the missing middle — ingest the capture ecosystem, serve the agent ecosystem via MCP, compete with neither. Their memories are assertions; ours are conclusions with lineage to bytes. (3) FIVE FALSIFIABLE CLAIMS in positioning.md, each with its check command/demo: XOR provenance enforced 3x (try to violate); replay non-idempotent w/ archive (cem.3); no silent truth (authority types); LLM-as-build-step (cem.4 — state honestly 'contract shipped, client pending' — the built-vs-intended distinction is itself credibility); failures render as gaps (cem.2). (4) CREDIBILITY MAP: pitch-line -> evidence-artifact -> status table maintained alongside the demo portfolio. (5) Cheapest external move: publish concepts page + positioning + 3 scrubbed shelf entries (cem.1, cem.2, one recall-v2) before any new feature. TRANSMISSION TEST = acceptance: an outsider who read only the concepts page correctly predicts behavior on three probes (re-import same file? parser fixed — old conclusions? can LLM write truth?).
acceptance_criteria: A stranger given only the concepts page can state what the system is and what makes it different; positioning claims are each falsifiable; no personal capture data reaches the public surface.
notes: Also fold in: the central-bet risk register (handoff U11) into .github/target-vision-claim-ledger.md as part of this pass. Agent-orientation content (sinex-7z0.3) is GENERATED from the same ladder source — internal and external explanations must not fork. Graphiti study-before-entity-lane pointer also recorded on sinex-2nd.

title: P15: dead-lane deletion sweep (pre-release: delete, don't deprecate)
description:
Verified dead lanes to delete outright (pre-release, no users): batch_importer.rs; pressure.rs (+the false claim in resource-scoping.md:83); runtime/material/ (3 unused exports + counter stub); occurrence_filter in both crates (tests-only, no production callers); IntegrityRepository; ~5/8 unwired PkmService methods; empty metrics schema (registry.rs:45-49). Also fix-or-delete: entity_enricher UTC histogram (tz bug), extractor first-match-only (should emit all matches), label sinexctl llm CLI as inert until an emitter exists (or land P8 first).

Cross-check each against the confirmed-delivery branch before deleting (some surfaces move there). Refs: handoff P15; audit 015 §D11.
design: Keep-list nuance: PkmService::create_entities and link_entities are NOT dead — lane promote (sinex-2nd) needs them; delete only the ~5 truly unwired methods. Cross-check every deletion against feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery before removing (some surfaces move there). Also fix the false claim at nixos/modules/resource-scoping.md:83 when deleting pressure.rs.
acceptance_criteria: rg proves zero non-test references before each deletion; xtask check --full clean afterwards; entity_extractor emits all (bounded) matches with a test; entity_enricher histogram is timezone-correct or deleted.
notes:
Also from the same lens: consider a dead-lane detector / reachability lint (tooling that flags typed surfaces with zero production callers) so this sweep does not need to be rediscovered by hand next time.
Also from the validity inventory: entity confidence 0.9 for email->person is a heuristic labeled as measurement — same fix class as the recall confidences: evidence-derived (once the golden set exists) or absent.
2026-07-03 subagent packet integrated: full deletion ordering and branch-conflict notes live at .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-subagent-beads-packets.md#sinex-y7l---dead-lane-deletion-sweep. Research sharpened the sweep into pure-deletion batches plus separate behavior Beads for entity-enricher timezone, multi-entity extraction, and inert semantic-llm surfaces.
2026-07-03 codebase-audit addition: AnchorComputer / compute_anchor (crate/sinexd/src/runtime/ingestion_helpers.rs:229-236) is dead code with a MISLEADING stub — compute_anchor ignores its _offset param and always returns record_boundary, reading as if intra-record anchoring is coarse. Real anchoring is correct and unaffected (adapter_source.rs uses anchor.offset_start via from_material). Delete AnchorComputer + validate_anchor + the anchor_rule_id/version fields if they are equally unused; verify no serialized state depends on them first. This is delete-not-fix: the live path already does the right thing.

title: Deploy the dark sources: generic scheduled static-import glue
description:
21 of ~48 real source contracts are complete but have zero NixOS deployment wiring (email = 77KB contract + 3 adapters incl IMAP IDLE; no emailGlue/financeGlue/socialGlue exists in sources.nix). Progress: services.sinex.sources.staticImports with git-commit-history + raindrop defaults landed on local master (92fe16cea) — the pattern to generalize.

Do: generalize the scheduled static-import glue (mkDocumentUnits-timer pattern) so each remaining StaticFileAdapter/DirectoryWalk source is one attribute; then per-family glue where secrets are needed (email needs agenix wiring — see gh#1469 remaining). Cheapest breadth in the codebase.

Refs: handoff P12; audit 015 §C9.
design: DECISION: (1) ONE parameterized scheduled-static-import glue function (template: mkDocumentUnits timer, nixos/modules/sources.nix:1540,1859) covering StaticFileAdapter/DirectoryWalk sources; (2) git dir-target already addressed via staticImports on local master (92fe16cea) — verify, then extend; (3) wire remaining batches: social/music/health/finance after git+raindrop verification (sinex-2bk); email is separate (mkEmailGlue + agenix OAuth plumbing — see the email epic). Also consolidate the SQLite-WAL read-write invariant into one shared helper (3 drift-prone sites: sources.nix:1149-1157, :760-849, xtask infra manifest — commit 371caaa7d was drift-fix #3).
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl sources shows the new sources with materials + events on the live host after the timer fires; catalog status flips to deployed; the WAL invariant has exactly one definition site (rg proves). VERIFY: xtask test vm --category smoke + live switch; per-source event counts.
notes: 2026-07-03 codebase-audit correction: the '21 of ~48 real source contracts' figure in the description does not match the current tree — register_source_contract! = 35 and register_source_runtime_binding! = 24 (measured 2026-07-03), i.e. ~11 contracts without a runtime binding, not 21-of-48. The '48' appears to overcount (possibly counting payload/adapter variants or planned contracts). Re-derive the real dark-source set as the FIRST step of this bead: enumerate register_source_contract! minus register_source_runtime_binding! minus already-deployed NixOS glue attributes, and treat THAT list as the deployment backlog. The nbi.1 census will reconcile the vocabulary-vs-inventory numbers.

title: Predictions and calibration domain: forecasts as first-class events
description:
New capture domain derived from the operator rawlog: predictions/forecasts with later resolution and calibration scoring. Payload spec drafted in handoff U7 (prediction.stated / prediction.resolved, probability, horizon, resolution criteria, Brier scoring as derived events). Natural devloop dogfood: agents predict slice outcomes/durations; calibration report becomes both a product demo and an agent-quality instrument. Manual intake path exists as precedent (sinexctl record — health intake/effect records, record.rs:41-43,123-185).

Refs: handoff P11 + U7.
design: DECISION: prediction.registered (statement, probability, resolver-criteria, due, predictor-identity) / prediction.resolved (outcome, resolver evidence refs) events; reducer to current-prediction state; sinexctl record prediction + predictions report (Brier score + calibration buckets per predictor). Third instance of the event-sourced reducer pattern — template: sinex-primitives/src/task_domain.rs:271 reduce_task_event, handler api/handlers/tasks.rs:39-91. Payload spec drafted in handoff 02-uncovered-designs.md U7. First deployment: agents predicting devloop outcomes — pre-registration gives inherent construct validity.
acceptance_criteria: register -> resolve -> report roundtrip works; calibration report correct on a synthetic set with known Brier score; MCP-exposed so agents can self-register predictions.

title: Semantic lane promote: close the shadow -> curation -> canonical loop
description:
The authority type system (Proposal/Judgment) is wired for exactly one kind (curation duplicates) and no lane->canonical promotion path exists; curation finalize currently finalizes nothing (emits an event no one consumes, curation.rs:391-470). Implement `semantic lane promote`: walk an approved lane diff into PkmService::create_entities/link_entities. This closes the loop the whole L4 adjudication design was built for.

Refs: handoff P10; audit 015 §B8.
design: DECISION: sinexctl semantic lane promote <lane> [--diff <id>] walks an APPROVED diff into PkmService::create_entities/link_entities, recording a curation.finalized-style derived event with proposal+judgment parents as provenance. Promotion REQUIRES judgment (authority gate) — never auto-promote. TARGETS: sinex-db/src/repositories/semantic.rs:467-573 (lane seeding), sinex-primitives/src/semantic.rs:242 (diff_entity_relation_lanes), api/handlers/curation.rs:391-470 (finalize that finalizes nothing), api/handlers/pkm.rs:34-88 (the only canonical writer today). CAUTION: this is the FIRST canonical-truth writer beyond manual PKM — the adjudication doctrine (Proposal/Judgment is the only path to truth) is settled; implement inside it.
acceptance_criteria: Promoted entities/relations appear in core.entities/entity_relations with provenance to proposal+judgment; re-promoting is idempotent at the occurrence level; unjudged diffs refuse with a clear error.
notes: 2026-07-03 ecosystem pointer (from the external-landscape analysis): study Zep's Graphiti before building/promoting the entity lane — it independently converged on the two-clock design (event time vs ingestion time) and incremental entity resolution; validates the L3/L4 architecture and is the closest prior art for promotion mechanics. Differentiator to preserve: their memories are assertions; Sinex conclusions carry lineage to bytes.

title: Query units for the derived plane (sessions, entities, summaries, tags)
description:
Make derived events addressable: the query-units language has 1/6 units server-lowered; sessions/entities/summaries/tags have no unit. Plumbing per unit is small (unit enum + row mapper, query_units.rs:67-75). Exposes the derived plane through sinexctl query AND MCP sinex.query in one move — every automaton output becomes agent-addressable.

Refs: handoff P9; audit 015 §B7.
design: DECISION: add units sessions, summaries, entities, tags following the existing unit-enum + row-mapper pattern (sinexctl/src/commands/query_units.rs:67-75,109-117,397). Server-side lowering where the events AST path allows — all four are event-backed, so reuse event_query_from_sinex_query with type/source constraints.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl query 'sessions where ts_orig > ...' works via CLI AND MCP sinex.query; new units listed in descriptors. VERIFY: query_units tests + MCP smoke.

title: Small correctness sweep: seven verified small bugs
description:
Batch of small verified findings (audit 015 §A4), each independently fixable:
1. Replay preview depth 64 vs execution 100 (state_machine.rs:716 vs constants.rs:175) — preview can undercount the cascade.
2. Replacement-lineage mismatch is warn-only (replay_writer.rs:193-203) vs documented DLQ promise.
3. stage_as_you_go orphan sweep only covers same-process memory (stage_as_you_go.rs:366-421).
4. Recovery-spool 1000-line permanent-loss cap (event_transport.rs:375).
5. Acquisition publish-before-mirror hash window (acquisition_manager.rs:695-720).
6. Coordination handoff monitor never resubscribes (coordination.rs:595-619).
7. Fail-open equivalence dedup has no debt counter (admission.rs:410) — DB error silently disables dedup with no operator-visible debt.

One PR or a couple of themed PRs; each fix cites its file:line and adds a focused test where behavior-bearing.
design: All seven have settled fix shapes: unify preview/execution depth to one constant (state_machine.rs:716 vs constants.rs:175); promote replacement-lineage mismatch from warn (replay_writer.rs:193-203) to a recorded operation outcome surfaced in ops replay output (docs at sinex-primitives/src/events/mod.rs:91 promise DLQ); recovery-spool overflow (event_transport.rs:375,391-403) -> compacted on-disk archive + operator debt, not silent discard; acquisition: mirror locally before/atomically-with NATS publish (acquisition_manager.rs:695-720); coordination handoff monitor resubscribes after subscribe failure (coordination.rs:595-619); stage_as_you_go orphan sweep reconciles from registry rows, not process memory (stage_as_you_go.rs:366-421,696-762); admission equivalence-key fail-open gets a debt counter (admission.rs:511-518).
acceptance_criteria: Each fix has a focused test where behavior-bearing (depth parity on a deep synthetic cascade; induced lineage mismatch visible in operation record; spool overflow recoverable; orphan sweep finds a cross-process orphan; debt counter increments on simulated DB error). xtask check --full clean.

title: Dev postgres port 5432 collision: socket-only, fail-fast, stale pidfile cleanup
description:
Every checkout's dev postgres binds TCP 5432; a second checkout's postgres can never start and xtask's SQLx schema-apply bootstrap hangs forever ("Applying checkout-local schema..." with no cargo spawn). Connections already use the per-checkout unix socket, so TCP is unnecessary.

Fix: listen_addresses='' (socket-only, cleanest) or per-checkout derived port; bootstrap should fail fast with a clear port-conflict error instead of hanging; clean stale postmaster.pid from killed postmasters. Blocks concurrent agent/worktree lanes.
design: DECISION: socket-only (listen_addresses='') — connections already use the per-checkout unix socket, TCP serves nothing; per-checkout derived ports rejected (still a shared namespace, still collides eventually). Three changes in the xtask infra postgres lifecycle: (1) render listen_addresses='' into the generated postgresql.conf; (2) bootstrap preflight: if pg_ctl start fails, surface the actual postmaster log tail and exit nonzero — never hang on 'Applying checkout-local schema...' (the hang is the bug as much as the collision); (3) stale postmaster.pid: on start, if the pidfile's pid is dead or owns a different data-dir (proc cmdline check, same ownership-proof pattern as infra stop --all-checkouts), remove it and proceed. PITFALL: anything that reads DATABASE_URL with host=TCP assumptions (none known — verify with rg 'localhost:5432|127.0.0.1:5432' across xtask + devshell hooks) must be clean before removing TCP.
acceptance_criteria: Two checkouts run xtask infra start concurrently and both come up (the original repro); netstat shows no 5432 listener from dev postgres; a killed postmaster leaves a pidfile that the next start cleans automatically; a genuinely-failing start exits fast with the postgres log tail in the error. VERIFY: xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(infra)' + the two-checkout manual proof recorded.
notes: 2026-07-05: PR #2375 opened from feature/xtask/postgres-bootstrap-robustness. Implemented devshell cargo+xtask wrapper stale postmaster.pid cleanup and fail-fast pg_ctl startup log tails in flake.nix. Verification: nix eval .#devShells.x86_64-linux.default.drvPath --raw; nix develop --command bash -lc 'printf PGHOST/PGPORT/DATABASE_URL; xtask infra status --json'; xtask test -p xtask --lib -E "test(postmaster_pid_state) | test(force_cleanup) | test(ensure_runtime_config) | test(pg_ctl_start_command)" invocation 2005745 passed 5/5; xtask check --changed-strict origin/master passed/no Rust packages affected; sudo ss showed checkout Postgres pid 76165 owns only /var/cache/sinex/sinity/566e9bf8d5e8/dev-state/run/.s.PGSQL.5432 while TCP 5432 belongs to system Postgres pid 440105. Remaining before close: rerun two-checkout concurrent infra-start proof when host memory PSI is sane.
close_reason: Completed: PR #2375 landed socket-only dev Postgres and robust bootstrap cleanup. Two-checkout proof on 2026-07-05 used main checkout /realm/project/sinex (dev-state 566e9bf8d5e8) and detached proof worktree /realm/tmp/worktrees/sinex-2206-proof (dev-state a853b8a0179a). Both xtask infra status commands succeeded; proof worktree xtask infra start completed with Postgres running and NATS on port 4290 while main Postgres remained running. sudo ss showed TCP 5432 owned only by system Postgres pid 440105, with Sinex checkouts listening only on Unix sockets /var/cache/sinex/sinity/566e9bf8d5e8/dev-state/run/.s.PGSQL.5432 and /var/cache/sinex/sinity/a853b8a0179a/dev-state/run/.s.PGSQL.5432. Proof worktree infra was stopped after capture.

title: Schema-apply convergence gaps: dup CHECKs, required-index set, CAGG constant, COPY boot verify
description:
Five silent convergence gaps in sinex-schema apply.rs that neither diff nor strict_diff detects:
1. Duplicate CHECK constraints accumulate (named NOT VALID added, auto-named inline twins never dropped; prod had ~46 vs ~24 needed — every INSERT evaluates all).
2. ix_events_equivalence_key (load-bearing for admission dedup) + ix_events_sinex_telemetry not in EVENTS_REQUIRED_INDEXES — manual drop is invisible and dedup falls to seq-scan on the hypertable (real perf cliff).
3. current_system_state CAGG missing from TELEMETRY_CONTINUOUS_AGGREGATES existence check.
4. events_check7 (ts_orig future-skew) absent from DECLARED_INLINE_CHECKS.
5. verify_event_copy_contract() is lazy (first >=50 COPY batch panic) — verify at startup alongside schema apply instead.

Full findings: .agent/scratch/research-audit-db-schema.md.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the five gaps closed with its own proof: (1) apply converges duplicate CHECKs (dedup logic + test: seeded twin inline/named CHECK gets dropped, prod-shape fixture goes 46->24); (2) ix_events_equivalence_key + ix_events_sinex_telemetry in EVENTS_REQUIRED_INDEXES (drop-then-apply restores them; test); (3) current_system_state in TELEMETRY_CONTINUOUS_AGGREGATES existence check; (4) events_check7 in DECLARED_INLINE_CHECKS (strict-diff detects manual drop); (5) verify_event_copy_contract() invoked at startup schema-apply path (boot fails loud on drift, not first-COPY-batch panic; lazy OnceLock path removed). VERIFY: xtask test -p sinex-schema; xtask schema strict-diff against dev store clean; the research doc's findings table updated to all-closed.

title: CAS digest charset validation: path traversal + unbounded read from ReadOnly token
description:
ContentKey::validate (sinex-primitives/src/domain.rs:1998) only checks the '--' separator; digest flows into Path::join (content_store/mod.rs:582) so '..' or leading '/' escapes the CAS root; read at manager.rs:438 has no size cap. Reachable from content.retrieve_blob at ReadOnly role: arbitrary-file read into daemon memory (OOM vector) + fs-existence oracle. Mitigated by single-user/loopback/mTLS but a real sandbox escape; fix is cheap.

Fix: constrain digest to BLAKE3 hex fixed length in validate(); canonicalize resolved path and assert within CAS root; size-cap the read. Notes+repro: .agent/scratch/research-audit-api-security.md.
acceptance_criteria: ContentKey::validate rejects any digest that is not exact-length lowercase BLAKE3 hex (unit tests: traversal payloads ../, leading /, overlong, uppercase, empty); resolved blob path canonicalized and asserted within the CAS root with a test proving escape attempts error; blob read size-capped (cap from config, default sane) with an over-cap test; content.retrieve_blob at ReadOnly exercises all three in an integration test. VERIFY: xtask test -p sinex-primitives -E 'test(content_key)'; xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(content_store)'; repro from .agent/scratch/research-audit-api-security.md no longer reproduces.
close_reason: Implemented local CAS digest validation and canonical containment. Evidence: xtask test -p sinex-primitives -E 'test(content_key_validation_and_parsing)' job 2001758 passed; xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(content_key_parser)' job 2001761 passed; CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd --test content_service_test -E 'test(content_retrieve_rejects_local_cas_symlink_escape) | test(content_retrieve_rejects_file_that_exceeds_retrieval_limit)' job 2001763 passed; xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinex-db -p sinexd job 2001764 passed; xtask check --changed-strict origin/master job 2001767 passed.

title: Historical-import pacing: operator-configurable rate + progress visibility
description:
The prod flap was triggered by an unthrottled 14-month journald import (1.24M RAW backlog). No operator control over backfill pace, no progress visibility.

Scope: (1) configurable rate/throttle for historical backfills so imports don't saturate pipeline/NATS/creation-time chunks; (2) surface import rate, position, ETA, backlog depth via sinexctl + telemetry. Overlaps gh#2182 remaining item 2 — implement as one pacing mechanism.
design: DECISION: pacing lives at the SOURCE SCAN LOOP (scan_historical), not the event engine — throttling the consumer just moves the backlog into NATS (the incident shape). Mechanism: a rate budget in ScanArgs/RuntimeConfig (events/sec and bytes/sec, operator-set via binding config + overridable per replay/import operation), enforced by the scan loop between batches; progress = the source's checkpoint position vs the known horizon, emitted as reflexive progress events + exposed via sinexctl ops (import list showing rate/position/ETA/backlog depth). Backpressure coupling: pause when raw-stream depth exceeds a threshold from the n23.3 capacity table (pull the threshold from config now, formalize when n23.3 lands). One mechanism for all three entry points: historical scans, replay re-ingest, staged imports. PITFall: do not gate realtime/continuous capture behind the same budget — pacing applies to catch-up work only.
acceptance_criteria: A deliberately large historical import (fixture or journald window) run at a configured rate holds raw pending below the threshold for the whole drain; sinexctl shows live rate/position/ETA; unpaced behavior is impossible without an explicit --unlimited override; continuous capture latency unaffected during a paced import (measured). VERIFY: e2e paced-import test + live dev-store run with xtask analytics store snapshots.
notes: 2026-07-04 storage-growth citation: use xtask analytics storage-growth --projection-days 365 --limit 20 --json from sinex-n23.2 for pacing parameters. Live dev-store artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/storage-growth-live-20260704T0254Z/summary.json. Key numbers: 6,313 materials; 27,985,746,914 material bytes; 60,416,482 parsed events; browser.history = 94.1% of material bytes; projected 365d material growth about 4.12 TB under the current short-window catch-up rate; core.events = 2 chunks / 66,914,197,504 bytes; Timescale compression configured but 0 compressed chunks. Pacing decisions should use this command as the pre-run baseline and re-run it after large imports; do not tune from incident anecdotes alone.

title: Reflection 1-byte materials: remove prime(), defrag capability, granularity config, rename
description:
75% of registry materials (25,667/34,225) are exactly 1 byte, minted by self-observation.* automata. ROOT CAUSE NAILED: AutomatonRuntime::initialize (adapter/mod.rs:381-404) builds a fresh SelfObserver and prime() appends vec![b'\n']; on drop the buffered writer finalizes the 1-byte material. Every automaton restart mints one (~2,800/day during the ack_pending crisis).

Scope:
- E (first, ~1 line): remove observer.prime() at adapter/mod.rs:404 — the 5s FK-race retry from #1243 already covers what prime() guarded. Stops all future creation.
- A: general source-material defragmentation capability (coalesce rewrite respecting occurrence identity = (source_material_id, anchor_byte) columns; new interpretation ids; archive cascade). Existing 25,667 are dormant (~25KB) — no urgency.
- B: per-source granularity config + UX (sinexctl sources).
- D: rename self-observation -> reflection (changes source_identifier strings in prod data — decide migrate vs leave-old-prefix).

Refs: gh#2184 comments, .agent/scratch/research-2184-fragmentation.md.
design: SEQUENCING (E is committed-or-trivial, the rest is deliberate): E — remove observer.prime() (adapter/mod.rs:404); the #1243 5s FK-race retry covers what prime() guarded; verify no automaton-restart mints a material afterward. THEN the general capability in value order: A — defragmentation as an OPERATOR-VISIBLE operation built on existing machinery (coalesce = write merged material, re-anchor via replay semantics: occurrence identity is the (source_material_id, anchor_byte) COLUMNS so coalescing mints new interpretations + archive cascade — never mutate rows in place; this is a special case of replay, implement it through the replay/ops plane, not a new subsystem); B — per-source granularity config surfaced in sinexctl sources (the knob n23.2's growth model will want); D — the rename decision: self-observation -> reflection changes source_identifier strings in prod data; DECIDE explicitly: rename-with-migration (replay-friendly: new events under new id, old stay archived) vs leave-old-prefix-document-alias — default to alias-only unless the telemetry-lane split (x9i) forces a hard cut, in which case fold D into x9i. PITFALL: the 25,667 existing 1-byte materials are ~25KB dormant — do NOT run defrag against prod just to tidy; defrag lands as capability + test, actual prod compaction waits for a reason.
acceptance_criteria: E: zero new 1-byte materials across a week of automaton restarts (registry query by size+created-after). A: defrag operation demonstrated on a fixture store — N fragment materials coalesce, events re-anchor with preserved occurrence identity, old interpretations archived, lineage intact. B: granularity visible+settable per source. D: decision recorded here with rationale; if rename executed, prod queries by both names return coherent results.
notes: 2026-07-04 storage-growth citation: use xtask analytics storage-growth --projection-days 365 --limit 20 --json from sinex-n23.2 for granularity and defrag decisions. Live artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/storage-growth-live-20260704T0254Z/summary.json. Key numbers: 0 one-byte materials in current dev state; 27,985,746,914 registered material bytes; browser.history = 94.1% of material bytes; core.events compression configured with 0 compressed chunks. This means lb8 defrag should remain an operator-visible capability/test rather than a prod-cleanup urgency for current dev state; granularity knobs should be justified by per-source bytes/day from this command.

title: Boot robustness for large imports: idempotent schema-apply guards, paced drain, DLQ triage
description:
The compression-policy flap fix is on master (auto policy removed). Remaining, per the final corrected comments (NOTE: import-mode UUIDv7-from-ts_orig and chunk-interval changes were RETRACTED — never falsify ts_coided):

1. Schema-apply boot-race fix: BOOTSTRAP_SQL (apply.rs:1168-1207), PRIVACY_SCHEMA_SQL (:1375), and create_triggers_and_functions (:896) re-take AccessExclusive every boot via CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION / DROP+CREATE TRIGGER, racing CAGG refresh policies -> 55P03 flap. Fix: ensure_* guards that structurally match live objects via pg_trigger/pg_get_functiondef and SKIP when current (zero exclusive locks on no-change boot). Design: .agent/scratch/research-schema-apply-idempotency.md. Quiescing the TS scheduler stays operator-runbook only.
2. Paced restart backlog drain (RAW ~1.24M/2GiB at incident) — overlaps gh#2185.
3. DLQ triage (~29.6K/2GiB) — characterize + fix cause.

Relevant to the production-restore campaign: do (1) before prod bring-up.
acceptance_criteria: Remaining scope only (prong 1 closed via sinex-yss): (2) paced restart drain — after a restart with a large seeded raw backlog, drain proceeds at bounded rate without OOM/lock contention, sharing the sinex-2n9 pacing mechanism (do not build a second throttle); (3) DLQ triage — the ~29.6K/2GiB incident-era DLQ characterized by failure class with counts, each class either replayed (recovered count recorded), fixed at cause (bead or commit cited), or explicitly discarded with rationale; DLQ ends the task at a decided-zero state. VERIFY: paced-drain e2e scenario + a triage report artifact (analysis-lane friendly) linked here.
notes: 2026-07-03 update: prong 1 schema-apply boot-race fix closed via sinex-yss. Local implementation guards no-change function/trigger DDL, skips destructive telemetry read-model rebuild when telemetry drift is empty, skips telemetry CA/policy SQL unless drift exists, and uses if_not_exists on telemetry CA policy adds. Proof: xtask check -p sinex-schema job 2001349 exit 0; targeted sinex-schema guard tests job 2001350 exit 0. Remaining ddy scope: paced backlog drain and DLQ triage.

title: Email: remaining runtime tier (continuous IDLE runtime, disclosure render paths, secret deployment)
description:
Umbrella: email.mailbox package family. Nearly complete: staged RFC822/Maildir/MBOX modes, Gmail/IMAP scheduled sync + IDLE execution, OAuth refresh + XOAUTH2 runtime (#2169-#2171), durable provider state, mailbox projection + derivations, ALL operator operations real (authorize/sync/pause/resume/inspect/replay/forget-account/fetch-attachments/export/rebuild-projection), Gmail/IMAP raw material fetch/export, View+DLQ disclosure regressions.

Remaining:
- Continuous IMAP IDLE runtime robustness (long-lived runner, reconnect/backoff as runtime behavior not just bounded op).
- DisclosureContext Completion/Telemetry/Log render paths (variants exist, no render path anywhere — cross-cutting, not email-only).
- Deployment secret wiring: agenix *.age -> file-path env like SINEX_API_ADMIN_TOKEN_FILE (Sinnix boundary, gated by gh#1738).

Full spec on GitHub #1469 (body + long comment trail is the AC record).
acceptance_criteria: Remaining three items each proven: (1) IDLE runtime — a mailbox with IDLE enabled survives connection drop + server restart with automatic reconnect/backoff, capturing a message that arrives during the outage after recovery (e2e or live proof); (2) DisclosureContext Completion/Telemetry/Log variants render through real paths (cross-cutting — land in the shared disclosure layer, cite the render sites); (3) agenix secret wiring: prod mailbox syncs with credentials from *.age via file-path env, no secret in the Nix store (gated by gh#1738 — if still blocked, record the gate and close the other two). Full AC record remains gh#1469's body+trail; this bead closes when the three named remainders are individually verified there.

title: Media capture: remaining runtime tier (audio driver, screen video, capture-runtime health, raw-media lifecycle)
description:
Umbrella: comprehensive audio/screen-video/OCR capture. Most of the contract tier is DONE (payloads, contracts, admission, staged parsers, bounded worker execution w/ worker_budget+capture_debt, live screen capture driver via general SourceDriver + NixOS glue #2172-#2174, disclosure regressions).

Remaining (from the 2026-06-27 trail comment — same general-runtime pattern as screen):
- Audio capture driver (AudioCaptureBackend trait, e.g. pw-record/parecord) with on-demand session + live-session modes.
- Screen video (record_video) — encoder/segment material.
- media.capture_runtime.observed event for capture health/coverage (general analog of email.capture_runtime.observed).
- Raw-media lifecycle/disclosure via the existing privacy/lifecycle subsystems (deny/quarantine/encrypted/derived-only/retention/deletion, visible posture).
- Runtime-populated coverage/debt rows (model unavailable, private-mode block, budget pressure, backlog).

Full spec + AC matrix lives on GitHub #1043; the body is authoritative there. Verify: xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(/media/)'; NixOS eval with media+desktop enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Per the remaining list: audio driver captures a live session via the AudioCaptureBackend (pw-record or parecord) in both on-demand and live-session modes with materials registered; record_video produces a playable segment material; media.capture_runtime.observed events flow and appear in sources status; raw-media lifecycle posture (deny/quarantine/encrypted/derived-only/retention) visible and enforced for one exemplar file per mode; coverage/debt rows populate under induced budget pressure. VERIFY: xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(/media/)'; NixOS eval with media+desktop enabled; live dev capture smoke. Full AC matrix remains authoritative on gh#1043.
notes: 2026-07-03 positioning note (operator): the screenpipe-shaped capability (24/7 screen+OCR+audio, local-first) is built HERE, ourselves — this epic is that work and the contract tier + live screen driver already exist. screenpipe itself is a pattern donor and potential future source adapter (its plugin 'pipes' and capture formats are ingestible evidence), not a dependency and not the implementation. Same posture as the ecosystem analysis: ingest the capture ecosystem, compete with none of it.

title: Surface JetStream raw/DLQ/material stream state in xtask analytics store
description:
The SQL DLQ table showed zero rows while the live JetStream DLQ held 4,381 messages / ~413MB — xtask analytics store is blind to the actual live failure mode. Promote JetStream stream/consumer metadata (raw pending, DLQ depth/bytes, material/assembler stream state) into `xtask analytics store` so catch-up and jam triage don't require hand nats calls.

Refs: OPERATING-LOG.md 2026-07-03T05:22 next-decision; gh#2204 evidence section.
design: DECISION: extend xtask analytics store (the existing triage surface) with a JetStream section — do not create a parallel command. Source of data: NATS management API via the existing infra client (same URL resolution as xtask infra status): per-stream messages/bytes/first-last seq for the raw, DLQ, confirmed, and material/assembler streams; per-consumer pending/ack_pending/redelivered for the event-engine + automaton consumers. Render in both human table and --json; flag rows breaching thresholds (DLQ depth > 0 is always notable; raw pending vs realtime boundary). Also fold the SQL-vs-JetStream DLQ cross-check into the section explicitly (the motivating blindness: SQL table 0 rows while JetStream held 4,381/413MB) — show both numbers side by side so divergence is visible, since they measure different stages.
acceptance_criteria: xtask analytics store shows JetStream stream+consumer state alongside the DB snapshot in one invocation; the SQL-DLQ vs JetStream-DLQ pair renders side by side; --json stable for scripting; works against the dev store with NATS at :4308 without hand nats-cli calls. VERIFY: run against a dev store with seeded DLQ messages; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(analytics)'.
close_reason: Completed: xtask analytics store now reports JetStream stream/consumer state, renders SQL-vs-JetStream DLQ side by side, and emits the same data in JSON. Verification: xtask check -p xtask; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(analytics)'; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(runtime_store)'; xtask analytics store --json showed NATS nats://localhost:4308 available, 8 JetStream DLQ messages vs 0 SQL unresolved rows, and raw consumer backlog visible.

title: Audit stale devloop branch garden
description: Local branch inventory still contains many old pr/sinex-devloop/* branches that are not merged by Git's graph because the devloop train was squash-reconciled through later PRs, especially #2262. Build a branch-by-branch ledger that maps each old branch to the first-parent PR or Beads issue that superseded it, preserves any unique residual patch as a new issue/PR, then remove only branches whose value is proven redundant after explicit destructive-action confirmation.

title: Multi-device single-user sync: capture satellites to one substrate
description: Resolve the horizontal-expansion trust tension (018 meta-gap): the operator's phone + laptop + server as capture satellites feeding one substrate; host as a dimension (already on every event) plus a host.identity entity. NOT multi-tenant, NOT federation, NOT CRDT - single user, single logical writer per material, so conflicts are structurally impossible. The single-trust-domain non-goal is preserved (one user, many devices).

title: Ambient surfaces: standing digests and resume briefs (push, not pull)
description: The sessionstart-sinex-recall hook already pushes context on session start; generalize to oracle-style standing digests (episode summary, coverage-error report, derivation-health board) plus resume briefs. The habit-loop half of the 7z0 UX epic - Sinex surfaces, rather than only answering when queried.

title: Counterfactual replay: re-derive a past window under an alternate regime, diff
description: Replay is already non-idempotent (re-derives under CURRENT rules) and the shadow-lane diff already A/Bs entity/relation regimes. Generalize to interventional what-if: re-derive the whole derived plane over a past window under regime B, diff vs live. The artifact: 'here is what my derived plane WOULD look like with this new rule, before I commit'. Immutable material makes any past window re-interpretable without loss.

title: Reflection lane: an LLM proposes new derivation kinds as curation proposals
description: An LLM lane reads the derived plane and proposes NEW derivation kinds as CurationProposals; the taxonomy grows by adjudicated proposal, not hand-coding. This composes the three bold bets: deriv-dsl makes a proposal expressible as data, counterfactual-replay makes it evaluable before promotion, curation makes promotion safe. A system that extends its own ontology under adjudication.

title: Split the 3012-line sinexctl mcp.rs god-file
description: crate/sinexctl/src/mcp.rs is 3012 lines - the largest source file in the workspace - hosting the entire MCP tool surface (~60 tools) in one module. Review friction and merge-conflict hotspot; the agent-facing contract lives here so it changes often.

title: context_pack MCP tool: project scoping degraded to a source-prefix guess (#1095 DTO unfinished)
description: The sinex.context_pack MCP tool — an AGENT-FACING surface (the widest way agents consume Sinex) — does not actually scope by project. crate/sinexctl/src/mcp.rs:~2808: project_path is meant to drive a project-scoped query but the real context-pack DTO from #1095 was never finished, so the code falls back to treating the path as a SOURCE-PREFIX hint: 'if the path parses as an EventSource, filter sources to that string'. For any real project path (e.g. /realm/project/sinex) this silently matches nothing or the wrong thing, and the agent receives a context pack that looks scoped but isn't — the exact 'misleading surface' failure mode (standing rule 6: honest gaps). This is distinct from project.attribution (a4w.3.3, the deep entity-join that would make project scoping CORRECT): even before that lands, the tool must not pretend to scope. Options: (a) honest degradation — if project_path can't be properly resolved, return unscoped results WITH an explicit caveat naming the limitation and the blocking work (#1095 / a4w.3.3), rather than a silent wrong filter; (b) wire real project scoping once project.attribution exists. Do (a) now (cheap, honest), (b) when the substrate lands.
design: DECISION: mirror the recall honesty pattern — never silently mis-scope. Immediate fix: when project_path is present, attempt real resolution (project entity if project.attribution is live; else the documented fallback), and whenever the result is not a true project scope, attach a structured caveat to the context-pack output ('project_path scoping unavailable: returning source-hint/unscoped results; blocked on #1095 context-pack DTO / a4w.3.3 project.attribution') so the consuming agent knows the scope is approximate. Real scoping (b) becomes a follow-up wired to a4w.3.3. Check the other MCP tools in the same pass for sibling silent-degradation (nbi.6 covers the inert LLM read surfaces; this is the context-pack instance).
acceptance_criteria: context_pack with a project_path either scopes correctly or returns results carrying an explicit scope-limitation caveat naming the blocking work; no path silently produces a wrong-but-plausible filter; a test asserts the caveat appears when real scoping is unavailable; the #1095 follow-up for true project scoping is linked to a4w.3.3.
close_reason: Implemented context_pack project_path honesty on this branch: project_path now resolves to an explicit scope state; true project scoping is not claimed until #1095 / sinex-a4w.3.3 lands; real filesystem paths return unscoped packs with context_pack.project_scope_unavailable caveat; source-like strings return source-hint filtered packs with the same scope-limitation caveat; no-project_path remains uncaveated unscoped. Verification: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(context_pack)' job 2001435 passed; CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask check -p sinexctl job 2001436 passed.

title: Application-level semantic hooks: neovim buffer events + kitty deep remote-control (editor/terminal semantics)
description: From the operator's multi-level-capture design conversation: the highest-semantic capture happens INSIDE applications, above the compositor. Two named targets not yet sourced (browser is rkv.3; terminal COMMANDS are shell.atuin already): (1) NEOVIM — a Lua autocmd hook emitting buffer/edit semantics (BufEnter/BufLeave/BufWrite: file path, filetype, mode, cursor, project) — the editor's view of what was being worked on, which fs-watcher (file.modified) cannot see (fs sees the write; nvim sees the intent, the navigation, the time-in-buffer before saving); feeds work.episode/change.episode with editor-grain attention. (2) KITTY deep remote control — beyond command capture: focus events with cwd + foreground processes + scrollback state (kitty's remote protocol exposes these), giving terminal ATTENTION and working-directory context that shell history alone lacks. The operator's framing: 'different levels of abstraction... correlated in all kinds of fun ways later' — each level is deliberately partial; the join is where value emerges. The multi-level-redundancy design principle (capture the same act at compositor + app + system level, eliminate redundancy downstream) is Sinex-native: overlapping interpretations of one occurrence, coalesced/tombstoned via the lifecycle+defrag machinery (lb8) when one level provably subsumes another — record that as doctrine, not a new mechanism.
design: DECISION: each app hook is an EXTERNAL PRODUCER (nvim Lua -> systemd-cat/NATS bridge; kitty python-kitten -> bridge) publishing EventIntent — same external-producer pattern as polylogued/hyprland-plugin, sinexd hosts nothing. Kinds by role: editor.buffer.focused / editor.buffer.written (Activity+Content role, Point; file ref for entity-join to project.attribution and fs events; cursor/mode as payload); terminal.focus.observed (Activity role; cwd + foreground_processes for project entity-join, complementing shell.atuin command events). Occurrence keys from (app session, buffer/window id, timestamp). ts_orig = the in-app event time (RealtimeCapture). REDUNDANCY DOCTRINE (record in the derivation catalog, do not build new machinery): the same real act may be captured at multiple levels (nvim BufWrite + fs file.modified + hyprland focus); these are distinct interpretations of related occurrences, NOT duplicates to suppress at ingest — they carry different information; downstream coalescing/eviction (lb8 defrag, lpz events-are-cache) removes a level only when another provably covers its information, as an explicit operator-visible lifecycle op. START SMALL per the operator's own 'add only what annoys you not having' — nvim buffer hook first (highest editor-semantic value, trivial Lua), kitty deep hook second. PITFALL: do not let app hooks re-capture what a lower level already has well (kitty COMMANDS stay atuin's job — capture terminal ATTENTION/cwd here, not re-log commands).
acceptance_criteria: A neovim editing session produces buffer-focus/write events with file+project refs queryable and joinable to the same window's fs events; a kitty pane's cwd and foreground process are captured as terminal attention distinct from the atuin command log; both run as external producers; the multi-level redundancy doctrine is written into the derivation catalog; no app hook duplicates a lower level's well-covered data (kitty does not re-log commands). VERIFY: nvim + kitty bridges emit to dev NATS; cross-level join query (nvim buffer x fs modified x window focus for one edit) returns coherent multi-level evidence.

title: Compositor-plugin telemetry tier: focus-change reasons, input latency, render/damage perf (Hyprland-internal)
description: From the operator's capture design conversation: a Hyprland PLUGIN can access data available NOWHERE else — the compositor internals. Distinct from the IPC socket2 fix (sinex-60r, which gives clean real-time focus/workspace/window transitions from the socket); this is the deeper tier that requires living inside the compositor. Genuinely-unique signals the operator flagged as most valuable: (1) FOCUS-CHANGE REASONS — not just that focus changed but WHY (mouse click / alt-tab / workspace switch / window close) — turns attention.stream from 'what had focus' into 'how attention moved', which is qualitatively richer for interruption.event (a4w.3.2) and work.episode; (2) input latency (keypress -> window receipt) and compositor render/damage/frame perf — a machine-health signal tied to the actual desktop, feeding machine.context (a4w.3.5) ('that felt laggy' becomes measurable); (3) window geometry/animation/group operations. The operator also settled the storage question in-conversation: single events table with event_type as the virtual-table discriminator + partial indexes handles 144Hz fine — which is exactly Sinex's existing model (one hypertable, typed payloads, event-type routing), so NO separate table is needed; high-frequency perf events are just a declared high-volume class.
