design: DECISION: this is an OPTIONAL deep tier layered above sinex-60r, not a replacement — 60r's socket2 driver is the baseline attention source and ships first; the plugin tier is additive and Hyprland-specific (accept the coupling: operator is single-user on Hyprland, extension.md-style external-producer if/when portability matters). Shape: a Hyprland plugin (C++) as an EXTERNAL PRODUCER publishing EventIntent to NATS (same pattern as polylogued) — sinexd does not host it; the plugin writes to a shm/socket bridge that publishes. SPLIT BY VOLUME CLASS + SEMANTIC ROLE into distinct kinds so the high-frequency perf lane never drowns the semantic lane (the telemetry-lane-separation lesson, x9i, applied at capture): desktop.focus.changed (Activity role, Point, carries the REASON enum — the high-value low-volume signal); desktop.render.sampled (Reflexive/machine role, high-volume — declared volume class, its own retention, feeds machine.context, NOT the activity plane); desktop.input.latency (machine role, sampled not per-event). Interval lifting (focus spans) stays L2 (catalog rule 4) — the plugin emits transitions with reasons, not spans. PITFALL: do not emit per-frame at 144Hz into the main event stream — sample/aggregate render perf at the source (e.g. per-second percentiles) or route to the reflection lane; raw per-frame is capture abuse. The operator's single-table conclusion is correct for Sinex and needs no schema change.
acceptance_criteria: Focus changes carry a reason (mouse/alt-tab/workspace/close) distinguishable in queries; a laggy interval is visible as elevated desktop.render latency joinable to what was focused; the perf lane's volume does not appear in activity-plane recall (separated like telemetry); the plugin runs as external producer with sinexd unaware of its process; render sampling is bounded at source (no raw 144Hz in the stream). VERIFY: plugin emits to dev NATS, events queryable; volume-class check that render events route to the reflection lane.

title: AT-SPI2 semantic capture: accessibility widget trees as lossless digital text (the anti-OCR source)
description: From the operator's comprehensive-capture design conversation (2026-07-03 download): the standout insight was that AT-SPI2 accessibility APIs expose complete widget trees — 'so much semantic data', 'why isn't this shit already done'. This is the CONTENT role's answer without OCR: on-screen text as actual digital strings (labels, text fields, document content, menu items) with structural context (widget hierarchy, roles, the owning application), not lossy pixel recognition. It fills exactly the catalog's weakest role (Content = 'weak: documents live, screen-OCR partial'). Complementary to OCR (a4w.3.7), not redundant: AT-SPI2 gives exact text for accessibility-aware toolkits (GTK/Qt apps expose it); OCR remains the fallback for canvas/pixel surfaces (some Electron, games, video). The operator's own framing: hook toolkit accessibility to 'supplant lossy OCR' where digital text already exists; route by surface type downstream (AT-SPI2-capable window -> skip OCR).
design: DECISION: a continuous SourceDriver subscribing to the AT-SPI2 D-Bus registry (org.a11y.atspi) for focus/text-changed/document events, emitting semantic text observations anchored to the active window interval. Kind: ui.text.observed (Content role, StateObservation/Point) carrying text content BY MATERIAL ANCHOR (accessibility snapshots can be large — register the snapshot as material, event references it; never embed bulk, catalog rule 2), widget role, app identity, and the window ref for entity-join to attention.stream. Occurrence key = (window, widget path, content hash) so an unchanged field re-observed does not duplicate (equivalence key). ts_orig = observation time (RealtimeCapture — it is live perception). PRIVACY: this reads on-screen text including passwords fields and private content — declare Sensitive tier; password/secret widgets (role PASSWORD_TEXT) suppressed at source; relies on the central redaction chokepoint for the rest. VOLUME: potentially high on text-heavy focus churn — bounded by change-detection (only on text-changed/focus events, not polling) and volume-classed before joining wildcard fan-in. SURFACE-ROUTING: emit a capability signal (window is AT-SPI2-covered) so the OCR lane (a4w.3.7) can skip covered surfaces — the operator's 'we know we have it here, don't bother with OCR' routing. PITFALL: AT-SPI2 coverage is app-dependent (native GTK/Qt strong, Electron/Wayland-native variable); the source must render coverage honestly (which windows it can and cannot see) rather than implying total capture.
acceptance_criteria: Focused text in a GTK/Qt app (e.g. a document, a form field) is captured as digital text queryable via sinexctl, anchored to the window interval and resolvable to the app; password-role widgets produce no cleartext (test); an AT-SPI2-blind window (canvas/pixel app) renders as an explicit coverage gap, not silent absence; re-observing unchanged text does not duplicate occurrences; a covered window emits the OCR-skip capability signal. VERIFY: driver test against a known-accessible app fixture + live dev capture; privacy suppression test for PASSWORD_TEXT role.

title: Phase B: Sinex-primary evidence — Polylogue SQLite demotes to rebuildable read model
description: The long-term conceptually-correct form (epic backend trajectory): Sinex holds the evidence (session materials, attachment blobs, normalized observation events, provenance); Polylogue's SQLite becomes what Sinex doctrine calls a consumer-maintained projection — Polylogue-owned, Polylogue-shaped, rebuildable from the substrate. Polylogue keeps: its ontology (authority over what session/message/compaction/work-packet mean), its read models and indexes (FTS, embeddings, session topology — SQLite remains the right store for these), its UX and CLI, and full standalone operation (no Sinex -> Polylogue ingests exports into its own store exactly as today; the substrate is an upgrade, not a dependency). What changes with Sinex present: the archive's ground truth is the Sinex material registry + CAS (session JSONL, attachments, provider exports registered once, referenced everywhere); Polylogue's normalization writes observations to Sinex (sibling vocabulary child) and maintains its SQLite BY CONSUMING them (or its own parse — implementation choice, but the rebuild test is the contract); deletion/privacy actions flow through Sinex lifecycle (tombstones/archive) so the evidence plane has one authority for retention.
design: DECISIONS: (1) the contract is the REBUILD TEST, not a storage trait: polylogue rebuild --from-sinex reconstructs the SQLite read models from Sinex materials+events and a parity diff against the incumbent store comes back explainable-empty — that command existing and passing IS Phase B; internal storage abstraction is Polylogue's own business (polylogue-repo work, filed there when execution starts). (2) No dual-truth: while both stores exist, writes have one direction per data class — materials/observations: Polylogue producer -> Sinex; read models: Sinex/parse -> SQLite; never SQLite -> Sinex backfill except the one-time historical import. (3) Polylogue features that are pure read-model (marks, read-state, analysis notes) REMAIN SQLite-native but their actions also emit polylogue.annotation.* observations (vocabulary child) so the evidence plane sees operator behavior — the read state itself is not evidence, the act of reading is. (4) Sequencing: strictly after the vocabulary child proves stable at full grain and the moment-search child proves the substrate answers questions Polylogue's own store cannot — Phase B without demonstrated substrate value is architecture for its own sake (construct-validity gate).
acceptance_criteria: polylogue rebuild --from-sinex passes the parity test on the real archive; Polylogue standalone mode still fully functional (its suite green with no Sinex configured); retention/deletion of a session flows through Sinex lifecycle and the rebuilt read model reflects it; the one-direction write rule holds (no code path writes SQLite-derived data back as Sinex evidence).

title: Satellite capture hosts: horizontal expansion beyond sinnix-prime, with a multi-host VM suite
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03): design horizontal expansion beyond the current single-machine setup. Precision on the negative space: 'no federation' stays — ONE user, ONE database, ONE trust domain, ONE adjudication authority. What expands is CAPTURE topology: additional hosts (laptop, VPS, future devices) run capture-only Sinex — sources + privacy + NATS publisher, no event engine, no Postgres, no API — publishing to the central NATS on sinnix-prime. The substrate is already shaped for this: events carry host; NATS is the transport boundary; source contracts are host-agnostic; mTLS material exists. What's missing: (1) a capture-only deployment profile in the NixOS module (sources subset + publisher config, engine/api/db disabled); (2) disconnection tolerance — a satellite must buffer capture across network partitions without loss (JetStream leaf node with local persistence, or a local spool with replay-on-reconnect; leaf node is the NATS-native answer — decide with a bounded spike); (3) transport security over the tailnet (mTLS certs per host, token scoping so a satellite can publish raw events and nothing else); (4) per-host source bindings in the manifest (host -> enabled sources); (5) MaterialReadySet and source-material registration flow when the material's bytes live on the satellite — DESIGN QUESTION: materials ship to central CAS (upload lane) vs register-remote-and-fetch-on-demand; shipping to central CAS is the doctrine-consistent answer (L0 lives where the truth lives) — size-bounded with the existing 10MB NATS discipline pushing large blobs through a side channel. (6) VM-suite revamp: the current suite is single-VM; multi-host needs a two-node NixOS VM test (central + satellite) proving capture on the satellite persists centrally, partition-and-reconnect loses nothing (checkpoint honesty under partition = r6d territory), and a satellite cannot exceed its token scope.
design: DECISION: satellites are dumb captors — no local interpretation, no local truth; every satellite event flows the normal pipeline with host provenance; the central host remains the only writer of canonical state. Sequence: (a) spike leaf-node-vs-spool with partition test, (b) capture-only NixOS profile, (c) cert/token provisioning story (agenix per host), (d) material upload lane, (e) two-node VM test as the merge gate. Do not build device-specific glue here — phone stays export-bridge (rkv.5) until this lane exists and proves itself on a laptop.
acceptance_criteria: Two-node VM test green: satellite-captured events queryable centrally with correct host attribution; partition during capture -> zero loss after reconnect (counted); satellite token cannot query or write beyond raw publish; capture-only profile evaluates with engine/api/db asserted off; a real second host (laptop) deployment documented as the runbook.

title: Subsumption ledger + retirement gate: per-module verdicts with parity evidence
description: The census and close-out instrument for the epic. One tracked table (docs/ or .agent/, tracked in git) covering every Lynchpin module across all four layers: sources.*, substrate.*, derived products, analysis.* — column shape: module -> question it answered -> verdict {covered-by-sinex | port-as-source | port-as-standing-derivation (L3 automaton) | port-as-recorded-analysis | port-as-query-unit | retire-dead} -> sinex surface -> parity artifact -> status. EXCLUDED rows (operator 2026-07-03): dms/, code_index, code_quality, code_history_claims, tool_rules (codebase analytics — leave be for now); thoughtspace is not Lynchpin-internal but is likewise dead-end, not a porting target. This absorbs the ledger half of sinex-44q (whose scope was sources-only); 44q remains the sources-porting execution bead. Retirement rule from the epic: a module's schedule/invocation turns off only when its ledger row has a parity artifact; the final state is Lynchpin running nothing that Sinex answers.
design: FORMAT: one markdown table checked in at crate/sinexd/docs/automata/lynchpin_subsumption_ledger.md (next to the derivation catalog it feeds); columns: module | question it answered | verdict {covered | port-as-source | port-as-L3-automaton | port-as-recorded-analysis | port-as-query-unit | retire-dead | EXCLUDED} | sinex surface | parity artifact | status. POPULATION: enumerate mechanically (ls lynchpin/{sources,substrate,analysis} + the derived-products dir), then judge each — the judging is analysis-lane work (one recorded analysis producing the first full draft is the ideal bootstrap, dogfooding k4c.1 if it exists by then; plain authorship otherwise). EXCLUDED rows filled immediately (dms/, code_index, code_quality, code_history_claims, tool_rules) so the census is complete from day one. The ledger is living: verdicts flip to done as ports land; the epic's exit criteria read from it.
acceptance_criteria: Ledger exists tracked in-repo with a row for every module in lynchpin/{sources,substrate,analysis}/ and the derived-products directory; no verdict is blank; every retire/covered verdict cites evidence; ported rows link beads. VERIFY: ls-based module enumeration cross-checked against ledger row count.

title: Physiological source: sleep/health export import (the taxonomy's zero-coverage role)
description: The derivation catalog's role table lists Physiological as 'zero live'. The data exists: Samsung Health and Sleep As Android exports under /realm/data/exports/health/. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (operator, 2026-07-03): do NOT build this on Lynchpin's intermediate formats. Lynchpin's sources.health/sources.sleep read 'processed' exports — its own ad-hoc preprocessing output. Sinex ingests the FUNDAMENTAL data: the original provider export files (Samsung Health export ZIP's per-datatype CSVs; Sleep As Android records export) registered as source materials exactly as the provider produced them, parsed by Sinex parsers that understand the raw provider schema. /realm/data/derived/lynchpin and any Lynchpin-processed form are not inputs. Higher-order products (daily aggregates, sleep-architecture features, sleep-x-productivity joins) are NOT computed at import time — they are derived events in Sinex's own layers, modeled properly (see the Lynchpin-subsumption epic), superseding the Lynchpin stopgap rather than porting it.
design: DECISION: (1) Materials = original provider files as-is; one material per export file; anchor = row byte offset; occurrence key = provider row identity (Samsung rows carry stable ids/timestamps; SaA rows are (start,end,tz) tuples) via equivalence key so re-export overlap does not duplicate. (2) L1 event kinds at raw grain per the catalog's extension rules: sleep.session (Interval, IntrinsicContent ts_orig, consumer: routine.baseline/machine.context), plus state observations for HR/steps/stress/SpO2/weight samples at the provider's native sampling grain — no import-time downsampling; volume-class first (Samsung HR can be ~1/min — bounded, fine). (3) L2: daily physiological rollups are windowed-automaton derived events keyed (domain, local-day) — NOT import-side aggregation. (4) Lynchpin's sleep_architecture_productivity/health_modeling analyses map to L3 composites under the subsumption epic; nothing from lynchpin.sources.health/sleep is referenced by this implementation. (5) Delivery via the staged-export lane + scheduled static-import glue (sinex-stm); new exports drop into the same directory and re-scan.
acceptance_criteria: Raw provider files registered as materials (registry rows point at the original CSVs, not derived/lynchpin paths); sleep sessions + samples queryable with ts_orig = provider-intrinsic timestamps; re-import of an overlapping export does not duplicate occurrences; daily rollup arrives as a derived event with the import events as parents; catalog role table updated from 'zero live'. VERIFY: parser tests against real export fixtures (scrubbed); live dev-store query; rg proves no lynchpin path references in the source contract.

title: Label or gate inert semantic llm read surfaces until P8
description: Dead-lane sweep follow-up from sinex-y7l. sinexctl semantic llm route-explain is deterministic and useful, but prompts and budget-report read event types that currently have no production emitter. Avoid a misleading CLI surface by labeling those commands as empty/read-only until P8, or gating them behind the embedding/ledger worker.
design: DECISION: label, do not gate — route-explain stays fully public; prompts and budget-report remain in the tree but their empty state must be self-explaining: when the backing event types have no producer, emit a structured empty-result envelope naming (a) the event types they read, (b) that no producer is currently deployed, (c) the bead/feature that wires it (5v6 / the P8 embedding-ledger worker) — so an operator or agent reading the output learns the system state instead of inferring zero cost. Implement once in the shared command-output layer if other inert read surfaces exist (check bm1's matrix for siblings) rather than hand-writing per command.
acceptance_criteria: route-explain remains usable. prompts and budget-report either produce explicit empty-until-P8 messaging with source/event requirements, or are gated from the public command tree until the producer exists. Tests cover the empty/gated behavior so users do not infer missing data means zero prompts/cost.
notes: Folded guidance: this is the CLI-surface instance of standing rule 6 (honest gaps) — absence of data must render as a stated gap with a cause, not as a plausible zero.
close_reason: Completed by PR #2292 / cf7b5ba43: route-explain remains usable under the focused llm test filter; prompts and budget-report now emit ViewEnvelope/CaveatView empty-producer messaging for llm.prompt_template.registered and llm.budget.ledger across CLI and MCP; API budget-report carries source.absent; tests cover the empty behavior so zero prompts/cost is not inferred from missing producers.

title: Calendar source (CalDAV/ics): the only capture of planned time
design: S-M effort. Calendar events as intentional-layer intervals: planned blocks with start/end/title(policy-scoped)/attendees(policy-scoped, feeds people dimension later). Shape: ics static-import first (fits the generic glue), CalDAV scheduled sync second (email provider-sync pattern). Occurrence identity = (calendar id, event uid, recurrence instance). Unlocks plan.vs.actual — the only source that captures intent-before-the-fact besides predictions.
acceptance_criteria: Planned blocks queryable as intervals; recurring events produce correct per-instance occurrences; plan.vs.actual join demonstrable for one real week; attendee disclosure policy-gated.

title: Browser content pipeline: crawl visited/saved pages into the content layer
design: Converts the biggest live activity stream (page.visited, ~58M parsed) into CONTENT: crawl pipeline off webhistory/raindrop URLs into document materials -> existing document layer (chunks, FTS, embeddings once the worker exists) -> entities. M effort. Design constraints: crawl is an acquisition (materials with fetch provenance + fetch time as ts_coided-like interpretation moment, original visit keeps its own event); politeness/budget caps; auth-walled pages skipped honestly as coverage debt; content-bearing rule 2 (CAS refs, never embed). Feeds: consumption.episode, topics, hybrid search value.
acceptance_criteria: Visited/saved URLs crawl into document materials with provenance; doc search returns them; skip/failure classes visible as coverage debt; volume budget declared; consumption.episode unblocked.

title: NixOS generations source: system-state evolution as events
design: Small (S) source, parse generations from the profile store + activation journal entries: generation number, timestamp, flake rev/inputs when recoverable. Every 'it broke after the rebuild' question becomes queryable — and joins machine.context ('this window ran generation N'). Deliciously self-similar for a NixOS operator. Shape: static/dirwalk over /nix/var/nix/profiles + journald activation records already captured.
acceptance_criteria: generation.activated events with flake rev where available; a rebuild during live capture produces the event; machine.context can annotate windows with the active generation.

title: change.episode / Agent Work Packet: intent, toil, verification, outcome per change
description: The self-hosting derivation and the Polylogue bridge object (Agent Work Packet from the recovered plan). One packet per change episode: agent session id(s) + agent identity (which agent/subagent), bead/intent ref (bd: — the intent leg from sinex-hlv), branch + commits + PR + CI/check runs (git/GitHub legs — rkv.1 supplies CI), xtask history (the toil/verification leg: checks run, tests passed/failed, wall time), tool spans (from agent.tool_call.* once sinex-4j2.1 flows), source-material refs, outcome class, and optionally follow-up lessons (annotation events). CRITICAL SHAPE RULE (settled, from the plan): there is NO intrinsic session_commit object — sessions are Polylogue's, commits are git's, checks are telemetry's; the packet is a DERIVED relation over them (entity-join on branch/repo/session ids + time-join on overlap), computed by this automaton, replayable, semantics-versioned. Consumers: Polylogue's agent-work UX (the front door for understanding agent work — layer 5 of sinex-4j2), the devloop's own retrospectives (cem.10), and the blinded-resumption demo arc (cem.7 wants exactly this packet as the resumption context).
design: DECISION: recipe per the catalog row — session ⊕ attention.stream ⊕ git ⊕ xtask-history ⊕ beads, entity-join keys = (repo, branch, session_id, bead_id), time-join = session interval overlap; emitted kind work.change_episode (Interval) with the packet as payload of REFS (every leg a resolvable ref, no copied content); packet completeness is honest — legs with no evidence render as named gaps (a packet with no CI leg says so); confidence absent (it is a join, not a guess). Field list is the AWP enumeration above; extension of legs (OTLP spans, blackboard notes) = new refs, not schema rework. PREREQS in dependency order: hlv (bead events), 4j2.1 (session/tool observations), rkv.1 (CI leg — optional, packet works without); attention.stream (.1) enriches but does not gate.
acceptance_criteria: For one real merged change: the packet exists as a derived event linking the actual session(s), bead, branch, commits, PR, checks, and xtask runs — every ref resolves; a change with a missing leg (no PR, direct commit) produces a packet with that gap named; Polylogue (or sinexctl) renders a work-packet view from it; replay after a recipe change re-derives packets with archived predecessors.

title: plan.vs.actual: the intentional loop closed with evidence
design: RECIPE: calendar intervals ⊕ attention.stream; predictions ⋈ resolutions; instructions ⋈ expectations (the last pair already exists via the reconciler). Prereqs: calendar source (capture epic) for the first join; predictions domain for the second. Emits plan.vs.actual comparison events per planned block: attended/ignored/displaced with evidence refs.
acceptance_criteria: For a week with calendar data: per-block verdicts with attention evidence; prediction-resolution joins render in the calibration report; no verdict without evidence refs.

title: screen.grounding: OCR text aligned to attention intervals
design: RECIPE: screen-OCR text ⊕ active-window interval -> 'what was literally on screen during this attention span'. Media lane machinery exists (screenshot driver + OCR worker path); this composite time-joins OCR segment events to focus spans. Content-bearing: references OCR materials, never embeds bulk (rule 2).
acceptance_criteria: Given a live capture window with screenshots+OCR, recall can show grounded screen text per attention span with material refs; disclosure policy applies to the rendered text.

title: routine.baseline + anomaly.event: typed deviations, never narratives
design: RECIPE: per-hour-of-week aggregates over attention.stream -> baseline; deviations -> anomaly.event with the measured delta. HARD RULE (standing operator feedback): typed data products only — the system computes deviations, the operator interprets them; no behavioral narratives, no psychological framing in payloads or rendering. Baseline window and deviation thresholds are declared constants with provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline queryable per hour-of-week; anomaly events carry the numeric delta + contributing evidence refs and zero interpretive prose; a synthetic injected deviation is detected (control).

title: machine.context overlay: state intervals contextualize anything
design: RECIPE: state intervals (load-high, swap-storm, VPN-on, on-battery — L2 interval lifting over system.monitor/journald transitions) ⊕ any queried window -> context annotations. 'That build was slow because swap storm.' Implementation is mostly L2 interval lifting plus a query-time overlay (not a stored composite per pair — overlay at render, rule: no materializing what nothing reads). The L2 state-interval kinds are the deliverable; the overlay is a recall/query feature consuming them.
acceptance_criteria: State-interval kinds flow (>=3: load, swap, network class); recall/context can annotate a window with overlapping state intervals; the slow-build-because-swap case demonstrable on real dev history.

title: Demo: devloop-as-source — Sinex answers questions about its own development
design: CLAIM: Sinex can answer questions about its own development (self-hosting closure). PROTOCOL: with the devloop + beads ingestion lanes flowing (sinex-hlv), ask via recall: 'what did the devloop decide about DLQ pressure on June 30 and what proof exists?' — the answer must interleave devloop decision events, bead lifecycle events, and git commit events from the same store, each row resolvable. INSTRUMENT: answers are checkable word-for-word against the tracked files (OPERATING-LOG/EVENTS.jsonl) — external ground truth by construction. FALSIFIER: wrong or unresolvable citations. PREREQ: sinex-hlv. CAPABILITY LANDING: none needed beyond hlv's contracts — that is the point; the demo is pure composition.
acceptance_criteria: Three distinct dev-history questions answered through the product surface (no raw SQL); every citation resolves; answers verified against tracked files in the artifact; one-command rerun; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: closed-loop focus actuation (desired-state loop, independently verified)
design: CLAIM: the desired-state loop generalizes beyond its pilot verb. PROTOCOL: session-boundary thrash detection -> a Proposal (the authority gate's second-ever kind) -> operator accepts -> instruction dispatches workspace-switch + notification-DND -> reconciler verdicts arrive from INDEPENDENT observation events (wm.hyprland/notification events — never the actuator's own claim). INSTRUMENT: the reconciler IS the measurement; verdicts from independent observation are the control against self-confirming actuation. FALSIFIER: verdict absent, wrong, or derived from the actuator's own output. PREREQ: instruction-verb registry (sinex-r7h) with the second verb (notification-DND). CAPABILITY LANDING: the thrash-detection heuristic lands as a real windowed automaton with evidence-derived thresholds, not demo glue.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end run recorded: detection event -> proposal -> judgment -> dispatch -> independent observation -> verdict, every hop a resolvable ref; the verdict provably cites observation events distinct from the instruction; one-command scenario driver; complete summary.json.

title: Automaton portfolio review: consume / gate-behind-profile / stop-shipping-enabled, per automaton
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-4.md#sinex-nbi4---automaton-portfolio-review. Review all 14 registered sinexd automata and classify each as keep default-enabled, profile-gate, consume-follow-up, or disable-default. Current evidence surfaces: crate/sinexd/src/automata/registry.rs; nixos/modules/lib/automata.nix; nixos/modules/default.nix currently defaults services.sinex.automata.enable and each automaton to true; nixos/modules/sources.nix builds SINEX_AUTOMATA_ENABLED; crate/sinexd/src/supervisor/mod.rs defaults unset env to all. Initial verdicts: keep canonicalizer/session/hourly/daily/health with explicit consumer evidence; keep analytics only if recall p1 consumes it; gate instruction-reconciler behind actuation; gate document-parser behind document profile until projection consumer replaces trigger path; stop default-enabled embedding-producer until real embedding worker/model effects land; gate tag/entity/resolver/relation/enricher behind semantic/shadow profile unless consumer evidence justifies default-on.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence table completed for all 14 automata with output event type, measured last-24h volume, downstream consumer, cost/incidents, and verdict. Operator signs off each row as keep/profile-gate/consume-follow-up/disable-default. Default deployment no longer runs zero-consumer automata without an explicit keep reason. Semantic/entity/relation automata are gated behind semantic/shadow profile. embedding-producer no longer emits truthful-looking receipts unless real embeddings/model effects are written. Nix examples and README show the new default/profile intent. SINEX_AUTOMATA_ENABLED behavior matches the Nix deployment surface. Verify with nix automata catalog eval, xtask check -p sinexd, and targeted automata tests.

title: NATS capacity model: one enforceable budget table for streams and consumers
design: DECISION (settled): replace incident-driven tuning (#2186 ack_pending -> #2200 in-flight byte budget -> #2204 retention: the same lesson three times) with explicit arithmetic, enforced. ARTIFACT: one table — per stream: max_bytes/max_msgs/retention; per consumer: ack_pending, fetch byte budget, expected fanout — with the worst-case aggregate memory formula (sum over consumers of ack_pending x avg-msg-size + fetch buffers, plus stream store) evaluated against the host budget. ENFORCEMENT: a supervisor startup check computes the aggregate from live stream/consumer configs and refuses (fail loud) when the worst case exceeds the configured host budget — the #2200 process-global byte budget becomes one term of a checked model instead of a lone guard. Include the reflection stream (telemetry-lane) once it exists. Keep the model in code next to the stream provisioning (runtime/jetstream_streams.rs), not in a doc that drifts.
acceptance_criteria: The budget table is computed from live config at startup; an over-budget configuration fails loud with the arithmetic in the error; the three past incidents replayed against the model (would it have caught them? show which term); doc comment names every constant's provenance.

title: Dead-lane detector: pub-surface reachability lint in xtask
design: Generalize the audit's zombie findings (batch_importer, pressure, material/, ConfirmationBuffer, occurrence_filter...) into a repeatable check: an xtask lint reporting pub items/lanes with zero non-test reachability (seed from rg call-site analysis; the codebase-memory graph can seed candidates but verify against source — its call edges are noisy on Rust). Run mode: advisory report first; promote to gate only if false-positive rate proves low. PURPOSE: 'typed 2x ahead of wiring' stays a strategy instead of decaying into entropy — the one-off sweep (dead-lane bead) cleans today's debt; this keeps it clean.
acceptance_criteria: xtask check surface (or standalone subcommand) emits the reachability report; current known zombies detected; false-positive review on one run documented; advisory-vs-gate decision recorded.

title: Analysis: storage growth model post-incident (settle granularity/pacing by numbers)
design: QUESTION: what does storage actually do over 12 months? INSTRUMENT: bytes/day/source from the registry + chunk layout + compression ratios from Timescale catalog views, projected forward. ARTIFACT: growth model with per-source contribution table. PURPOSE: settles the material-granularity and import-pacing parameter choices (reflection fix, pacing rates, chunk budgets) by numbers instead of re-litigating them per incident — the #2182/#2184/#2185 trio keeps being argued from single incidents.
acceptance_criteria: Model committed with per-source bytes/day, chunk-size distribution, compression ratios, and a 12-month projection; the granularity/pacing beads cite it for their parameter choices.
notes: Implemented executable storage-growth model on feature/xtask/storage-growth-analytics. New command: xtask analytics storage-growth --projection-days 365 --limit 20 --json. Output includes per-source bytes/day/projection, relation byte sizes, core.events chunk-size buckets, Timescale compression settings/chunk state, and assessment warnings. Live artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/storage-growth-live-20260704T0254Z/summary.json. Current live numbers: 6,313 materials; 27,985,746,914 material bytes; 60,416,482 parsed events; browser.history = 94.1% of material bytes; projected 365d material growth about 4.12 TB at current short-window catch-up rate; core.events = 66,914,197,504 bytes in two >=4GiB chunks; compression configured but 0 compressed chunks. Cited by sinex-2n9 and sinex-lb8 notes for pacing/granularity decisions. Verification: job 2001494 focused storage-growth tests passed; job 2001495 xtask check -p xtask passed; xtask docs check passed after docs sync; live command passed.
close_reason: Completed by xtask analytics storage-growth: committed model includes per-source bytes/day, chunk-size buckets, Timescale compression posture, 365-day projection, live artifact, and citations from sinex-2n9/sinex-lb8.

title: Analysis: privacy shadow audit — what default-zero-redaction leaves in cleartext
design: QUESTION: what does the deliberate default-zero-redaction posture currently leave in cleartext, concretely? INSTRUMENT: run the recognizer catalog (the #1042 recognizer work: Presidio/secret-scanner-derived seed rules) in REPORT-ONLY mode over a live sample of the dev store — no redaction applied, counts only. ARTIFACT: counts per recognizer class per source. PURPOSE: privacy stays dormant BY EVIDENCE rather than by default — the operator decides rule activation from real counts. This is posture measurement, not a security finding (single-user deployment; zero-redaction is by-design).
acceptance_criteria: Report-only counts per class/source committed; zero mutation of the store proven (row counts unchanged); operator shown the table; any activation decision becomes its own bead.
notes:
Research packet written: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-2-privacy-shadow-audit.md

Executable issue shape: add a report-only privacy shadow audit that runs the built-in recognizer catalog over bounded live-store event samples, walks payload string fields, and aggregates counts by recognizer/category/source/event/field without printing matched values or mutating stored data.

Key evidence: current dev DB policy tables sampled read-only show privacy.rules=0, enabled rules=0, field_rules=0, recognizer_backends=0. Bounded TABLESAMPLE found likely exposure classes in cleartext: home_path in shell.atuin/entity/webhistory, email in webhistory/git, and url_credentials in webhistory. Unbounded core.events aggregates were cancelled after ~1 minute, so implementation should default to bounded windows/limits, not full-store scans.

Proposed AC and verification commands are in the packet. Activation of any redaction rule is explicitly deferred to a separate bead.

title: Analysis: derivation lag distribution per automaton (p50/p99)
design: Executable issue packet written: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-n23-1-derivation-lag.md. First slice: add read-only xtask analytics derivation-lag over core.events derived rows. Primary metric is child.ts_coided - max(parent.ts_coided), grouped by automaton/output with p50/p95/p99/max/sample count/missing-parent/negative/replay counts. Existing sinexctl runtime automata lag is input-age-at-dispatch (now - ts_orig), not persisted derivation availability lag. No schema change needed for first slice; fall back to source/event_type/automaton_model when module_run_id is absent. Verification plan: xtask analytics derivation-lag --help; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(derivation_lag)'; xtask check -p xtask; manual JSON smoke plus comparison to sinexctl runtime automata.
acceptance_criteria: Per-automaton p50/p99 table exists with measurement window stated; recall/campaign caveats cite it; any automaton with pathological lag gets a follow-up bead.
notes: Research packet integrated 2026-07-03: see .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-2.md#sinex-n231-derivation-lag-distribution-per-automaton. Implementation shape: add xtask analytics derivation-lag over core.events derived rows; primary metric child.ts_coided - max(parent.ts_coided), grouped by automaton/output with p50/p95/p99/max/missing-parent/replay counts; keep ts_orig out of availability-lag measurement.

title: Analysis: consumption telemetry mining — which surfaces were ever invoked
design: QUESTION: which of the ~65 MCP tools, RPC methods, and CLI subcommands have EVER been invoked? INSTRUMENT: sinexd.api.* telemetry CAs (gateway stats) + xtask history for CLI; window = all available telemetry. ARTIFACT: usage-ranked surface list with zero-use surfaces flagged. PURPOSE: prune the vestigial (inert llm subcommands, unresolvable show kinds) with data instead of taste — feeds the dead-lane sweep and the descriptor-registry migration order.
acceptance_criteria: Every public surface has a usage count or an explicit 'telemetry cannot answer' caveat; zero-use list cross-checked against the consumption epic (something being built FOR does not count as vestigial); prune verdicts filed into the dead-lane sweep bead.
notes: Research packet written: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-nbi-2-consumption-telemetry.md. It turns the bead into an executable implementation issue: mine RPC usage from raw core.events gateway.rpc.call events; mine xtask usage from .sinex/state/xtask-history.db in query mode; emit sinexctl and MCP catalog rows with explicit catalog_only/telemetry_absent caveats until shell-history or first-class invocation telemetry exists. Proposed xtask surface: xtask analytics consumption --format json, with schema_version sinex.consumption-telemetry/v1, per-surface coverage/verdict/caveats, catalog joins, observation windows, implementation slices, AC, and verification commands. Live read-only sample: 886 gateway.rpc.call events across 24 methods since 2026-07-01; 19,257 xtask invocations across 31 command/subcommand pairs since 2026-02-13.

title: Analysis: payload vocabulary vs flow census (delete-or-implement list)
description: The payload registry has grown to a vocabulary far larger than the flowing inventory: 166 EventPayload structs (rg over crate/sinex-primitives/src derive+impl sites, 2026-07-03) against 35 register_source_contract! declarations and 24 register_source_runtime_binding! entries. Every struct with no emitter is vocabulary debt that the schema registry, docs, and derivation catalog all silently carry. This census is the delete-or-implement instrument feeding the dead-lane sweep (y7l) and the automaton portfolio decision (nbi.4).
design: QUESTION: which of the 166 typed EventPayloads actually flow to production emitters? (35 source contracts, 24 bindings, 14 automata — the payload registry is vocabulary, not inventory, and the gap grows silently.) INSTRUMENT: a recorded analysis (k4c.1 lane when it exists; plain script otherwise) cross-referencing payloads/ derive declarations against emitter call sites — rg for from_material/from_parents per payload type, plus the contract/binding/automaton-output registries. ARTIFACT: a three-column census — payload | emitter evidence | verdict(keep-flowing / implement-emitter / delete) — committed as the delete-or-implement list. NOTE: the 159-vs-~20 figure in earlier notes is superseded by 166/35/24 measured 2026-07-03.
acceptance_criteria: Census covers all 166 payload structs with an evidence-backed verdict each; DELETE verdicts feed y7l; implement-emitter verdicts become beads; the count discrepancy with the stm '48 contracts' figure is reconciled (see stm).
notes: Executable packet ready: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-nbi-1-payload-flow-census.md. Scope is no longer pure analysis: implement a compiled payload-flow census over get_all_payloads, all_source_contracts, source_runtime_bindings, parser_inventory_records, registered_source_factory_ids, hosted AUTOMATA, and explicit manual emitters/readers. First cleanup batch: delete or gate strongest dead declarations (Claude/ChatGPT ai.message, Raindrop bookmark, Samsung sleep, knowledgebase note, docs-library document, Reddit/Wykop social payloads), expand automata output schema coverage beyond the current 4-item test, and resolve embedding-producer document.embedded as typed schema or gated untyped output. AC and verification commands live in the packet.

title: Demo: blinded resumption duel (the thesis demo, campaign follow-up)
description: The headline. Runs only after the campaign's simpler committed baseline arm ships — do not let the stronger protocol delay the committed one.
design: CLAIM: Sinex makes an agent measurably better at cold-start work resumption. PROTOCOL: two identical agents answer 'reconstruct what the operator did yesterday 14:00-18:00 and propose the next action' — one with the Sinex MCP surface only, one with the status-quo toolkit (git log, atuin, filesystem). A judge scores dated factual claims against ground truth (operating log + operator recollection), BLINDED to which agent is which. INSTRUMENT: precision/recall of dated factual claims + usefulness rating of the proposed resumption action. FALSIFIER: the Sinex agent ties or loses. CONTROL: raw SQL banned — product surface only (exactly what earlier recall demos bypassed). PREREQ: the recall-v2 campaign at terminal state (sinex-9j9) — this is its blinded, agent-vs-agent upgrade; recall truthfulness (sinex-vhm) makes the Sinex arm honest. CAPABILITY LANDING: the ground-truth scoring rubric becomes the standing uplift-evaluation protocol for future context-surface changes.
acceptance_criteria: Blinding protocol documented and followed (judge cannot identify arms from transcripts); both transcripts + scoring committed; result reported either direction; one-command environment setup for both arms; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: semantic-lane duel with adjudicated promotion
description: Wave 3 tail — demonstrates the entire authority/semantic type system doing its actual job. Heaviest demo; do after the pair above proves the LLM plane.
design: CLAIM: the shadow-lane harness measures interpretation quality BEFORE anything becomes truth. PROTOCOL: regex entity lane vs LLM entity lane over the same semantic epoch; both scored against a PRE-REGISTERED golden set (labeled before the run); operator adjudicates disagreements in the curation workbench; the winning judgments promote to core.entities via lane promote. INSTRUMENT: precision/recall against the golden set + the lane diff report + the promotion provenance chain (proposal+judgment parents on every canonical row). FALSIFIER: the LLM lane losing to 4 regexes is a legitimate, reportable outcome. PREREQ: sinex-5v6 (model client) AND sinex-2nd (lane promote). CAPABILITY LANDING: the golden set becomes the standing lane-evaluation epoch fixture (the U9 class-4 drift check reuses it monthly).
acceptance_criteria: Golden set committed before lane runs; both lanes scored; >=1 adjudicated disagreement promoted with full provenance; the promoted rows' parents resolve to proposal+judgment events; complete summary.json.

title: Capture-domain portfolio: keep / prune / freeze decision per domain
description:
Operator-owned decision (situation-brief §9, the one real "overbuilt" question): of the ~48 source contracts and 24 capture domains, which are actually used and worth their upstream-format maintenance rot? Principle already stated by the brief: keep what serves ambient, honest, agent-facing context; prune or freeze the rest.

Agent prep (do): produce the decision input — per-domain table of {contract state, deployment state, event volume, last-used-in-consumption evidence, maintenance cost signal (upstream format churn)}. Most of this is queryable (source coverage + store analytics).
Operator decides: keep/prune/freeze per row. Prune = delete contracts (pre-release, no shims); freeze = keep code, no deployment, marked in catalog.

Do NOT let an agent make the portfolio call — flag for human decision when the input table is ready.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-3.md#sinex-5j8---capture-domain-portfolio-decision. This Bead produces an operator-ready capture-domain census and records the operator verdicts; it does not itself delete or deploy sources. Classify each domain as currently_flowing, dark_static_import, blocked_valuable, or dead_or_gate. Rows include source contract ids, namespace, source/event pairs, runtime binding modes, deployment surface, source-status readiness/continuity evidence, event_count, last_event_at, material_count, last_material_at, consumer/derivation evidence, upstream format/secret/runtime maintenance cost, proposed verdict, and linked Bead. Default rule: keep domains feeding honest agent-facing context or named L3 composites; implement-first domains unlocking high-value composites; freeze plausible domains with no current consumer; prune/gate vocabulary with no deployed flow and no named consumer.
acceptance_criteria: A committed capture-domain census exists with one row per domain and source-contract IDs; rows include deployment/readiness evidence from source coverage, not only code inventory; each row has proposed verdict keep/implement-first/freeze/prune-gate; every implement-first row links an existing implementation bead or names a follow-up; every prune/gate row links sinex-nbi.1, sinex-y7l, or a deletion/gating bead; operator verdicts are recorded separately from agent recommendations; no source contract is deleted or deployed as part of this decision bead.

title: Port Lynchpin sources into Sinex, with a promotion ledger
description:
Lynchpin is deliberately dormant with its sources slated to port into Sinex (operator-acknowledged, situation-brief §2). Nothing tracks which of its source families (ActivityWatch rollups, health, chats, git, shell, calendar views, evidence-graph inputs) are already covered by Sinex source contracts, which need porting, and which retire.

Do: (1) build the promotion ledger — one table: lynchpin source/analysis -> covered-by-sinex-contract | port | wrap-as-external | retire, with evidence for each verdict; (2) port the "port" column as normal source contracts through the static-import glue (sinex-stm); (3) record the ledger in the repo (docs or bead notes), not scratch.

Boundary: analysis/report products stay in Lynchpin until Sinex projections actually replace them — this issue ports SOURCES (evidence intake), not dashboards.
design: Now the sources-execution limb of the k4c subsumption epic (ledger authority moved to k4c.3 — this bead EXECUTES the port-as-source verdicts). RULES from the epic: every port re-lands on ORIGINAL provider data as materials (Lynchpin-processed intermediates are parity evidence only — the rkv.6 pattern is the template); delivery through the stm static-import glue; each ported source declares its consumer derivation per catalog extension rule 1; equivalence keys so re-imports don't duplicate. LIKELY PORT SET (confirm against the ledger): spotify, reddit, google-takeout products, goodreads/wykop/messenger exports, substance CSV, weather; already-covered set (activitywatch, terminal, git, web, machine telemetry) gets covered-verdict parity notes instead.
acceptance_criteria: Every ledger row with verdict port-as-source is either landed (source contract + deployment + events queryable + parity note) or has its own bead; no ported source reads a /realm/data/derived/lynchpin path; spot-check parity: for two ported sources, a day-level count comparison Sinex-vs-Lynchpin recorded as the parity artifact.

title: Architecture, hygiene, and design backlog
description: Architecture, hygiene, and design backlog: the census-then-decide plane. Holds the analyses that inventory what exists (payload vocabulary, consumption telemetry), the decisions they inform (automaton portfolio), and structural hygiene items that don't belong to a campaign epic (projection unification, timezone semantics, inert-surface gating). Distinct from r6d (loss/honesty) and n23 (load): this epic is about the system's SHAPE staying intentional as it grows.
design: PAIRINGS (the census feeds the knife): nbi.1 (payload vocabulary vs flow census — 159 payloads vs ~20 contracts) + nbi.2 (consumption telemetry mining — which surfaces were ever invoked) => nbi.4 (automaton portfolio decision: consume / gate-behind-profile / stop-shipping-enabled per automaton) and the delete-or-implement list executed via y7l (dead-lane sweep, pre-release: delete don't deprecate). STRUCTURAL: nbi.3 (documents off the DB trigger — operator-decided; consumer-maintained projections are THE read-model mechanism), 05a (artifact descriptor registry — highest-leverage structural move, design round first), bm1 (show-ref honesty matrix). HYGIENE: nbi.5 (timezone semantics — also the template for the timezone-class sweep: when worked, grep for other raw-UTC-hour bucketing), nbi.6 (inert LLM read surfaces). All children are analysis-lane friendly (k4c.1): run censuses as recorded analyses.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: both censuses produced with recorded evidence; nbi.4 decided per-automaton with the census cited; delete-or-implement list has no unexecuted DELETE verdicts; documents projection migrated; no payload struct exists without either a flowing producer or an explicit keep rationale.

title: As-of belief time travel: query combinator over live + archived interpretations
description:
Design issue (gated on replay trust + product recall existing): answer "what did sinex believe about window W as of time T" by resolving against live events minus interpretations created after T plus archived interpretations that were live at T (audit.archived_events carries the supersession chain). Datomic-style as-of over the two-clock model — a headline demo of the interpretation-versioning thesis. Needs: archived_events indexed by (occurrence, archived_at), a query-unit flag, honest caveats where archives were pruned.

Refs: handoff U2; glossary ts_coided/ts_orig semantics.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03; prior art: Datomic as-of, SQL:2011 system-versioned tables).

SEMANTICS: --as-of T reconstructs the interpretation set Sinex believed at time T:
  live-at-T = { core.events | ts_coided <= T }  UNION ALL  { audit.archived_events | ts_coided <= T AND archived_at > T }
No id can appear in both (archival deletes from live), so no dedup needed. as-of operates ONLY on belief time (ts_coided / archived_at) — occurrence-time (ts_orig) filters compose orthogonally on top. That two-axis split IS the demo.

DECISIONS:
1. Implement as ONE combinator in the shared query substrate (an AsOf(T) wrapper that rewrites the event-source relation to the union above), not per-command flags — every query unit and recall/context inherits it (algebra, not silo).
2. Tombstones override history: occurrences in event_tombstones are excluded from as-of results unconditionally (privacy deletion is not time-travelable), with an honest excluded-count caveat in the result envelope.
3. Precondition check: audit.archived_events must carry archived_at and preserve original ts_coided/id; verify, add archived_at if absent (trivial).
4. No new indexes initially; if slow, (occurrence, archived_at) btree on archived_events — measure first.
5. Surface: sinexctl query '... as of <T>' + recall/context --as-of. Result envelope marks as-of mode + T so downstream consumers cannot mistake reconstructed belief for current belief.

DEMO (built-in construct validity): run recall over the same ts_orig window as-of two instants straddling a replay operation; diff the outputs; EVERY difference must trace to that replay operation id. Falsifier included by construction.

GATING: replay trust first (sinex-afd) — time travel over an archive that can silently lose rows is a lie. Product recall (sinex-vhm) should exist so as-of has a consumer surface.
acceptance_criteria: Query-substrate combinator: any event-backed query unit accepts as-of and returns the union semantics above; tombstoned occurrences never resurface (test with a tombstoned event archived before T); result envelope carries as-of marker + excluded-tombstone count; recall --as-of straddling a synthetic replay shows differences that all trace to the replay operation id. VERIFY: query-unit tests + a replay-then-as-of e2e.

title: Early-cutoff replay: green-subgraph restore keyed on derivation traces
description:
Design issue (do after replay correctness gh#2194 settles): make replay cost scale with the affected subgraph, not the archive size. Mechanism sketched in handoff U1: store an anchor_payload_hash per event; on replay, when a re-derived event's hash matches the archived interpretation, cut off the downstream cascade (Build Systems a la Carte early cutoff / Salsa red-green). Requires: hash definition that excludes interpretation-identity fields (id, ts_coided), cascade walker support, and honest semantics (cutoff is an optimization, never changes results).

Refs: handoff 02-uncovered-designs U1.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03; prior art: Build Systems a la Carte "early cutoff" + "constructive traces", Salsa red-green).

PROBLEM: replay archives + re-derives the FULL descendant cascade even when most re-parsed L1 events are identical to the archived interpretations (typical when a parser fix touches 1% of records). Replay cost scales with evidence volume, not with the diff of understanding.

DECISION — restore-in-place for green nodes:
1. Green test at L1 (material events): after re-ingest computes the fresh event for occurrence (source_material_id, anchor_byte), compare against the archived interpretation: payload hash + event_type + ts_orig equal -> GREEN. Do NOT mint a new event; restore the archived row verbatim (same id, same ts_coided) from audit.archived_events back to core.events. Privacy-rule changes are automatically covered: redaction runs pre-persist, so a policy change alters the fresh payload hash -> RED. anchor_payload_hash (sinex-primitives/src/events/mod.rs:91) is the comparison primitive.
2. Green test at derived levels: a derived node is GREEN iff ALL its parents are green AND its recorded semantics_version equals the automaton's current semantics_version (derived events already carry automaton_model/temporal_policy/semantics_version metadata — the trace check is local). Green derived nodes restore verbatim; any red parent or version bump -> re-run the automaton (RED cascade as today).
3. Id stability of green nodes is the load-bearing property: a re-derived red node can reference green parents by their ORIGINAL ids, so no provenance mutation is ever needed.

WHY THIS IS HONEST (doctrine refinement, not violation): "replay creates new interpretations" applies where interpretation CHANGED. A byte-identical payload under identical parser semantics and policy is the SAME interpretation; restoring it with its original id and ts_coided states exactly the truth. New id + new ts_coided remain mandatory for every red node.

REJECTED ALTERNATIVES:
- Re-parent archived descendants onto freshly-minted L1 ids (the U1 sketch's variant): mutates provenance of persisted events — violates immutability; rejected.
- Cut-invalidation-only (skip invalidation publish, leave descendants archived): silently loses the green subtree from the live set; wrong.
- Mint new ids but copy payloads: breaks downstream parent references, forces cascade-wide id remapping; strictly worse than restore.

IMPLEMENTATION ORDER: (1) blocked on the occurrence-keyed cascade-restore fix (sinex-afd) — early cutoff builds directly on that restore primitive; (2) replay planner computes green/red during re-ingest (needs the archived set indexed by occurrence in the operation scope, which the archive cascade already materializes); (3) per-operation report: green_restored / red_rederived counts + max cut depth, surfaced in ops replay output; (4) explicit --no-cutoff escape hatch for full re-interpretation.

VALUE: a parser fix touching 1% of a 52M-event history re-derives ~1% of the tower.
acceptance_criteria: Synthetic cascade test: replay after a parser change touching k of N records restores exactly the untouched subgraphs verbatim (same ids, same ts_coided) and re-derives only the affected ones + descendants of red nodes; a semantics_version bump on one automaton forces red for that automaton's outputs even with green inputs; a privacy-rule change forces red at L1; ops replay output reports green/red counts; --no-cutoff reproduces today's full-cascade behavior. VERIFY: replay e2e + state_machine unit tests; xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(replay)'.

title: Object-universe resolution matrix: make show <ref> honest about what resolves
description:
30-variant SinexObjectKind; ~16 kinds parse but do not resolve. Build the resolution matrix (kind x resolvable x view x MCP), wire the cheap missing resolvers, and make `sinexctl show <ref>` state honestly when a kind is recognized but unresolvable. Feeds P14 (descriptor registry) with ground truth about which kinds matter.

Refs: audit 015 target-inference.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-3.md#sinex-bm1---object-resolution-matrix. Build an explicit object-universe resolution matrix before the artifact descriptor registry. Current state: PublicSinexRef parses all 30 SinexObjectKind variants, but sinexctl show resolves only command, rpc-method, source-material, source-driver, operation, and event. Matrix rows cover every kind with public spelling, id shape, show resolver state, backing show/RPC/local surface, query-unit exposure, MCP exposure, cheap resolver candidacy, and descriptor-registry relevance. Use the matrix to make unsupported show responses specific and machine-readable, naming adjacent read surfaces when known. Wire only direct low-risk resolvers with exact id shape and typed read-only client methods; do not build the descriptor registry here.
acceptance_criteria: Every SinexObjectKind variant has exactly one matrix row and tests fail on unclassified new variants; existing show behavior for the six resolved kinds remains covered; every parseable unsupported kind returns status=unsupported with specific matrix-derived message and adjacent surfaces where known; cheap direct resolvers are wired only where id shape and typed read method are exact, with deferred candidates marked; matrix records query-unit and MCP exposure including absence of generic MCP show; verification includes targeted show/public-ref/query-unit/MCP catalog tests and no descriptor registry is introduced.

title: Meta-plane wiring: connect the disconnected verification machinery
description:
xtask is 33.5% of product LOC but its strongest machinery is wired to no enforced gate: no PR-blocking hosted CI exists (all workflow_dispatch except post-merge perf + issue-close verify); the 18-scenario VM suite runs from nothing; impact-planning/proof-reuse guards nothing automatically.

Highest-ROI wiring: (1) VM smoke + chaos-restart as a pre-deploy step for Sinnix switch; (2) path-filtered strict-diff gate on schema-touching PRs; (3) the missing multi-automaton aggregate-memory e2e (the #2186/#2200 OOM incident class has no test analog); (4) decide the minimal PR-blocking CI set (relates gh#2179 bundle-check decision).

Refs: handoff P17; audit 015 §D12.
design: Also in scope beyond the four listed items: (5) impact-planner validation — replay the last ~30 merged PRs, compare balanced-plan verdicts vs full-suite ground truth for a measured false-skip rate, then keep-and-trust or delete (xtask/src/impact.rs:477-499,319-372); (6) demo-shelf validity gate — every summary.json requires a reproduction command, devloop-refresh-demos re-executes it, failures demote the artifact. VM suite entry: tests/e2e/nixos-vm/default.nix (~18 scenarios); resource-exhaustion e2e today covers single-consumer only and is all-ignored (tests/e2e/tests/resource_exhaustion_test.rs).
acceptance_criteria: Each wired gate demonstrably fires: VM smoke blocks a broken deploy in a drill; strict-diff triggers on a schema-touching PR; the aggregate-memory e2e fails when the in-flight byte budget is removed; impact-planner verdict report exists with a measured false-skip rate.
notes: Impact-planner validation (item 5 in design) is now sequenced AFTER the planner fixes in the staged-hunk/staleness bug bead — measure the fixed planner, not the broken one.

title: Perf packet: derived-insert COPY, telemetry CA churn, orphaned durable consumers
description:
Remaining perf items after the latency-diagnosis issue:
1. Derived inserts never use COPY (automaton-heavy workloads always take the QueryBuilder path) — measure, then route large derived batches through COPY if the REPEATABLE READ + FOR KEY SHARE parent-liveness semantics allow.
2. Telemetry CAs are dropped+recreated on EVERY schema apply — hand-tuning evaporates and refresh history resets; converge instead of recreate.
3. Automaton consumer names embed the filter list -> changing an automaton's input filters ORPHANS the old durable consumer on the stream (leak + retention pinning). Add GC or stable names.
4. COPY uses pooled conn + TEMP table (audit correction: not non-pooled) — verify pool interaction under concurrent batches.

Refs: handoff P16 + U6 (NATS gotchas); audit 015 mental-model corrections.
design: Item details: (1) derived COPY needs a batched parent-liveness precheck (single FOR KEY SHARE over the parent union) before COPY — write a design note first against the persistence validation invariants (persistence.rs:55-73,858-952; validation.rs:14-83,117-123); matters most for replay-driven mass re-derivation. (2) Also in scope: a Windowed bounded-state contract — enforce max accumulator size/age in AutomatonRuntime (precedents: session 10K force-close automata/session.rs:76,132-143; the relation-extractor O(N^3) incident relation_extractor.rs:29-41). (3) CA converge-not-recreate: apply.rs:1080-1133 drop+recreate on every apply — diff definitions like everything else. (4) Consumer-name filter-embedding orphans need GC or stable names.
acceptance_criteria: Each sub-item lands with a measurement (before/after for COPY; a test for bounded-state; CA definitions survive an apply with no change; orphaned durable consumers detected/GC'd on a filter change).

title: Artifact descriptor registry: one declaration per kind generates query unit, ref resolution, card, MCP
description:
Deepest structural move from the algebra analysis: one descriptor registration should yield query unit + ref resolver + view + MCP tool, collapsing today's parallel per-kind registries (30-variant SinexObjectKind with ~16 parseable-but-unresolvable kinds, separate query units, view renderers, MCP tools). DO NOT start with code: produce a gated design artifact (options, tradeoffs, decision) covering registry shape, migration order, and which existing registries fold in first. Touches many surfaces — sequence after the consumption packets prove which shapes matter.

Refs: handoff P14; audit 015 opportunity D; PROCESS.md design-gate doctrine.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-4.md#sinex-05a---artifact-descriptor-registry. Current duplicated surfaces: SinexObjectKind/public_ref spelling, sinexctl show resolver match, closed query-unit enum/descriptors/execute_query_unit, generic event card fallback keys, and manually parallel MCP tool/catalog/call matches. First slice after sinex-lv8: keep SinexObjectKind and QueryUnitId; add inventory-backed ArtifactDescriptor module in primitives; register descriptors for landed event-backed lv8 units only (sessions, summaries, entities, tags); lower descriptor query units through shared event query constrained by descriptor event type/source; route execute_query_unit through descriptors beside legacy match; add descriptor-aware show for UUID event-backed DefaultEventById refs only; update sinex.query MCP only through existing executor. Defer object-kind newtype, custom non-event resolvers, generated MCP catalog, and deletion of old match arms.
acceptance_criteria: sinex-lv8 units still work through CLI and MCP, but metadata/card projection comes from ArtifactDescriptor. Adding a toy event-backed artifact in a test requires one descriptor registration and no central query/show/card match edits. Descriptor query lowering uses the shared event-query path with descriptor constraints, not bespoke per-unit executors. Legacy query/show behavior remains unchanged. show event:<uuid> still resolves; descriptor-backed event artifact refs resolve through the default event resolver where applicable. Unsupported non-event/custom kinds remain explicit unsupported until migrated. rg evidence shows lv8 unit-specific card/query metadata is no longer duplicated outside descriptors.
notes:
Sequencing correction: let sinex-lv8 land the simple unit-enum way FIRST; registry phase 2 then migrates those working units onto descriptors. Collapse from working instances — do not co-develop the units and the registry (speculative-generalization risk).
Silo-ranking note (algebra section, operator-flagged): descriptor-driven GENERIC server-side query lowering is what makes (unit x predicate x format) multiplicative — the generated units from this registry must lower through one shared path, not per-unit code. And the collapse ranking names this registry the single highest-leverage combinatorial move in the codebase.

title: Add schema-bundle drift check to the pre-push guard (decision: bundle-only, path-filtered)
description: Bundle already regenerated (#2180); remaining scope is only the enforcement decision: should `xtask docs check` (or a cheaper bundle-only subset) join the pre-push drift guard / a CI gate so schemas/v1 cannot silently drift again? Full docs check may be too heavy for pre-push; a scoped bundle check is the likely answer. Record decision + implement the chosen gate.
design: DECISION (settled 2026-07-03): the schemas/v1 bundle check joins the pre-push drift guard; full xtask docs check stays manual/dispatch (too heavy for pre-push). Mechanism: .githooks/pre-push runs xtask docs schema-bundle --check (exists per TESTING.md) ONLY when the push's changed paths touch payload/schema surfaces (crate/sinex-primitives/src/events/**, crate/sinex-macros/**, crate/sinex-schema/**, schemas/v1/**) — same path-filter idiom as changed-strict. SINEX_SKIP_DRIFT_GUARD=1 bypass continues to cover it. Rationale: #2179's drift accumulated invisibly across ~20 files precisely because nothing automatic ran the bundle check; a path-filtered bundle-only check is cheap (no compile) and closes exactly that hole.
acceptance_criteria: A payload-touching commit with a stale bundle is rejected at pre-push with a message naming xtask docs sync; a docs-only push does not pay the check; SINEX_SKIP_DRIFT_GUARD=1 bypasses. VERIFY: deliberately stale bundle + push dry-run in a scratch branch.
close_reason: Implemented by PR #2382: pre-push now runs xtask docs schema-bundle --check only for payload/schema/bundle diffs, preserves changed-strict for Rust diffs, skips unrelated pushes, and keeps SINEX_SKIP_DRIFT_GUARD as the bypass.

title: Reduce compile tax for material-assembler policy tests (2.5 min / 7 GiB for one test)
description:
A single assembler policy test pays the whole sinexd daemon test-harness compile: nextest-compile 125s vs nextest-run 1.5s, peak process memory 7.2 GiB (job 2001299). Investigate a narrower test boundary for material_assembler (unit-level harness or crate split of the test surface) or better xtask proof selection so policy-level tests are cheap.

Refs: OPERATING-LOG.md 2026-07-03T05:57 compile-tax evidence.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-3.md#sinex-jvs---material-assembler-compile-tax. Reduce the measured material-assembler policy-test compile tax by extracting the pure stale-timeout decision out of the sinexd daemon compile graph. First slice targets end_before_begin_without_slices_short_timeouts: represent minimal policy inputs in a low-dependency internal workspace crate, call that policy from MaterialAssembler::find_stale_materials, and move the pure policy test to the new crate. Do not split DB/NATS/content-store assembler behavior in this slice and do not change public behavior; prove one policy test can run without compiling full sinexd, then decide whether broader extraction is worth doing.
acceptance_criteria: A low-dependency internal workspace crate owns stale-assembly timeout decision used by MaterialAssembler::find_stale_materials; end-before-begin/no-slices rule is covered without TestContext, NATS, DB pool, MaterialContentStore, or MaterialAssembler; sinexd delegates existing stale-material classification with no behavior change for finalizing, pending-end, buffered-slice, and normal slice-timeout cases; xtask freshness explain for the new crate proof does not include sinexd/runtime-binary; targeted new-crate test passes with nextest-compile materially below job 2001299 baseline 125.46s/7208.93MiB; focused sinexd check passes after wiring.

title: Mobile hemisphere: export-based bridges first (location, photos, messaging, screen-time)
design: Structural gap (L): Sinex is currently a desktop interpretive OS — the phone half of life is invisible. SETTLED APPROACH: export-based bridges FIRST — Takeout/Samsung Health/Sleep-as-Android archives already sit in /realm/data/exports with some parsers dark (overlaps the Lynchpin-port ledger); a live phone capture agent is a SEPARATE FUTURE EPOCH, explicitly out of scope here. Slices: location history import, photos metadata (not bytes initially), messaging exports, screen-time/app-usage. Each slice is a normal staged source through the generic glue.
acceptance_criteria: At least location + screen-time export lanes flowing through staged import with honest ts_orig quality (StagedAt vs intrinsic); the phone-agent epoch explicitly NOT started; coverage shows mobile families as present-but-export-lagged rather than absent.

title: comm.thread + relationship.activity: the people dimension
design: RECIPE: email ⊕ messaging ⊕ notifications ⋈ person -> communication threads and per-relationship activity intervals. THE glaring semantic hole today (nothing dyadic exists). BLOCKED on comms deployment (email epic runtime tier + messaging exports via the static-import glue) and on L4 person resolution for the ⋈ (interim: per-account pseudo-entities, honest about non-merged identities). Lane-gated where inference goes beyond provider-declared threading.
acceptance_criteria: With email flowing: provider-declared threads as events; per-account activity intervals; NO inferred person merges without judgment (interim entities visibly per-account); people dimension queryable at all (from zero).

title: consumption.episode: read -> saved -> used chains
design: RECIPE: visit+dwell ⊕ (crawled content ⋈ topic) ⊕ raindrop save ⊕ later reference -> consumption episodes linking what was read to when it was used. BLOCKED on the browser-content source (capture epic) for the content/topic leg; visits+raindrop already flow. Sequence last of the quartet's successors.
acceptance_criteria: For instrumented history: episodes link visit -> save -> later terminal/doc reference with resolvable refs; topic leg present once browser content exists.

title: Sinex public-release readiness: secret scrub + audit-corpus decision
description:
Operator-owned decision (situation-brief §9): whether to cut Sinex public. Feasible and high-value per the brief, but turns on (a) a git-history secret scrub and (b) whether the audit corpus (operating logs, scratch, demo artifacts with live personal capture) ships or is stripped.

Agent prep (do, without deciding): (1) run a history secret scan (gitleaks/trufflehog class) and inventory findings; (2) inventory personal-capture-bearing tracked paths; (3) estimate the scrub shape (filter-repo plan per the git-workflows history-rewrite protocol — dry-run on a mirror, never on the live repo without explicit approval).
Operator decides: public/private, corpus ships/stripped, timing relative to the demo campaigns.

Related: the legibility bead (sinex-ej9) has the same personal-capture blocker for its evidence gallery.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-3.md#sinex-83g---public-release-readiness. Prepare a public-release disclosure decision packet without deciding public/private. Inventory tracked live personal capture, demo/audit proof material, encrypted secret material, fixture keys/certs, sample tokens, and host/operator topology; run or specify non-destructive scans over tracked source first; restrict history scans and filter-repo experiments to a mirror/disposable clone with backup refs; produce a corpus option matrix: ship as-is, strip, split private corpus, regenerate fixtures. Known candidate surfaces: .agent/dev/recall-artifacts/**, demo/sinex-recall/**, nixos/secret/**, tracked TLS/key fixtures under tests/**, and ignored local .agent demos/artifacts/logs as private corpus inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Tracked personal-capture-bearing paths are inventoried with disclosure class; likely secret-risk paths are classified as real secret, encrypted secret material, fixture, sample, false positive, or operator-review; safe scan tooling availability and actual/not-run commands are recorded; current-tree and history scan plans are non-destructive, with history restricted to mirror/disposable clone; filter-repo dry-run plan includes backup-ref, mirror-clone, candidate path/pattern removal, and validation commands; audit corpus matrix covers ship/strip/split-private/fixture-regenerate without choosing; follow-up Beads are listed for implementation slices.

title: Instruction-verb registry: actuation verbs as operator-declared data
description: The actuation plane (instruction -> dispatch -> reconcile -> expectation.status) is proven for exactly one hardcoded verb. This generalizes it. Far-backlog: consumption unlock outranks actuation; do not schedule before the consumption epic has landed its p1 items.
design: DESIGN (settled, handoff U10): a verb is data, not code: {verb_id, dispatch: {kind: hyprland_socket|dbus|exec, template}, expectation: {event_type, match: query-fragment, timeout}}. The reconciler already matches observations to pending instructions generically (automata/instruction_reconciler.rs:99-219); only the hyprland handler hardcodes dispatch. Registry-driven verbs make a new actuation = one registration + a NixOS-side allowlist entry. SECURITY BOUNDARY (non-negotiable): verbs are operator-declared; agents can only invoke declared verbs with schema-validated args — never raw exec. First three verbs: workspace-switch (exists — migrate it onto the registry), notification-DND (dbus), media-session-gate (wrap the existing session_gate).
acceptance_criteria: The existing hyprland verb runs through the registry with no behavior change; a second verb lands as registration-only (no new dispatch code beyond its kind); an undeclared verb invocation is rejected with a typed error; NixOS allowlist gates which verbs a deployment exposes.
notes: Its acceptance demo (folded from the portfolio): closed-loop focus actuation — session-boundary thrash detection -> Proposal (authority gate's second-ever kind) -> operator accepts -> workspace-switch + notification-DND dispatch -> reconciler verdicts from INDEPENDENT observation events (never the actuator's own claim).

title: Agent annotation intake: 4-form @@-DSL through the polylogue lane
description: From the operator rawlog (06-28 idea) via handoff U8. Sinex-side half of a cross-repo pair; do after the beads/devloop ingestion lanes (sinex-hlv) prove the pattern for jsonl-lane sources.
design: DESIGN (settled, handoff U8): exactly four line-anchored forms, greppable, no nesting: @@claim(id?): <text> | @@todo(due?): <text> | @@obs: <text> | @@ref(<kind:id>): <text>. One per line; ids are optional slugs. Division of labor: POLYLOGUE extracts annotations with span provenance (session/message/offset = the material anchor); SINEX ingests via the integration.polylogue lane as agent.annotation events with material provenance to the session export. Explicitly rejected: free-form XML, multi-line blocks, more semantic types — extend only after usage volume proves need. The value proposition: agents emit these mid-prose at zero tool-call cost. Cross-repo: the extraction half is a polylogue work item; this bead owns only the sinex-side contract + parser.
acceptance_criteria: agent.annotation events queryable with all four forms round-tripped; span provenance resolves back to the session material; malformed annotations admitted as debt, not dropped silently; a polylogue-side extraction issue is filed and linked before this closes.

title: Events-are-cache eviction: coverage-checked tombstoning + explicit re-materialization
description: Deferred by design until after telemetry-lane separation. The retention doctrine itself is settled (02-final-shape.md rule 4); this issue is its mechanism.
design: DESIGN (from handoff U3, doctrine rule 4): because every material event is re-derivable from source materials, core.events is formally a materialization cache and retention is cache policy. Mechanism: an eviction operation that (1) verifies superseding coverage before tombstoning — the candidate window's L2+ summaries' parent sets must span the evicted events (coverage check is a query over source_event_ids); (2) tombstones en masse via the existing lifecycle machinery (live -> archive -> tombstone), never DELETE; (3) recall renders evicted windows as an explicit 'evictable detail — re-materialize?' gap, and re-materialization is a scoped replay of the relevant materials, operator-visible. L0 materials retained forever; L3 evictable because effects are cached; L4 never evicted. GATE: do not implement before the telemetry lane (sinex-x9i) — reflection eviction alone removes most volume pressure, and this must not be used as a substitute for lane separation.
acceptance_criteria: Eviction refuses when coverage check fails (test: window with a gap in summary parents); evicted window renders as an explicit gap in recall with a re-materialize action; re-materialization restores the window through normal replay; L0/L4 provably untouchable by the operation.
